explanation: "pride of Barbados" (Caesalpinia pulcherrima), exhibits marginal placentation. This plant is part of the Fabaceae family, which commonly features marginal placentation.

topics.title: INTERNAL STRUCTURE OF A FLOWERING PLANT : LEAF

subject: Biology
q: The term caryopsis is used to describe a fruit in which the
options: seed coat and fruit wall are impermeable
explanation: A caryopsis is a small, simple fruit. It develops from a single pistil consisting of a single ovary. The pericarp and the seed coat are fused to form a covering over the entire seed. Examples are maize grain, rice, millet and guinea corn.

topics.title: INTERNAL STRUCTURE OF A FLOWERING PLANT : LEAF

subject: Biology
q: The sex of a foetus is determined during
explanation: The sex of a foetus is determined during fertilization. This is because the sex chromosomes that determine the sex of a foetus are carried in the sperm and egg cells, which combine during fertilization. The egg cell always carries an X chromosome, while the sperm cell carries either an X or a Y chromosome. If the sperm cell carries an X chromosome, the foetus will be female (XX), and if it carries a Y chromosome, the foetus will be male (XY).

topics.title: HEREDITY : PROBABILITY IN GENETICS AND SEX DETERMINATION

subject: Biology
q: Biological growth refers strictly to an increase in the
explanation: Biological growth refers strictly to an increase in the protoplasm of an organism. Protoplasm is the living part of a cell that is often referred to as the 'physical basis of life'. An increase in protoplasm leads to an increase in cell size and number, which results in the growth of an organism. Therefore, biological growth is not just about the increase in size or number of organisms, but more specifically about the increase in the protoplasm of an organism.

subject: Biology
q: The process of walking is under the control of the part of the brain called
explanation: The process of walking is under the control of the cerebellum. The cerebellum is a part of the brain that is responsible for motor control. It receives information from the sensory systems, the spinal cord, and other parts of the brain and then regulates motor movements. The cerebellum coordinates voluntary movements such as posture, balance, coordination, and speech, resulting in smooth and balanced muscular activity. It is also important for learning motor behaviors.

subject: Biology
q: The gland directly affecting metabolic rate, growth and development is known as
explanation: The thyroid gland directly affects metabolic rate, growth, and development. The thyroid gland is a butterfly-shaped organ located in the base of your neck. It releases hormones that control metabolism—the way your body uses energy. The thyroid's hormones regulate vital body functions, including breathing, heart rate, central and peripheral nervous systems, body weight, muscle strength, menstrual cycles, body temperature, and cholesterol levels.

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer the question


The diagram represents
explanation: Motor neurones are cells in the brain and spinal cord that allow us to move, speak, swallow and breathe by sending commands from the brain to the muscles that carry out these functions.

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer the question

The part labelled X is the
explanation: Cell body. Also known as a soma, the cell body is the core section of the neuron. The cell body contains genetic information, maintains the neuron's structure, and provides energy to drive activities.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following ions is involved in the conduction of the nerve impulse?
explanation: The conduction of nerve impulses, also known as action potentials, is primarily facilitated by the movement of sodium (Na+) and potassium (K+) ions across the neuron's membrane. This process is known as the sodium-potassium pump. In the resting state, the neuron's membrane is polarized, with more positive charges outside than inside. When an action potential occurs, the membrane depolarizes, and Na+ ions rush into the neuron. This is followed by the repolarization phase, where K+ ions rush out of the neuron, restoring the membrane's polarity. Therefore, the correct answer is K+ ions.

subject: Biology
q: The depth of illumination in a water body can be measured with a
explanation: The depth of illumination in a water body, also known as the photic zone or the depth to which light penetrates in the water, can be measured using a Secchi disc. A Secchi disc is a circular disc used to measure water transparency in oceans and lakes. The disc is lowered into the water until it is no longer visible, with the depth at which it disappears being recorded as the Secchi depth. Therefore, the correct answer is a Secchi disc.

topics.title: NATURAL HABITAT : AQUATIC TERRESTRIAL AND ARBOREAL

subject: Biology
q: In a food chain involving a primary producer, a primary consumer as well as a secondary consumer, the sharing of trophic energy is in the form that the
options: the primary consumer has more energy than the primary producer
secondary consumer takes up all the energy contents of the primary consumer
energy is shared equally between the three groups of organism
secondary consumer gets only a small portion of the energy contained in the primary producer
explanation: In a food chain, energy is transferred from one trophic level to the next. However, not all energy is transferred. Only about 10% of the energy from one trophic level is transferred to the next. This is known as the 10% rule in ecology. Therefore, the secondary consumer gets only a small portion of the energy contained in the primary producer.

topics.title: ECOLOGY : FOOD CHAINS FOOD WEBS AND TROPHIC LEVELS

subject: Biology
q: In spite of the removal of carbon dioxide from the atmosphere, its amount remains more or less constant because
options: it is produced by green plants during photosynthesis
it is produced during respiration of animals
it is absorbed inn ocean water
green plants release it during the day
explanation: The amount of carbon dioxide in the atmosphere remains more or less constant because it is part of a cycle known as the carbon cycle. In this cycle, carbon dioxide is produced during the respiration of animals and other organisms. It is then absorbed by plants during photosynthesis, which convert it into organic compounds. These compounds are then consumed by animals, which respire and release carbon dioxide back into the atmosphere, completing the cycle.

subject: Biology
q: In a tropical rain forest, non-epiphytic ferns and allies occur as
explanation: Non-epiphytic ferns and allies in a tropical rain forest are shade-loving species. This is because they are adapted to grow in low light conditions under the canopy of the forest. They are not middle or upper storey species because they do not grow high up in the trees. They are also not emergent species because they do not stand out above the rest of the vegetation.

topics.title: ECOLOGY : FACTORS AFFECTING THE DISTRIBUTION OF ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: Colonization of a bare rock surface is termed
explanation: Colonization of a bare rock surface is termed primary succession. Primary succession is the series of community changes which occur on an entirely new habitat which has never been colonized before. For example, a newly quarried rock face or sand dunes. Evolution and specification are processes related to the development and diversification of species, not to the colonization of habitats. Secondary succession is the series of community changes which take place on a previously colonized, but disturbed or damaged habitat.

topics.title: THE ECOLOGY OF POPULATIONS : ECOLOGICAL SUCCESSION

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following is a measure for the control of bilharzia?
options: Screening windows and doors with mosquito nets
explanation: Bilharzia, also known as schistosomiasis, is a disease caused by parasitic worms. One of the measures for the control of bilharzia is the application of molluscicides in water bodies. Molluscicides are chemicals used to kill snails and slugs. In the case of bilharzia, they are used to kill the snails that serve as intermediate hosts for the parasitic worms that cause the disease. The other options do not directly relate to the control of bilharzia.

subject: Biology
q: Some of the diseases caused by bacteria are
explanation: Tuberculosis, gonorrhoea, and syphilis are all diseases caused by bacteria. Tuberculosis is caused by the bacterium Mycobacterium tuberculosis, gonorrhoea is caused by Neisseria gonorrhoeae, and syphilis is caused by Treponema pallidum. AIDS, on the other hand, is caused by a virus (HIV), not a bacterium.

subject: Biology
q: Environmental pollutants which can work through the media of water, soil and air include
explanation: Sulphur(IV)oxide is a type of environmental pollutant that can work through the media of water, soil, and air. It is a gas that can dissolve in water to form acid rain, which can then contaminate soil and bodies of water. Carbon monoxide is primarily an air pollutant, while noise and smoke are not typically associated with soil pollution.

topics.title: HUMANS AND ENVIRONMENT : POLLUTION AND ITS CONTROL

subject: Biology
q: Human height is an example of a feature which depends on both
explanation: Human height is an example of a feature that depends on both genetic and environmental factors. The genetic factor refers to the genes inherited from parents, which can influence the potential height a person can reach. The environmental factor refers to external influences such as nutrition and health, which can affect how much of that potential height is actually achieved. Genotype refers to the genetic makeup of an individual, while phenotype refers to the observable characteristics, such as height, which are influenced by both the genotype and the environment.

topics.title: HEREDITY : INHERITANCE OF CHARACTERS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: A person with type O blood can donate to a patient with type A because the donor's blood
explanation: A person with type O blood can donate to a patient with type A because the donor's blood lacks antigens. Blood type O is considered the universal donor because it lacks A and B antigens on the red blood cells, which means it can be donated to any of the other blood types (A, B, AB, and O) without causing an immune response.

subject: Biology
q: The DNA molecule is a chain of repeating
explanation: The DNA molecule is a chain of repeating nucleotides. A nucleotide is the basic building block of nucleic acids, such as DNA and RNA. Each nucleotide consists of a sugar (deoxyribose in DNA), a phosphate group, and a nitrogenous base (adenine, thymine, cytosine, or guanine in DNA). The nucleotides are linked together in a chain through phosphodiester bonds between the sugar of one nucleotide and the phosphate of the next.

subject: Biology
q: The specific number of chromosomes in each somatic cell is represented by
explanation: The specific number of chromosomes in each somatic cell is represented by 2N. In humans, the diploid number (2N) is 46, which means there are 23 pairs of chromosomes in each somatic (body) cell. The symbol 'N' is used to represent the haploid number, which is the number of chromosomes in a gamete (sperm or egg cell).

subject: Biology
q: A man with blood group A is married to a woman with blood group A. Which of the following group combinations is possible if the family has three children?
explanation: A man and a woman with blood group A can have children with either blood group A or O. This is because the blood group A can be AA or AO, and the blood group O is OO. When the AO and AO combine, they can form AA (blood group A), AO (blood group A), or OO (blood group O). Therefore, the possible combinations for their three children could be A, O, A.

topics.title: HEREDITY : INHERITANCE OF CHARACTERS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: The F1 of a cross between a tall and a dwarf plant was tall. The F1 was advanced to F2. How many of 120 F2 plants will be dwarf?
explanation: In a cross between a tall plant (TT) and a dwarf plant (tt), the F1 generation will all be tall (Tt) because the tall trait is dominant. When the F1 generation is self-fertilized to produce the F2 generation, the ratio of tall to dwarf plants will be 3:1. Therefore, out of 120 F2 plants, 1/4 (or 30) will be dwarf.

topics.title: HEREDITY : INHERITANCE OF CHARACTERS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: All the sons of a colour-blind woman will be coloured blind regardless of the state of the father because
options: the egg determines the phenotype of the son
sons inherit the sex chromosome of their mothers
the father's sex chromosomes is weaker in sons
explanation: Colour blindness is a sex-linked trait that is carried on the X chromosome. Women have two X chromosomes, so a colour-blind woman must have two colour-blindness genes (one on each X chromosome). Sons inherit one X chromosome from their mother and one Y chromosome from their father. Therefore, all sons of a colour-blind woman will inherit the colour-blindness gene from their mother and will be colour blind, regardless of the state of the father.

subject: Biology
q: The slender, long and slightly curved beak of the sun-bird is an adaptation for breeding for feeding on
explanation: The sun-bird has a long, slender, and slightly curved beak which is an adaptation for feeding on nectar. This type of beak allows the bird to reach deep into flowers to extract nectar, which is its primary food source.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : STRUCTURAL ADAPTATIONS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: Scales on reptiles are a feature for
explanation: Scales on reptiles serve several functions, one of which is to conserve water. Reptiles live in various environments, many of which are arid or semi-arid. Their scales are made of keratin and provide a waterproof barrier, preventing the loss of water through the skin.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : STRUCTURAL ADAPTATIONS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: The colour of the ventral surface of a fish is lighter than that of the dorsal. This is mainly
explanation: The coloration of a fish, with a lighter ventral surface and a darker dorsal surface, is a form of camouflage known as countershading. This makes the fish harder to see, both from above and below, protecting it from predators and allowing it to sneak up on prey.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : ADAPTIVE COLOURATION AND ITS FUNCTIONS

subject: Biology
q: The least evidence in support of the theory of evolution is provided by the study of
explanation: The theory of evolution is supported by various scientific studies, including anatomy, geology, and embryology. However, ecology provides the least evidence in support of the theory of evolution. Ecology is the study of the interactions between organisms and their environment, and while it can provide some insights into evolutionary processes, it does not provide as direct or extensive evidence as the other fields mentioned.

subject: Biology
q: From which group of animals are the mammals generally believed to have most recently evolved?
explanation: Mammals are generally believed to have most recently evolved from a group of animals known as therapsids, or 'mammal-like reptiles', during the Mesozoic Era. Therefore, reptiles are the correct answer. Fish, amphibians, and birds are not considered direct ancestors of mammals in the evolutionary tree.

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer the question


The structure labelled I is the
explanation: The endoplasmic reticulum (ER) is a large, dynamic structure that serves many roles in the cell including calcium storage, protein synthesis and lipid metabolism.

topics.title: LIVING ORGANISMS : CELL STRUCTURE AND FUNCTIONS OF CELL COMPONENTS

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer the question

Protein is synthesized in the part labelled
explanation:
In the diagram provided, the part labeled for protein synthesis is typically the rough endoplasmic reticulum (RER), which is involved in protein synthesis due to the presence of ribosomes on its surface.

The rough endoplasmic reticulum (RER) is typically depicted as a series of connected, flattened sacs covered with ribosomes, often represented by small dots.

In this specific diagram, the rough endoplasmic reticulum appears to be the part labeled I, which fits the typical description and depiction of the rough endoplasmic reticulum.

topics.title: LIVING ORGANISMS : CELL STRUCTURE AND FUNCTIONS OF CELL COMPONENTS

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following is the youngest plant tissue
explanation: Meristem is the youngest plant tissue. It is a type of plant tissue consisting of undifferentiated cells that can continue to divide and differentiate. Meristematic cells give rise to various tissues and organs of a plant and are responsible for growth. Phloem, epidermis, and xylem are differentiated tissues, which are typically older than meristematic tissue.

topics.title: LIVING ORGANISMS : CELL STRUCTURE AND FUNCTIONS OF CELL COMPONENTS

subject: Biology
q: Viruses differ from all forms of life because they
options: feed on waste products of other organisms
explanation: Viruses are different from all forms of life because they require other living cells to multiply. Viruses are considered non-living outside a host cell because they lack the ability to reproduce or carry out metabolic processes on their own. They need to infect a host cell and use the host's cellular machinery to replicate.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : BEHAVIOURAL ADAPTATIONS IN SOCIAL ANIMALS

subject: Biology
q: The cnidoblast cells found in hydra are used for
explanation: The cnidoblast cells found in hydra are used for offence and defence. Cnidoblasts, also known as cnidocytes, are specialized cells that contain a stinging organelle called a cnidocyst. These cells are used by the hydra to capture prey and for protection against predators.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : STRUCTURAL ADAPTATIONS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: Annelids differ from nematodes in that they
explanation: Annelids differ from nematodes in that they are metamerically segmented. Metamerism is a type of body segmentation where the body is divided into repeating segments, each of which contains the same set of body structures. Annelids, such as earthworms, exhibit this type of segmentation, while nematodes, or roundworms, have a smooth, unsegmented body.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following performs similar functions?
explanation:
Ascospores and ascocarp
Ascospores: Sexual spores.
Ascocarp: Fruiting body containing asci.
Related but different functions: spore production vs. spore housing.
Antherozoids and rhizoids
Antherozoids: Male gametes.
Rhizoids: Root-like structures for anchorage and absorption.
Different functions: reproduction vs. support/nutrient absorption.
Sorus and indusium
Sorus: Cluster of sporangia.
Indusium: Protective covering for sorus.
Related but different functions: spore production vs. protection.
Strobili and inflorescence
Strobili: Cone-like reproductive structures.
Inflorescence: Cluster of flowers.
Similar functions: reproductive structures in different plant groups.

subject: Biology
q: In ferns, the sporophyte
options: is haploid and dependant on gametophyte
is diploid and independent of the gametophyte
explanation: In ferns, the sporophyte is the diploid multicellular stage in the life cycle. It develops from a fertilized egg (zygote) and grows into a mature fern. It is independent of the gametophyte, the haploid multicellular stage, as it can photosynthesize and does not rely on the gametophyte for nutrients.

subject: Biology
q: The group of insects that undergoes complete metamorphosis is
explanation:
Complete metamorphosis is a form of insect development which includes four life stages - egg, larva, pupa, and adult. Houseflies, beetles, and butterflies all undergo complete metamorphosis.
A common acronym for insects that undergo complete metamorphosis (holometabolous insects) can be CHAF:

C: Coleoptera (beetles)
H: Hymenoptera (ants, bees, wasps)
A: Lepidoptera (butterflies, moths)
F: Diptera (flies)

So, CHAF stands for Coleoptera, Hymenoptera, Lepidoptera, and Diptera.

For some common insects that undergo incomplete metamorphosis (hemimetabolous insects), use the acronym GOHB:

G: Gryllidae (crickets)
O: Orthoptera (grasshoppers, locusts)
H: Hemiptera (true bugs)
B: Blattodea (cockroaches)

So, GOHB stands for Gryllidae, Orthoptera, Hemiptera, and Blattodea.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : STRUCTURAL ADAPTATIONS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: The nitrogenous substance that is excreted by birds in order to conserve water is
explanation: Birds excrete uric acid as a nitrogenous waste product. This is because uric acid is less toxic and requires less water to excrete, which helps birds to conserve water. This is different from mammals, which excrete urea, and fish, which excrete ammonia.

subject: Biology
q: In mammals, the exchange of nutrients and metabolic products occurs in the
explanation: In mammals, the exchange of nutrients and metabolic products occurs in the lymph. The lymphatic system is a network of tissues and organs that help rid the body of toxins, waste and other unwanted materials. The primary function of the lymphatic system is to transport lymph, a fluid containing infection-fighting white blood cells, throughout the body.

subject: Biology
q: The part of the stomach nearer the gullet is called
explanation: The part of the stomach nearer the gullet (esophagus) is called the cardiac sphincter. This is a circular muscle that prevents the acidic contents of the stomach from moving upwards into the esophagus.

subject: Biology
q: Trace elements are required by plants mainly for the
explanation: Trace elements are required by plants mainly for the formation of pigments and enzymes. Trace elements are minerals that are needed in small amounts by plants for various physiological functions. Some trace elements are used to form pigments, which give plants their color, and enzymes, which are proteins that speed up chemical reactions in the plant's cells.

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer the question


The part that is similar to the bone is labelled
explanation:
Enamel (I): The hard, outermost layer of the tooth, much harder than bone.
Dentine (II): Lies beneath the enamel and is similar to bone in its composition and function.
Pulp cavity (III): Contains nerves and blood vessels.
Cementum (IV): Covers the tooth root and helps anchor it in place.

While enamel is the hardest substance in the body and provides the tooth with a protective outer layer, it is actually the dentine that is more similar to bone in terms of composition and function.

Dentine and bone are both made of similar mineralized connective tissue and have comparable hardness and flexibility, whereas enamel is much harder and more brittle than bone.

So, the part that is most similar to bone is dentine (labeled II), not enamel.

topics.title: INTERNAL STRUCTURE OF A MAMMAL : MAMMALIAN INTERNAL ORGANS

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer the question

The part labelled I and II constitute the
explanation: The crown is the functional part of the tooth that is visible above the gum. In a mammalian tooth, the parts labeled I (enamel) and II (dentine) constitute the crown of the tooth. The crown is the visible part of the tooth above the gum line.

topics.title: INTERNAL STRUCTURE OF A MAMMAL : MAMMALIAN INTERNAL ORGANS

subject: Biology
q: A food substance was treated with a few drops of Sudan III solution and a red coloration was obtained. The food contained
explanation: Sudan III is a dye used in biology to stain lipids, triglycerides and lipoproteins on frozen paraffin sections. It has the appearance of reddish brown crystals. It is a lysochrome (fat-soluble dye) diazo dye used for the staining of lipids, triglycerides and lipoproteins in paraffin sections. The staining process involves the application of Sudan III on the substance, and if the substance contains lipids or fats, it will stain them red.

subject: Biology
q: Oozing out of water from the leaves of plants in a humid environment is known as
explanation: Guttation is the process of oozing out of water from the leaves of plants in a humid environment. It is a type of water secretion that occurs in certain plants, where they exude water from the tips or edges of their leaves. This usually happens during the night or early morning, when the soil is very moist and the transpiration rate is low. The water is pushed out from the leaf due to root pressure.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : STRUCTURAL ADAPTATIONS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: The element that is essential for the coagulation of blood is
explanation: The element that is essential for the coagulation of blood is calcium. Coagulation, also known as clotting, is the process by which blood changes from a liquid to a gel, forming a blood clot. It potentially results in hemostasis, the cessation of blood loss from a damaged vessel, followed by repair. The coagulation process is initiated by calcium ions (Ca2+).

subject: Biology
q: Anaerobic respiration differs from aerobic respiration by the production of
explanation: Anaerobic respiration differs from aerobic respiration by the production of less amount of energy and alcohol. Anaerobic respiration is a type of respiration that does not use oxygen. It is used when there is not enough oxygen for aerobic respiration. It produces less energy because the glucose is only partially broken down. Alcohol or lactic acid are often produced as byproducts.

subject: Biology
q: Stomatal pores open when there is
options: an increase in the sugar content of guard cells
a decrease in the osmotic concentration of the guard cell
a decrease in the sugar content of mesophyll cells
an increase in the sugar content of mesophyll cells
explanation: Stomatal pores open when there is an increase in the sugar content of guard cells. The opening and closing of stomata are regulated by the water potential inside the guard cells. When the sugar content in the guard cells increases, it leads to an increase in osmotic potential causing water to move into the cells, making them turgid and causing the stomatal pore to open.

topics.title: INTERNAL STRUCTURE OF A FLOWERING PLANT : LEAF

subject: Biology
q: The process of deamination is essential for the
explanation:
Deamination is the process by which amino groups from amino acids are removed. The amino group is converted into ammonia, which is toxic to the body and is converted into urea by the liver. The urea is then excreted in the urine. Therefore, the process of deamination is essential for the formation of urea.
(digestion of protein) would not be correct if a student chose that because deamination occurs after proteins have already been digested and absorbed as amino acids. Here's the distinction:

Digestion of protein: Involves breaking down proteins into amino acids, which occurs in the stomach and small intestine with the help of enzymes like pepsin and trypsin.

Deamination: Takes place in the liver after amino acids have been absorbed into the bloodstream. It involves removing the amino group from amino acids, resulting in ammonia, which is then converted into urea for excretion.

Thus, deamination is part of the process of nitrogen metabolism and waste removal (urea formation), not protein digestion.

subject: Biology
q: A band of connective tissue linking two bones in a joint is known as
explanation: A band of connective tissue linking two bones in a joint is known as a ligament. Ligaments are strong, flexible tissues that hold bones together at the joints. They provide stability and limit the movement of joints to prevent dislocation. Tendons, on the other hand, connect muscles to bones, cartilage provides cushioning between bones, and the synovial membrane produces synovial fluid for lubrication in the joint.

topics.title: SUPPORT AND MOVEMENT : TYPES AND FUNCTIONS OF THE SKELETON

subject: Biology
q: The appendicular skeleton is composed of the pectoral girdle
explanation: The appendicular skeleton is composed of the bones of the upper limbs (arms), the lower limbs (legs), and the girdles (pectoral and pelvic) that connect the limbs to the axial skeleton. Therefore, the appendicular skeleton is composed of the pectoral girdle, pelvic girdle, fore and hind limbs.

topics.title: SUPPORT AND MOVEMENT : TYPES AND FUNCTIONS OF THE SKELETON

subject: Biology
q: The companion cells are part of the
explanation: Companion cells are part of the phloem. The phloem is a tissue in vascular plants that transports nutrients from the leaves to the rest of the plant. Companion cells are specialized parenchyma cells, located in the phloem and connected to sieve elements. They assist in the transport and function of the sieve elements.

subject: Biology
q: In which of the following groups of vertebrates would the largest amount of yolk be found in the egg?
explanation: The largest amount of yolk would be found in the eggs of reptiles. Yolk is a nutrient-rich substance in an egg that provides food for the developing embryo. Reptiles, such as turtles and lizards, lay eggs that have a large amount of yolk to nourish the embryo until it can hatch and find food on its own.

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer the question


The function of the part labelled V is for the passage of
explanation: The part labelled V is the micropyle. It is an opening for the passage of air, water and pollen nucleus.

topics.title: INTERNAL STRUCTURE OF A FLOWERING PLANT : LEAF

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer the question

The female gamete is represented by
explanation:
The embryo sac contains the egg cell, which is the female gamete, the correct part representing the female gamete is the embryo sac.Therefore, the female gamete is represented by: III

The embryo sac, labeled III, houses the egg cell (female gamete) along with other cells involved in the fertilization process. The egg cell is the actual female gamete that, upon fertilization by a male gamete (sperm cell from pollen), will develop into a zygote and eventually form the seed.

subject: Biology
q: The type of vegetative reproduction illustrated in the diagram above is
explanation: The definition of adventitious buds is the formation of a plant organ in an unusual place. When plants normally produce new roots and shoots from buds, the production of these buds occurs from apical meristem tissues usually located at the base of stems.

subject: Biology
q: coconut and oil palm fruits can be grouped as
explanation: Coconut and oil palm fruits can be grouped as drupes. A drupe is a type of fruit in which an outer fleshy part surrounds a shell (what we sometimes call a pit) with a seed inside. Examples of drupes include peaches, plums, and cherries—but also almonds, walnuts, and coconuts.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : STRUCTURAL ADAPTATIONS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: The substance that is responsible for optical dominance in plants is known as
explanation: Auxin is the hormone responsible for phototropism in plants, which is the growth of organisms in response to a light stimulus. Phototropism is most often observed in plants, but can also occur in other organisms such as fungi. The cells on the plant that are farthest from the light have a higher concentration of auxin, which causes the cells to grow larger on the side of the plant furthest from the light, thus causing the plant to bend towards the light.

subject: Biology
q: The part of the brain that regulates most biological cycles in humans is
explanation:
medulla oblongata is the bottom-most part of your brain. Its location means it's where your brain and spinal cord connect, making it a key conduit for nerve signals to and from your body. It also helps control vital processes like your heartbeat, breathing and blood pressure.
The pineal body, also known as the pineal gland, plays a crucial role in regulating various biological cycles in humans, including:

- Circadian rhythms (sleep-wake cycles)
- Hormone secretion (e.g., melatonin, serotonin)
- Reproductive cycles (puberty, menstrual cycles)
- Stress response

While the other options are brain regions with important functions, they aren't primarily responsible for regulating biological cycles:

Olfactory lobe: processes sensory information related to smell
Optic lobe: involved in processing visual information
Medulla oblongata: regulates autonomic functions like breathing, heart rate, and blood pressure

subject: Biology
q: The ability of the eye to focus on both near and distant objects is termed
explanation: Accommodation refers to the process by which the vertebrate eye changes optical power to maintain a clear image or focus on an object as its distance varies. In this process, the eye changes its focal length by changing the shape of its lens to focus on near or distant objects.

topics.title: COORDINATION AND CONTROL : THE SENSE ORGANS

subject: Biology
q: The speed of wind can be measured with an instrument called
explanation: The speed of wind is measured using an instrument called an anemometer. An anemometer is a device used for measuring wind speed, and is a common weather station instrument. The term is derived from the Greek word anemos, meaning wind, and is used to describe any airspeed measurement instrument used in meteorology or aerodynamics.

topics.title: HUMANS AND ENVIRONMENT : POLLUTION AND ITS CONTROL

subject: Biology
q: Which organism in the food-web illustrated above is an omnivore?
explanation: An omnivore feeds on both flesh and plant,from the diagram the rat feeds on weevil(animal) and rice(plant).

topics.title: ECOLOGY : FOOD CHAINS FOOD WEBS AND TROPHIC LEVELS

subject: Biology
q: Toads and lizards in an ecosystem depend on a limited quantity of food. This is an example of
explanation: The situation described in the question is an example of interspecific competition. Interspecific competition, in ecology, is a form of competition in which individuals of different species compete for the same resources in an ecosystem (e.g. food or living space). This can lead to a decrease in the population of less competitive species.

subject: Biology
q: Adaptive features of plants to desert conditions include?
options: thick barks, succulent stems and sunken stomata
thin barks, succulent stems and sunken stomata
thin barks, air floats on stems and sunken stomata
air spaces in tissues, adventitious roots and thick barks
explanation: Adaptive features of plants to desert conditions include thick barks, succulent stems and sunken stomata. Thick barks help to reduce water loss, succulent stems store water, and sunken stomata (tiny openings in the leaf surface) reduce evaporation by being located below the leaf's surface.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : STRUCTURAL ADAPTATIONS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: The Southern Guinea Savanna differs from the Northern Guinea Savanna in that it has
explanation: The Southern Guinea Savanna differs from the Northern Guinea Savanna in that it has more rainfall and taller grasses. This is due to the fact that the Southern Guinea Savanna is closer to the equator and therefore receives more rainfall. The increased rainfall promotes the growth of taller grasses.

subject: Biology
q: After a heavy rainfall and the formation of a large pond, the most likely sequence of changes in the vegetation of the pond is
explanation: After a heavy rainfall and the formation of a large pond, the most likely sequence of changes in the vegetation of the pond is Euglena → Spirogyra → water lily → grass. This sequence represents a typical succession of pond vegetation, starting with unicellular organisms like Euglena, followed by filamentous algae like Spirogyra, then aquatic plants like water lilies, and finally terrestrial plants like grasses as the pond dries up.

topics.title: THE ECOLOGY OF POPULATIONS : ECOLOGICAL SUCCESSION

subject: Biology
q: In a soil sample, the relative amounts of the different soil particles can best be determined by the process of
explanation: In a soil sample, the relative amounts of the different soil particles can best be determined by the process of sedimentation. This process involves settling out of particles from a suspension in a liquid.

topics.title: SOIL : CHARACTERISTICS OF DIFFERENT TYPES OF SOIL

subject: Biology
q: Which is the most important pollutant of the marine environment in Nigeria?
explanation: The most important pollutant of the marine environment in Nigeria is oil. This is due to the high level of oil exploration and exploitation activities in the country, particularly in the Niger Delta region. Oil spills from these activities often find their way into the marine environment, causing significant pollution.

topics.title: HUMANS AND ENVIRONMENT : POLLUTION AND ITS CONTROL

subject: Biology
q: One of the most effective ways of controlling guinea worm is by
explanation: One of the most effective ways of controlling guinea worm is by the provision of portable drinking water. Guinea worm disease is caused by the parasite Dracunculus medinensis, which is transmitted through contaminated water. By providing clean, safe drinking water, the cycle of transmission can be broken, effectively controlling the disease.

subject: Biology
q: During binary fission in lower organisms, the nucleus is known to undergo
explanation: During binary fission in lower organisms, the nucleus is known to undergo mitosis. Binary fission is a type of asexual reproduction where an organism splits into two offspring. Before the organism splits, its DNA must be replicated. This replication is done through the process of mitosis, where the nucleus divides into two identical nuclei.

subject: Biology
q: One reason for the success of Mendel's work is that he
options: was the first to carry out research work on modern genetics
introduced quantitative and qualitative elements into his work
selected two characteristics only at a time for study
drew up his laws of inheritance based on his knowledge of chromosomes
explanation: Mendel's success in his genetic experiments was largely due to his methodical approach and his choice of traits to study. He selected two characteristics at a time for study, which allowed him to observe the inheritance of individual traits, unencumbered by the effects of other traits. This approach is known as the law of independent assortment.

topics.title: HEREDITY : APPLICATION OF THE PRINCIPLES OF HEREDITY

subject: Biology
q: The homozygous condition HbsHbsresults in sickle cell anaemia whereas HbaHbs has the sickling trait. What is the probability that a couple with the sickling trait will give birth to one normal child?
explanation: AA, AS, AS SS . so the probability is number of required outcome/ number of possible outcome. no of required outcome = 1 (AA)(normal). no of possible outcome =4 (AA, AS, AS, SS). 1÷4= 1/4.

topics.title: HEREDITY : PROBABILITY IN GENETICS AND SEX DETERMINATION

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following characters is NOT sex-linked?
explanation: River blindness is not a sex-linked trait. It is caused by a parasitic worm transmitted through the bite of a blackfly, not by inheritance of a particular gene. The other options (baldness, haemophilia, and colour blindness) are all examples of sex-linked traits, which are traits that are determined by genes located on the sex chromosomes.

subject: Biology
q: Water loss is regulated in plants and animals by both the
explanation: leathery cuticle and the feathers help reduce water loss

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : STRUCTURAL ADAPTATIONS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: What combination of character should a prey develop to survive the environment of its predator?
options: Camouflage, well developed limbs and effective vision
Showy colour, big body and well developed limbs
Showy colour, well developed muscles and an acute sense of smell
explanation: To survive in an environment with predators, a prey animal needs to be able to hide (camouflage), escape quickly (well-developed limbs), and detect threats (effective vision). This combination of traits allows the animal to avoid detection, escape if detected, and be aware of its surroundings. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Camouflage, well developed limbs and effective vision'.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : ADAPTIVE COLOURATION AND ITS FUNCTIONS

subject: Biology
q: The three classes in a termitarium are
explanation: A termitarium, or termite mound, is a complex structure that houses a termite colony. The colony is divided into three main classes or castes: workers, soldiers, and reproductives (which include the king and queen). Workers are responsible for foraging and building, soldiers defend the colony, and reproductives are responsible for reproduction. Therefore, the correct answer is 'workers, soldiers and reproductives'.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : BEHAVIOURAL ADAPTATIONS IN SOCIAL ANIMALS

subject: Biology
q: Fossil records found in sedimentary rocks offer some explanation for the theory of evolution because
options: the deposits have remains of organisms characteristic of when they were formed
different strata have remains of organisms of the same kind
only organisms with strong parts are fossilized
most animal and plant fossils bear little resemblance to present day living specimens
explanation: Fossil records found in sedimentary rocks do offer some explanation for the theory of evolution because the deposits have remains of organisms characteristic of when they were formed. This is because as organisms die, they get buried in sediment, which over time hardens into rock and preserves the remains of the organisms. These fossils provide a record of the history of life on Earth and can show how different species have evolved over time.

subject: Biology
q: Long neck in giraffe is used to illustrate the theory of
explanation: Long neck in giraffe is used to illustrate the theory use and disuse of body parts

subject: Biology
q: The nucleus is considered the central organelle of a cell because it
options: is located at the center of the cell
explanation: The nucleus is considered the central organelle of a cell because it contains the genetic material. This genetic material, in the form of DNA, controls the activities of the cell and is responsible for inheritance. The other options are not the primary reasons why the nucleus is considered the central organelle.

topics.title: LIVING ORGANISMS : CELL STRUCTURE AND FUNCTIONS OF CELL COMPONENTS

subject: Biology
q: The procaryotic cell type is characterized by a
options: complex cytoplasm in which different regions are poorly defined
localization of different regions of the cell into tissues
explanation: Prokaryotic cells are characterized by having a simple structure with poorly defined regions. They lack membrane-bound organelles and their genetic material is not contained within a nucleus. This makes their cytoplasm less complex compared to eukaryotic cells.

topics.title: LIVING ORGANISMS : CELL STRUCTURE AND FUNCTIONS OF CELL COMPONENTS

subject: Biology
q: The natural tendency of organisms as they evolve is to
explanation: As organisms evolve, they tend to develop more specialized structures. This is a process known as differentiation, which allows organisms to adapt to their environment and perform specific functions more efficiently.

subject: Biology
q: In snails, the hard calcareous shells are secreted by the
explanation: In snails, the hard calcareous shells are secreted by the mantle. The mantle is a specialized tissue that produces the shell by secreting calcium carbonate, which hardens to form the shell.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : STRUCTURAL ADAPTATIONS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer the question


The structure labelled I is formed as a result of the fusion of
explanation: The structure labelled I is formed as a result of the fusion of several pairs of nuclei

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer the question

The special name of the part labelled II is
explanation:
In fungi, suspensors are filamentous structural formations having the function of holding a zygospore between two strains of hyphae.
The labeling (i) is the zygospore
The labeling (II) is the suspensor.
The hypha is the vertical line from your image from which the suspensor arises.

subject: Biology
q: The ability of the cockroach to live in cracks and crevices is enhanced by the possession of
explanation: The ability of the cockroach to live in cracks and crevices is enhanced by the possession of a dorso-ventrally flattened body. This body shape allows them to squeeze into small spaces and hide in cracks and crevices. The other options like wings and segmented body, compound eyes, and claws on the leg do not directly contribute to their ability to live in cracks and crevices.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : STRUCTURAL ADAPTATIONS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: The caste of termites that lacks pigmentation is the
explanation: In a termite colony, the worker caste lacks pigmentation. Workers are usually the smallest of the castes in a colony, and they are responsible for constructing and maintaining the nest, foraging for food, and caring for the young. The king, soldier, and queen castes have pigmentation.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : BEHAVIOURAL ADAPTATIONS IN SOCIAL ANIMALS

subject: Biology
q: The structure that prevents food particle from escaping through the fish gills are called gill
explanation: The structure that prevents food particles from escaping through the fish gills are called gill rakers. Gill rakers are comb-like structures that trap and filter out food particles from the water before it passes over the gills. The other options like gill arches, filaments, and lamellae are parts of the gill structure but do not serve the function of preventing food particles from escaping.

topics.title: STRUCTURAL/BEHAVIOURAL ADAPTATIONS OF VERTEBRATES : BONY FISH

subject: Biology
q: A distinguished feature of mammals is the possession of
explanation: Mammals are the only group of animals that have hair. Hair provides insulation, camouflage, sensory input, and a means of communication.

topics.title: INTERNAL STRUCTURE OF A MAMMAL : MAMMALIAN INTERNAL ORGANS

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following structures is capable of producing more tissues in the stem of a herbaceous flowering plant?
explanation: Cambium is a type of plant tissue located between the xylem and the phloem in the stem and along the roots of a plant. It is responsible for secondary growth in stems and roots (the thickening of the stem and roots), which produces wood and bark.

topics.title: INTERNAL STRUCTURE OF A FLOWERING PLANT : STEM

subject: Biology
q: The manufacture of carbohydrates by plants takes place only in
explanation: Photosynthesis, the process by which plants manufacture carbohydrates, requires chlorophyll. Chlorophyll is the green pigment found in chloroplasts of plant cells that absorbs light energy for photosynthesis. Therefore, any part of the plant that contains chlorophyll can manufacture carbohydrates.

subject: Biology
q: In a water culture experiment, a plant showed poor growth and yellowing of the leaves. These may be due to deficiency in
explanation: The symptoms described in the question are typical of a magnesium deficiency in plants. Magnesium is a vital component of chlorophyll, the molecule that absorbs sunlight for photosynthesis. Without sufficient magnesium, the plant cannot produce enough chlorophyll, leading to yellowing of the leaves (chlorosis) and poor growth.

subject: Biology
q: In million's test, when the reagent is added to a protein food item, a white precipitate is produced which turns
explanation: In Million's test, a protein food item is tested by adding a reagent. If a white precipitate is produced, it turns brick-red colour on heating. This confirms the presence of proteins.

subject: Biology
q: Regulation of blood sugar level takes place in the
explanation: The regulation of blood sugar level takes place in the pancreas. The pancreas produces two hormones, insulin and glucagon, which work together to maintain a steady level of glucose, or sugar, in the blood. Insulin decreases the level of glucose in the blood, while glucagon increases it.

subject: Biology
q: Unicellular organisms transport essential nutrients directly to all parts of their bodies by the process of diffusion because, they have
options: a large volume of surface area ratio
a large surface area to volume ratio
explanation: Unicellular organisms have a large surface area to volume ratio which allows for efficient diffusion of nutrients throughout the organism. Diffusion is the process by which molecules move from an area of higher concentration to an area of lower concentration. In the case of unicellular organisms, this means that nutrients can easily and quickly reach all parts of the organism.

subject: Biology
q: The heart of the adult frog consist of
explanation: The heart of an adult frog consists of one ventricle and two auricles. The ventricle is the chamber of the heart that pumps blood out of the heart, while the auricles (also known as atria in mammals) are the chambers where blood enters the heart.

subject: Biology
q: In adult mammalian blood, the cells which lack nuclei are the
explanation: In adult mammalian blood, the cells which lack nuclei are the erythrocytes, also known as red blood cells. These cells lack nuclei and other organelles in order to maximize space for hemoglobin, the protein that binds to oxygen and allows erythrocytes to transport oxygen throughout the body.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following movements occur during exhalation?
options: The diaphragm contracts and the intercostal muscle relax
explanation: During exhalation, the diaphragm and intercostal muscles relax. This causes the thoracic cavity to decrease in volume, increasing the pressure and forcing air out of the lungs. Therefore, option 1 is correct.

topics.title: RESPIRATION : THE MECHANISM OF GASEOUS EXCHANGE IN PLANTS AND MAMMALS

subject: Biology
q: In which of the following groups of animals is the malpighian tubule found?
explanation: Malpighian tubules are found in insects and are used for the removal of nitrogenous wastes. They are not found in reptiles, amphibians, or worms. Therefore, the correct answer is option 2, which includes crickets, houseflies, and grasshoppers, all of which are insects.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following is not a function of the mammalian skeleton?
explanation: The mammalian skeleton serves several functions including support, protection, and aiding movement. However, it does not play a direct role in transpiration.

topics.title: SUPPORT AND MOVEMENT : TYPES AND FUNCTIONS OF THE SKELETON

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer the question


The ovary represented is
explanation: inferior: Refers to an ovary that is below the attachment of the petals, sepals and stamens. The hypanthium may be fused to the ovary wall or attached to the top of the ovary.

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer the question

The corolla is partly represented by
explanation: I-In plants, the term "corolla" refers to a collection of petals that strongly displays colour and encircles the stamen and carpel, the reproductive organs of a flower. Therefore, corolla or petals refers to the second whorl of a flower, which is internal to the calyx.

topics.title: INTERNAL STRUCTURE OF A FLOWERING PLANT : LEAF

subject: Biology
q: The most reliable estimate of growth is by measuring changes in
explanation: The most reliable estimate of growth in organisms is by measuring changes in dry weight. This is because dry weight is a measure of the total mass of the organism excluding water. It is a more accurate measure of growth as it takes into account all the components of the organism, including bones, muscles, and organs, unlike length, volume, or surface area which may not accurately reflect the overall growth of the organism.

subject: Biology
q: A dry fruit formed from two or more carpels containing several seeds is a
explanation: A dry fruit formed from two or more carpels containing several seeds is a capsule. A capsule is a type of dry fruit that is derived from two or more carpels, and typically contains multiple seeds. Other options like follicle, legume, and schizocarp are also types of dry fruits, but they are not typically formed from two or more carpels or contain multiple seeds.

subject: Biology
q: The outermost embryonic membrane in the mammal is the
explanation: The outermost embryonic membrane in mammals is the chorion. The chorion is one of the four main embryonic membranes, and it is the outermost one, forming the fetal part of the placenta in mammals. The other options, amnion, allantois, and yolk sac, are also embryonic membranes, but they are not the outermost.

topics.title: INTERNAL STRUCTURE OF A FLOWERING PLANT : LEAF

subject: Biology
q: The small masses of nervous tissues in which many neurones have their nuclei are called
explanation: The small masses of nervous tissues in which many neurons have their nuclei are called ganglia. Ganglia are structures in the nervous system that contain a number of nerve cell bodies. They are found in the peripheral nervous system and are responsible for relaying information to and from the central nervous system.

subject: Biology
q: A group of organisms of different species living in a particular area is described as a
explanation: A group of organisms of different species living in a particular area is described as a community. In ecology, a community is an assemblage of populations of different species, interacting with one another. It differs from a population, which refers to individuals of a single species, and from a colony, which refers to individuals of the same species living closely together.

topics.title: ECOLOGY : FACTORS AFFECTING THE DISTRIBUTION OF ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following is the direct consequence of transferring energy from one trophic level to another?
options: An increase in the total numbers of resulting individuals
explanation: The direct consequence of transferring energy from one trophic level to another is a decrease in the resulting biomass. This is due to the fact that not all energy is transferred from one trophic level to the next. Some energy is lost as heat, and some is used by the organisms for their own metabolic processes. As a result, the biomass (the total mass of organisms) decreases as you move up the trophic levels.

topics.title: ECOLOGY : ENERGY FLOW IN THE ECOSYSTEM

subject: Biology
q: The condition that encourages denitrification is
explanation: Denitrification is the process by which nitrate (NO3-) in the soil is reduced by bacteria to form nitrogen gas (N2), which is then released into the atmosphere. This process is anaerobic, meaning it occurs in the absence of oxygen. Therefore, low soil oxygen encourages denitrification.

subject: Biology
q: A fresh water plant such as water lily can solve the problem of buoyancy by the possession of
explanation: Aerenchyma is a type of plant tissue that contains large air spaces, which provide a low-resistance internal pathway for the exchange of gases such as oxygen and ethylene between the plant above the water and the submerged tissues. This helps the plant to float or maintain buoyancy in water. Therefore, a fresh water plant such as water lily can solve the problem of buoyancy by the possession of aerenchymatous tissues.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : STRUCTURAL ADAPTATIONS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: The sequence of the biomes in Nigeria from port Harcourt to Damaturu is
options: estuarine → rain forest → Guinea Savannah → Sahel Savannah
estuarine → Guinea savannah → rain forest → Sahel savannah
explanation: Biomes are large ecological areas on the earth's surface, with fauna and flora (animals and plants) adapting to their environment. In Nigeria, moving from Port Harcourt (located in the south) to Damaturu (located in the north), the sequence of biomes is as follows: estuarine (near the coast), rain forest (further inland), Guinea Savannah (even further inland), and Sahel Savannah (in the far north, near the Sahara desert). Therefore, the correct sequence is estuarine → rain forest → Guinea Savannah → Sahel Savannah.

topics.title: LOCAL(NIGERIAN) BIOMES : TROPICAL RAINFOREST~~LOCAL(NIGERIAN) BIOMES : GUINEA SAVANNA

subject: Biology
q: Soil micro-organisms are beneficial because of their involvement in
explanation: Soil micro-organisms are beneficial because they play a crucial role in the cycling of nutrients. They help in the decomposition of organic matter and the release of nutrients into the soil, which can then be used by plants. This process is not related to photosynthesis, translocation, or respiration using soil air.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following groups of diseases are associated with water? I Onchocerciasis II Schistosomiasis III Dracunculiasis IV Elephantiasis V Taeniasis
explanation: Onchocerciasis, Schistosomiasis, and Dracunculiasis are all diseases that are associated with water. Onchocerciasis is caused by a parasitic worm that is transmitted through the bite of infected blackflies that breed in fast-flowing rivers. Schistosomiasis is caused by parasitic worms that live in certain types of freshwater snails. Dracunculiasis, also known as Guinea worm disease, is caused by a parasitic worm that people get from drinking unfiltered water containing tiny water fleas infected with the larvae of the Guinea worm.

subject: Biology
q: One of the ways which body cells differ from gamete cells is in the
explanation: Body cells, also known as somatic cells, differ from gamete cells in the number of chromosomes they contain. Body cells are diploid, meaning they contain two sets of chromosomes, one from each parent. On the other hand, gamete cells, which are the reproductive cells (sperm and egg), are haploid, meaning they contain only one set of chromosomes. The type of centromeres, chromatide they contain do not differentiate body cells from gamete cells, and the concept of 'chromosome pairs' is not applicable to gamete cells as they are haploid.

subject: Biology
q: In blood transfusion, agglutination occurs when
options: white blood cells from two individuals meet
explanation: Agglutination in blood transfusion occurs when contrasting antigens and antibodies meet. Antigens are substances that can trigger an immune response, causing the production of an antibody as part of the body's defense against infection and disease. Antibodies are proteins produced by the immune system to neutralize or destroy antigens. If the antigens in the donor blood and the antibodies in the recipient blood are incompatible, agglutination (clumping of cells) occurs, which can be fatal.

subject: Biology
q:
Use the graph below to answer the question


The unrestricted pattern is represented by
explanation: The unrestricted pattern in the graph is represented by II.

topics.title: THE ECOLOGY OF POPULATIONS : FACTORS AFFECTING POPULATION SIZES

subject: Biology
q:
Use the graph below to answer the question

The line that represents efficient recycling combined with stringent conservation is
explanation: The line that represents efficient recycling combined with stringent conservation is IV

topics.title: HUMANS AND ENVIRONMENT : CONSERVATION OF NATURAL RESOURCES

subject: Biology
q: After one week of life, the weight of five chicks of the same sex hatched simultaneously from the eggs of the same hen and fed on the same diet were 45g, 40g, 35g, 33g and 30g. This is an example of
explanation: The differences in weight among the chicks, despite being of the same sex, hatched simultaneously from the eggs of the same hen and fed on the same diet, is an example of variation. Variation refers to the differences in physical or genetic characteristics between individuals within the same species. These differences can be due to genetic factors, environmental factors, or a combination of both.

topics.title: VARIATION IN POPULATION : APPLICATION OF VARIATION

subject: Biology
q: The phenotype of an individual can be summed up as the
explanation: The phenotype of an individual refers to the observable physical properties of an organism; these include the organism's appearance, development, and behavior. The totality of the expressed traits is the phenotype.

topics.title: HEREDITY : INHERITANCE OF CHARACTERS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: The correct increasing order of size for the cell components responsible for heredity is
explanation: The correct increasing order of size for the cell components responsible for heredity is DNA → gene → chromosome → nucleus. DNA (Deoxyribonucleic acid) is the smallest unit and it forms the genes. Genes are segments of DNA that carry hereditary information and are located on chromosomes. Chromosomes are located inside the nucleus of the cell, which is the largest of the four.

subject: Biology
q: A sex-linked character cannot be passed on directly from
explanation: A sex-linked character cannot be passed on directly from father to son. This is because sex-linked traits are located on the X chromosome. Males have one X and one Y chromosome (XY), while females have two X chromosomes (XX). A father passes on his Y chromosome to his son, not his X chromosome, so he cannot pass on X-linked traits directly to his son.

subject: Biology
q: The biological association that contributes directly to succession in a community is
explanation: The biological association that contributes directly to succession in a community is competition. Succession is the process by which the structure of a biological community evolves over time. Competition between species for resources such as food, water, and space drives this process, as some species become more dominant over time, while others decline or disappear.

topics.title: THE ECOLOGY OF POPULATIONS : ECOLOGICAL SUCCESSION

subject: Biology
q: The group of insects that have mouth parts adapted for both piercing and sucking is
explanation: The mouthparts of mosquitoes, tse-tse flies, and aphids are adapted for both piercing and sucking. They have a proboscis, a long, slender, tubular feeding and sucking organ, that they use to pierce the skin or plant tissues and suck up the fluids. This is not the case for cockroaches, houseflies, moths, beetles, and grasshoppers.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : ADAPTIVE COLOURATION AND ITS FUNCTIONS

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer the question


The bird's bill adapted for fishing is labelled
explanation: The bird's bill adapted for fishing in the diagram is labelled IV,pointed beak

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : ADAPTIVE COLOURATION AND ITS FUNCTIONS

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer the question

Toes of the feet ending in sharp, curved hook suitable for holding and tearing are most likely to belong to the bird with the bill in
explanation: Toes of the feet ending in sharp, curved hook suitable for holding and tearing are most likely to belong to the bird with the bill in II

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : ADAPTIVE COLOURATION AND ITS FUNCTIONS

subject: Biology
q: In the honey bee colony, the drones are
options: sterile males with reduced mouth parts
explanation: A drone is a male honey bee. Unlike the female worker bee, drones do not have stingers. They gather neither nectar nor pollen and are unable to feed without assistance from worker bees. A drone's only role is to mate with an unfertilized queen.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : BEHAVIOURAL ADAPTATIONS IN SOCIAL ANIMALS

subject: Biology
q: In the whistling pine, leaves are reduced to brown scales and young stems are green. This is an adaptation for
explanation: In the whistling pine, the leaves are reduced to brown scales and the young stems are green. This is an adaptation for reducing transpiration. Transpiration is the process where plants lose water to the air. By reducing the surface area of the leaves, the plant can conserve water by reducing the amount of water that is lost to the air.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : STRUCTURAL ADAPTATIONS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: The best explanation for the theories of natural selection is that
options: all organisms have equal capacity for survival in their habitats
organisms have varying capacities for survival in their habitats
organisms compete for resource and better competitors survive and thrive
habitats allow only organisms that will not have to compete for survival
explanation: The theory of natural selection, also known as survival of the fittest, suggests that species that adapt to their environment are more likely to survive and pass on the genes that aided their success. This process causes species to gradually evolve. Option 3, 'organisms compete for resources and better competitors survive and thrive', best explains this theory as it highlights the competition for resources and survival of the fittest.

subject: Biology
q: The basic point of impact by changes which produce mutation is the
explanation: A mutation is a change that occurs in our DNA sequence, either due to mistakes when the DNA is copied or as the result of environmental factors such as UV light and cigarette smoke. Mutations can occur in both the gametes and the somatic cells. However, only mutations that occur in the gametes can be passed on to offspring. Therefore, the basic point of impact by changes which produce mutation is the chromosomes, which are part of every cell, including gametes.

subject: Biology
q: The habitat of the cysticercus of Taenia solium is
explanation:
The cysticercus of Taenia solium , also known as the pork tapeworm, is a stage in the life cycle of the parasite. This stage of the parasite is typically found in the muscles of pigs, making 'Muscles of pig', the correct answer.
The habitat of the cysticercus of Taenia solium is the muscle tissue of pigs (the intermediate host). In humans (the definitive host), the cysticerci can also develop in various tissues, including the brain, muscles, and other organs, leading to a condition called cysticercosis.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : ADAPTIVE COLOURATION AND ITS FUNCTIONS

subject: Biology
q: The organism that has a hydrostatic skeleton is
explanation: An earthworm has a hydrostatic skeleton. A hydrostatic skeleton is a structure found in many soft-bodied animals consisting of a fluid-filled cavity, the coelom, surrounded by muscles. The pressure of the fluid and action of the surrounding muscles are used to change an organism's shape and produce movement, such as burrowing or swimming.

topics.title: SUPPORT AND MOVEMENT : SUPPORTING TISSUES IN ANIMALS

subject: Biology
q: The possession of scales, laying of eggs with shells and bony structure of the head are characteristics shared by
explanation: Birds and reptiles share the characteristics of having scales, laying eggs with shells, and having a bony structure of the head. Fishes do not lay eggs with shells, and molluscs do not have a bony structure of the head.

topics.title: STRUCTURAL/BEHAVIOURAL ADAPTATIONS OF VERTEBRATES : BIRD~~STRUCTURAL/BEHAVIOURAL ADAPTATIONS OF VERTEBRATES : BONY FISH

subject: Biology
q: The group of Arthropods that has no antennae is the
explanation: Arachnida, which includes spiders, scorpions, mites, and ticks, is the group of Arthropods that does not have antennae. The other groups listed, Crustacea, Chilopoda, and Diplopoda, all have antennae.

subject: Biology
q: The structure that is most commonly identified in all living cells under the light microscope is the
explanation: The nucleus is the most commonly identified structure in all living cells under the light microscope. It is the control center of the cell and contains the cell's DNA. The other options, mitochondrion, chloroplast, and ribosome, are also present in cells but are not as easily identifiable under a light microscope due to their smaller size.

topics.title: LIVING ORGANISMS : CELL STRUCTURE AND FUNCTIONS OF CELL COMPONENTS

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following is an organ?
explanation: An organ is a group of tissues that work together to perform a specific function or set of functions in an organism's body. The liver is an organ that performs many vital functions, including detoxification, protein synthesis, and production of biochemicals necessary for digestion. The other options, guard cell, xylem bundle, and blood, are not organs. A guard cell is a type of cell found on plant leaves. A xylem bundle is a type of tissue in plants, and blood is a body fluid.

topics.title: INTERNAL STRUCTURE OF A MAMMAL : MAMMALIAN INTERNAL ORGANS

subject: Biology
q:

explanation:
II-Antenna
