topics.title: COORDINATION AND CONTROL : THE SENSE ORGANS

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer the question

The structure labeled III represents the
explanation: The labium is often called an insect's lower lip. The labium is positioned towards the back of the head and helps hold food in place when the insect feeds. The labium actually represents the fused pair of ancestral second maxillae.

topics.title: COORDINATION AND CONTROL : THE SENSE ORGANS

subject: Biology
q: The division of a body into two equal halves along a longitudinal plane is called
explanation: The division of a body into two equal halves along a longitudinal plane is called bilateral symmetry. This type of symmetry is seen in organisms like humans, where the left and right sides of the body are mirror images of each other. The other options, transverse section, radial symmetry, and longitudinal section, do not correctly describe this concept.

subject: Biology
q: In which of the following organisms does each cell combine the function of nutrition, reproduction and growth?
explanation: Spirogyra is a type of green algae where each cell is capable of carrying out all the life processes independently.

topics.title: LIVING ORGANISMS : CELL STRUCTURE AND FUNCTIONS OF CELL COMPONENTS

subject: Biology
q: They key event in the transition of the amphibians from water to land is the
explanation: The replacement of gills with lungs allowed amphibians to breathe in air, which is a necessary adaptation for life on land.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : STRUCTURAL ADAPTATIONS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: In plant cells, the chloroplast are located
explanation: The cytoplasm is the site where most of the cell's metabolic reactions occur, including photosynthesis, which is carried out by the chloroplasts.

topics.title: LIVING ORGANISMS : CELL STRUCTURE AND FUNCTIONS OF CELL COMPONENTS

subject: Biology
q: In the woody stems, gaseous exchange takes place through the
explanation: In woody stems, gaseous exchange takes place through the lenticels. Lenticels are small, corky pores or narrow slits in the stem of a woody plant that allow direct exchange of gases between the internal tissues and atmosphere through the bark, which is otherwise impermeable to gases.

topics.title: INTERNAL STRUCTURE OF A FLOWERING PLANT : STEM

subject: Biology
q: Substance manufactured by the leaves are transported to other parts of the plant through the
explanation: Substances manufactured by the leaves are transported to other parts of the plant through the phloem. The phloem is the vascular tissue in plants that conducts sugars and other metabolic products downward from the leaves.

subject: Biology
q: After a meal of yam has been digested, the highest concentration of glucose is to be found in the
explanation: After a meal of yam has been digested, the highest concentration of glucose is to be found in the hepatic portal vein. The hepatic portal vein is a blood vessel that carries blood from the gastrointestinal tract, gallbladder, pancreas and spleen to the liver. This blood contains nutrients and toxins extracted from digested contents.

subject: Biology
q: The dark reaction of photosynthesis involves
options: fixation of carbon (IV) oxide to give six-carbon sugar
fixation of carbon (IV) oxide with the help of oxygen
use of carbon (IV) oxide to produce glucose using ATP
fixation of carbon (IV) oxide on chlorophyll using hydrogen
explanation:
The dark reaction, also known as the Calvin cycle, is a light-independent reaction in photosynthesis. It uses the energy from ATP and NADPH produced in the light-dependent reactions to fix carbon dioxide (CO2) and produce glucose (C6H12O6). This process occurs in the stroma of the chloroplast and involves the several steps.

The other options are incorrect because:

Fixation of carbon (IV) oxide to give six-carbon sugar: This option is incorrect because the dark reaction produces a 3-carbon molecule (G3P), not a 6-carbon sugar.

Fixation of carbon (IV) oxide with the help of oxygen: This option is incorrect because the dark reaction does not involve oxygen; it's a light-independent reaction that uses ATP and NADPH produced in the light-dependent reactions.

Fixation of carbon (IV) oxide on chlorophyll using hydrogen: This option is incorrect because chlorophyll is involved in the light-dependent reactions, not the dark reaction. Additionally, hydrogen is not directly involved in the carbon fixation process in the dark reaction.

subject: Biology
q: Mammals acclimatize to reduced oxygen content at high altitudes by
options: the stimulation of marrow to reduce the amount of erythrocytes produced
increasing the rate at which erythrocytes are destroyed
the stimulation of marrow to produce more erythrocytes
increasing the break down of the protein portion of the erythrocytes
explanation: Mammals acclimatize to reduced oxygen content at high altitudes by the stimulation of marrow to produce more erythrocytes. Erythrocytes, also known as red blood cells, carry oxygen from the lungs to the rest of the body. When the oxygen content is reduced, the body responds by producing more erythrocytes to increase the oxygen-carrying capacity of the blood.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : ADAPTIVE COLOURATION AND ITS FUNCTIONS

subject: Biology
q: The modes of nutrition in Nitrobacter, Ascaris and Homo sapiens respectively are
explanation: The modes of nutrition in Nitrobacter, Ascaris and Homo sapiens respectively are chemosynthetic, parasitic and holozoic. Nitrobacter is a type of bacteria that obtains energy by oxidizing nitrite into nitrate, a process known as chemosynthesis. Ascaris, a type of parasitic worm, obtains nutrients by feeding on the host's blood and tissues. Homo sapiens, or humans, have a holozoic mode of nutrition, which involves the ingestion of complex organic substances that are then broken down into simpler substances through digestion.

subject: Biology
q: The richest source of vitamin A are
explanation: Vitamin A is a fat-soluble vitamin that is good for healthy vision, skin, bones and other tissues in the body. Vitamin A often works as an antioxidant, fighting cell damage, but it also has many other uses. Carrots are rich in beta-carotene, a compound your body changes into vitamin A (retinol). Palm oil is also a good source of beta-carotene. Therefore, the richest sources of vitamin A are palm oil and carrot.

subject: Biology
q: The force that holds water together in the form of a stream within the xylem tube is the
options: cohesion of water molecules to one another by hydrogen bonds
force of gravity attacking the water molecules through the hydrogen bonds
attraction between the water and xylem osmotic force
normal flow of water from the ground with help of gravitational force
explanation: The force that holds water together in the form of a stream within the xylem tube is the cohesion of water molecules to one another by hydrogen bonds. This is known as cohesion-tension theory. The water molecules 'stick' to each other, creating a continuous flow of water from the roots to the leaves. This is possible due to the property of cohesion (attraction between similar types of particles or molecules) and adhesion (attraction between different particles or molecules).

subject: Biology
q: Yellowing of leaves is a symptom associated with deficiency of
explanation: Yellowing of leaves, also known as chlorosis, is a symptom associated with deficiency of several nutrients including magnesium, nitrogen and iron. Magnesium is a component of the chlorophyll molecule and its deficiency results in chlorosis. Nitrogen is a major component of amino acids, proteins, and nucleic acids and its deficiency also results in chlorosis. Iron is necessary for chlorophyll synthesis and its deficiency leads to chlorosis.

subject: Biology
q: The lymphatic system of mammals rejoins the blood circulatory system at the
explanation: The lymphatic system drains excess fluid from tissues and returns it to the bloodstream. This system rejoins the blood circulatory system at the subclavian vein. The subclavian vein is a pair of large veins, one on either side of the body, that carry blood from the arms back to the heart.

subject: Biology
q: In dissection, the rib cage of mammal has to be opened in order to expose the
explanation: In dissection, the rib cage of a mammal has to be opened in order to expose the heart. The heart is located in the thoracic cavity medial to the lungs and posterior to the sternum. It's surrounded by the rib cage for protection, hence the need to open the rib cage to expose it.

topics.title: INTERNAL STRUCTURE OF A MAMMAL : MAMMALIAN INTERNAL ORGANS

subject: Biology
q: Fertilization in humans usually takes place in the
explanation: Fertilization in humans usually takes place in the upper part of the oviduct, also known as the fallopian tube. After ovulation, the egg is swept into the fallopian tube where it can be fertilized if sperm are present.

subject: Biology
q: Insects visit flowers in order to
explanation: Insects visit flowers primarily to feed on the nectar. This is a form of mutualism where the insect gets food and the flower gets pollinated. While insects do help in pollination, it is not their primary purpose for visiting the flowers. Therefore, the correct answer is 'feed on the nectar'.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : BEHAVIOURAL ADAPTATIONS IN SOCIAL ANIMALS

subject: Biology
q: In epigeal germination, the cotyledons are
options: carried above the ground by the elongation hypocotyl
pulled underground by the elongating epicotyl
carried above the ground by the elongating epicotyl
explanation: In epigeal germination, the cotyledons are carried above the ground by the elongation of the hypocotyl. The hypocotyl is the part of the stem of an embryo plant beneath the stalks of the seed leaves (cotyledons) and directly above the root. Therefore, the correct answer is 'carried above the ground by the elongation hypocotyl'.

topics.title: GROWTH : GERMINATION OF SEEDS AND CONDITION NECESSARY FOR GERMINATION OF SEEDS

subject: Biology
q: Bacteria multiply rapidly by means of
explanation: Bacteria multiply rapidly by means of binary fission. Binary fission is a form of asexual reproduction used by most prokaryotes. In this process, a single cell divides into two identical cells. Therefore, the correct answer is 'binary fission'.

subject: Biology
q: The correct sequence for the perception of smell in mammals is
options: dissolved chemicals → sensory cells → olfactory nerve → brain
explanation: The correct sequence for the perception of smell in mammals is dissolved chemicals → sensory cells → olfactory nerve → brain

topics.title: COORDINATION AND CONTROL : THE SENSE ORGANS

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer the question


The part labelled III is for
explanation: It is for insulation.The part of the skin responsible for insulation is the subcutaneous layer (hypodermis). It is the deepest layer of the skin, located beneath the dermis. The hypodermis consists mainly of adipose tissue (fat cells) and connective tissue. It provides insulation by trapping heat, cushioning to protect muscles and bones from impact, and energy storage in the form of fat. The thickness of this layer varies depending on factors like age, sex, and body region.

topics.title: INTERNAL STRUCTURE OF A MAMMAL : MAMMALIAN INTERNAL ORGANS

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer the question

The structure labelled II is known as
explanation: It is known as sweat gland. The structure is the sweat gland. In a skin structure diagram, the sweat gland appears as a coiled tubular structure located in the dermis. It consists of a coiled secretory portion and a straight or wavy duct that extends to the skin surface, where it opens as a sweat pore.

topics.title: INTERNAL STRUCTURE OF A MAMMAL : MAMMALIAN INTERNAL ORGANS

subject: Biology
q: In mammals, the organ which performs digestive and endocrine functions is the
explanation: The pancreas in mammals performs both digestive and endocrine functions. It produces digestive enzymes that help in the breakdown of food. It also produces hormones like insulin and glucagon that regulate blood sugar levels, hence performing endocrine functions.

subject: Biology
q: The chitin in the exoskeleton of many arthropods is strengthened by
explanation: The chitin in the exoskeleton of many arthropods is strengthened by calcium compounds. Chitin is a complex carbohydrate that provides structural support to the exoskeleton. The addition of calcium compounds further hardens and strengthens the exoskeleton.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : STRUCTURAL ADAPTATIONS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer the question


The structure labelled II articulates with III to form
explanation: A hinge joint is a type of synovial joint that allows movement primarily in one plane, such as bending (flexion) and straightening (extension). Examples include the elbow joint, which allows flexion and extension of the forearm, the knee joint for bending and straightening the leg, and the ankle joint, which facilitates upward and downward movement of the foot.

topics.title: SUPPORT AND MOVEMENT : TYPES AND FUNCTIONS OF THE SKELETON

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer the question

Which of the bones is the radius?
explanation:
IV-radius. To distinguish between the radius and ulna in a diagram of the forearm:
- Look for the radius's smooth, rounded head at its proximal end, which articulates with the humerus.
- Note the ulna's prominent olecranon process extending towards the elbow.
- Identify the ulnar styloid process at the distal end of the ulna, contrasting with the radius, which articulates with the wrist without a prominent styloid process.

topics.title: SUPPORT AND MOVEMENT : TYPES AND FUNCTIONS OF THE SKELETON

subject: Biology
q: The construction of dams may lead to an increase in the prevalence of
explanation: The construction of dams can lead to an increase in the prevalence of diseases such as malaria, bilharziasis, and onchocerciasis. This is because the stagnant water bodies created by dams provide breeding grounds for the vectors of these diseases. Malaria is transmitted by mosquitoes, bilharziasis (also known as schistosomiasis) is caused by a type of flatworm that lives in fresh water, and onchocerciasis (or river blindness) is caused by a type of roundworm that is transmitted by blackflies that breed in fast-flowing rivers and streams.

subject: Biology
q: Floating microscopic heterotrophs are mostly grouped as
explanation: Floating microscopic heterotrophs are mostly grouped as zooplankton. Zooplankton are a type of plankton that consists of small animals and the immature stages of larger animals. They are heterotrophic (i.e., they cannot produce their own food) and float in the water column. Phytoplankton, on the other hand, are autotrophic (i.e., they can produce their own food through photosynthesis) and are not included in this group. Microbes and nekton are also not included in this group because they are not necessarily microscopic or floating.

topics.title: ECOLOGY : ENERGY FLOW IN THE ECOSYSTEM

subject: Biology
q: Vaccination is carried out in order to
options: increase the activity of white blood cells
increase the number of red blood cells
explanation: Vaccination is carried out in order to stimulate the production of antibodies. When a vaccine, which contains a weakened or dead form of a pathogen or its toxins, is introduced into the body, the immune system responds by producing antibodies. These antibodies can recognize and neutralize the pathogen if it enters the body in the future, thereby providing immunity against the disease caused by the pathogen.

subject: Biology
q: A soil consisting of alumina and iron (II)oxide is known as
explanation: Laterite is a soil type rich in iron and aluminium, and is commonly considered to have formed in hot and wet tropical areas. Nearly all laterites are of rusty-red coloration, because of high iron oxide content. They develop by intensive and prolonged weathering of the underlying parent rock.

topics.title: SOIL : CHARACTERISTICS OF DIFFERENT TYPES OF SOIL

subject: Biology
q: The sign + is used to indicate an association where an organism gains, while 0 is used where an organism is unaffected. An association indicated as +0 is known as
explanation: Commensalism is a type of relationship where one organism benefits while the other is unaffected. This is represented by the +0 symbol. The organism that benefits is called the commensal while the organism that is unaffected is called the host.

topics.title: ECOLOGY : SYMBIOTIC INTERACTIONS OF PLANTS AND ANIMALS

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer the question


The biomass is likely to increase in the sequence
explanation: The biomass is likely to increase in the sequence of 5→4→3→1

q:

Primary consumers are found in
explanation: Primary consumers in the diagram are found in 2, 3 and 7

topics.title: ECOLOGY : FOOD CHAINS FOOD WEBS AND TROPHIC LEVELS

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following characterizes the white mangrove?
explanation: White mangroves are characterized by their breathing roots, also known as pneumatophores. These roots protrude from the ground and help the plant to breathe, as they often grow in waterlogged soils where oxygen is scarce.

topics.title: ECOLOGY : FACTORS AFFECTING THE DISTRIBUTION OF ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: If the offspring of a cross between a brown mouse (bb) and a black mouse (BB) are allowed to interbreed, how many different genotypes would result?
explanation:
They will form three different genotypes. The BB is same as B while the bb is same as b so in the first filal generation,
B * b
Bb ( first filal generation)
So. Bb * Bb
BB Bb Bb bb ( second filal generatio)
So three genotypes ( BB, Bb and bb) are formed

topics.title: HEREDITY : INHERITANCE OF CHARACTERS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: The biological factor that is unique to each individual is the
explanation: DNA is unique to each individual. While eye color, blood group, and RNA can vary among individuals, they are not unique identifiers. DNA, on the other hand, contains the genetic code that determines all of an individual's physical traits and is different for each individual, except for identical twins.

subject: Biology
q: From evolutionary standpoint , the older a fossil-bearing rocks is, the more likely it is to contain
explanation: From an evolutionary standpoint, the older a fossil-bearing rock is, the more likely it is to contain invertebrates as opposed to vertebrates. This is because invertebrates evolved before vertebrates in the history of life on Earth. Therefore, older rocks are more likely to contain fossils of invertebrates, the first complex organisms to evolve.

subject: Biology
q: The very bright colours in some types of mushroom
options: are warning that they may be poisonous
perform the same function as bright colours in flowers
explanation: The bright colors in some types of mushrooms serve as a warning signal to potential predators that they may be poisonous. This is a form of aposematism, a biological phenomenon where a species develops conspicuous markings or colors as a warning to predators that it is harmful or unpalatable.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : ADAPTIVE COLOURATION AND ITS FUNCTIONS

subject: Biology
q: The least adaptive feature of aboreal life is the
explanation: The least adaptive feature of arboreal life is the counter shading of coat colour.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : ADAPTIVE COLOURATION AND ITS FUNCTIONS

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following is one of Lamarck's theories?
options: Some variations are more favourable to existence in a given environment than others
All living organisms are constantly involved in a struggle to existence
The size of a given population remains fairly constant
New species originates through the inheritance of acquired traits
explanation: One of Lamarck's theories is that new species originate through the inheritance of acquired traits. This is known as Lamarckism or Lamarckian inheritance. According to this theory, an organism can change during its lifetime in response to its environment, and those changes are then passed on to its offspring. This theory has largely been discredited in modern evolutionary biology in favor of Darwinian natural selection.

subject: Biology
q: The loud cry made by a brooding hen when a predator is around is meant to
options: attract cocks to come and fight the predator
advertise the boundaries of its territory to intruders
warn its chicks and other chickens of impending danger
explanation: The loud cry made by a brooding hen when a predator is around is meant to warn its chicks and other chickens of impending danger. This is a common behavior in many bird species, known as alarm calling. The purpose of this behavior is to alert other members of the group to the presence of a potential threat.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : BEHAVIOURAL ADAPTATIONS IN SOCIAL ANIMALS

subject: Biology
q: The most important environment factor which epiphytes in the rain forest compete for is
explanation: Epiphytes are plants that grow on other plants, but do not take nutrients from them. They are often found in rainforests where they can reach the light by growing on taller trees. Therefore, the most important environmental factor they compete for is light. Without sufficient light, they cannot perform photosynthesis effectively.

topics.title: ECOLOGY : FACTORS AFFECTING THE DISTRIBUTION OF ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer this question.


The part that will develop into an organ for feeling is labelled
explanation: It is the antenna. It is a sensory appendage attached to the head of a butterfly. It is used for the sense of smell and balance.

topics.title: COORDINATION AND CONTROL : THE SENSE ORGANS

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer this question.

The part labelled II is the
explanation: The silk thread is produced as a thick liquid, excreted through glands near the mouth, which is then spun into a thread by a spinneret located behind the jaws.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : STRUCTURAL ADAPTATIONS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following features are all associated with monocot?
options: Fibrous root system, branched network of veins and one seed leaf
Fibrous root system, two seed leaves and floral parts in threes
One seed leaf, petals in threes or groups of threes and parallel venation of leaves
One seed leaf, net-veined leaves and petals in threes or multiples of three
explanation:
Feature In monocotsIn dicots
Number of parts of each flowerin threes(flowers are trimerous)in fours or fives (tetramerous or pentamerous)
Number of furrows or pores in pollen one three
Number of cotyledons (leaves in the seed) one two
Arrangement of vascular bundles in the stem scattered in concentric circles
Roots are adventitious develop from the radicle
Arrangement of major leaf veins parallel reticulate

Monocots, or monocotyledons, are a group of flowering plants that are characterized by having one seed leaf or cotyledon, petals in threes or groups of threes, and parallel venation of leaves. Therefore, the features associated with monocots are one seed leaf, petals in threes or groups of threes and parallel venation of leaves.

topics.title: INTERNAL STRUCTURE OF A FLOWERING PLANT : LEAF

subject: Biology
q: The set of fins that controls steering, balancing and change of direction and pitch in fish is
explanation: The pectoral and pelvic fins in fish are responsible for steering, balancing, and changing direction and pitch. The pectoral fins are located on either side of the fish, just behind the gills, and are used for steering and maintaining balance. The pelvic fins, located on the underside of the fish, help with up and down movement and turning.

topics.title: STRUCTURAL/BEHAVIOURAL ADAPTATIONS OF VERTEBRATES : BONY FISH

subject: Biology
q: The most recently evolved structure in animals is the
explanation: Hair is the most recently evolved structures in animals.

subject: Biology
q: Coelom is absent in the class of animals termed
explanation: Coelom, a fluid-filled body cavity, is absent in the class of animals termed Coelenterata (also known as Cnidaria). This group includes animals like jellyfish and sea anemones. They have a simpler body structure compared to coelomates, with a single opening serving as both mouth and anus.

subject: Biology
q: A characteristic feature of vertebrates that is unique to mammals is
explanation: The possession of a scrotum is a characteristic feature that is unique to mammals. The scrotum is an external pouch of skin and muscle that contains the testes. It is not found in other vertebrates. While other options like pentadactyl limbs and pulmonary circulation are found in other vertebrates as well, and parental care is not exclusive to mammals.

subject: Biology
q: The order in which organic evolution has progressed in plants is
explanation: The order of organic evolution in plants is believed to have progressed from simpler to more complex forms. The correct order is Schizophyta (simplest bacteria), Thallophyta (algae and fungi), Bryophyta (mosses and liverworts), Pteridophyta (ferns and their relatives), and Spermatophyta (seed plants, including gymnosperms and angiosperms).

subject: Biology
q: In which part of the human body does the secretion of the growth hormone occur?
explanation: The growth hormone, also known as somatotropin, is secreted by the pituitary gland, which is located at the base of the brain in the head region. This hormone plays a crucial role in cell reproduction, cell regeneration, and growth in humans.

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer this question.


The parts labelled I and II make up the
explanation: The Malpighian body, also known as the renal corpuscle, is a key component of the nephron in the kidney. It consists of the glomerulus, a network of capillaries where blood filtration occurs, and Bowman's capsule, a cup-shaped structure that surrounds the glomerulus and collects the filtrate. This structure initiates the process of urine formation through blood filtration in the kidneys.

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer this question.

The mammals, reabsorption of salt takes place in
explanation: Reabsorption of salt takes place in the proximal convoluted tubule.

subject: Biology
q: The part of the brain that controls body posture in mammals is the
explanation: The cerebellum is the part of the brain that controls body posture in mammals. It is responsible for coordinating voluntary movements, balance, muscle coordination, and body posture. The thalamus is involved in sensory and motor signal relay and the regulation of consciousness and sleep. The spinal cord is the main pathway for information connecting the brain and peripheral nervous system. The cerebrum is responsible for higher brain functions such as thought and action.

subject: Biology
q: Peripheral arrangement of vascular tissues in dicots is a characteristic of the internal structure of the
explanation: The peripheral arrangement of vascular tissues is a characteristic of the internal structure of the stem in dicots. In dicot stems, the vascular bundles (xylem, phloem, cambium) are arranged in a ring pattern around the pith. The leaf's vascular tissue is not peripherally arranged, it is spread throughout the leaf in veins. The petiole, which is the stalk that joins a leaf to a stem, has vascular bundles but not in a peripheral arrangement. The root has a central vascular cylinder, not a peripheral arrangement.

topics.title: INTERNAL STRUCTURE OF A FLOWERING PLANT : STEM

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer this question.


The substance labelled II originates from
explanation: The substance labelled II is oxygen. Note that in photosynthesis, the oxygen given off comes from water (IV). During photosynthesis, the oxygen released comes primarily from water molecules (H2O) rather than from the Elodea plant itself. In the process of photosynthesis, plants use sunlight to convert water and carbon dioxide (CO2) into glucose (a form of sugar) and oxygen (O2). The oxygen gas released as a byproduct of this process originates from the splitting of water molecules within the plant cells, not from any external source such as the Elodea plant itself.

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer this question.

The gas occupying the space labelled I is
explanation: It is oxygen, tiny bubbles of the gas are seen to appear on the surface of the leaves which will eventually move up to the test tube. To be sure about the gas, it is tested with a glowing splinter. It was observed that it rekindles the glowing splinter, hence confirms it as oxygen.

topics.title: RESPIRATION : THE MECHANISM OF GASEOUS EXCHANGE IN PLANTS AND MAMMALS

subject: Biology
q: The scapula and the ischium are part of the
explanation:
The left and right clavicle and scapula make up the pectoral girdle.
The ischium forms the lower and back part of the hip bone, which is part of the pelvis. They are all part of the Appendicular skeleton.

topics.title: SUPPORT AND MOVEMENT : TYPES AND FUNCTIONS OF THE SKELETON

subject: Biology
q: Bacteria in the large intestine of man are important in the
options: synthesis of vitamins K and B2
explanation: Bacteria in the large intestine of man are important in the synthesis of vitamins K and B2. They also help in the digestion of cellulose.

subject: Biology
q: Short-sightedness can be corrected by lenses which are
explanation: Short-sightedness, also known as myopia, is a condition where the eye can see close objects clearly but distant objects appear blurred. This is due to the eye's lens focusing light in front of the retina, rather than directly on it. This can be corrected by using concave lenses. Concave lenses are thinner at the center than at the edges, and they spread out light rays so that they can be focused on the retina correctly.

topics.title: COORDINATION AND CONTROL : THE SENSE ORGANS

subject: Biology
q: The inner ear contains two main organs namely the
explanation: The inner ear contains two main parts: the cochlea, which is involved in hearing, and the semicircular canals, which are involved in balance. The cochlea is a spiral-shaped organ that transforms sound vibrations from the middle ear into electrical signals. The semicircular canals are three fluid-filled tubes that provide the brain with information about orientation to maintain balance.

topics.title: COORDINATION AND CONTROL : THE SENSE ORGANS

subject: Biology
q: For growth to occur in organisms, the rate of
explanation: Growth in organisms occurs when the rate of anabolism (the process of building up or synthesizing compounds in the body, which requires energy) exceeds that of catabolism (the process of breaking down compounds in the body, which releases energy). This is because growth involves the synthesis of new structures and tissues, which is an anabolic process.

subject: Biology
q: The production of violet colouration, when dilute NaOH solution is added to a solution of food substance, followed by drops of 1% CuSO4 solution while shaking, indicates the presence of
explanation: The test described in the question is the Biuret test, which is used to detect the presence of proteins. When a solution of a protein is treated with dilute NaOH and then with a few drops of 1% CuSO4 solution, a violet coloration is produced. This is due to the formation of a complex between the copper ions and the peptide bonds in the protein.

subject: Biology
q: The greatest amount of energy will be obtained by the oxidation of 100kg of
explanation: Butter, which is a fat, contains more energy per gram than meat (protein), sugar (carbohydrate), or biscuits (a mix of carbohydrates, fats, and proteins). This is because fats have a higher energy content per gram than proteins or carbohydrates. Therefore, the greatest amount of energy would be obtained by the oxidation of 100kg of butter.

topics.title: ECOLOGY : ENERGY FLOW IN THE ECOSYSTEM

subject: Biology
q: The chamber of the mammalian heart with the thickest wall is the
explanation: The left ventricle has the thickest wall because it needs to pump blood to the rest of the body, against higher pressure, so it's stronger and more muscular than the other chambers.

subject: Biology
q: Serum differs from blood plasma because it
explanation: Serum is essentially blood plasma without fibrinogen and other clotting factors. It is the component that is left after allowing the blood to clot and removing the clot. Therefore, it lacks the blood protein, fibrinogen.

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer this question.


The part labelled II is the
explanation: It is the mesocarp, In a typical fruit the mesocarp is the middle layer. It is found inbetween the endocarp and epicarp.
