q:
Use the diagram below to answer this question.

The fruit represented is mainly dispersed by
explanation: It is dispersed by water, it is dispersed by water due to its fibrous mesocarp trapping air enables it to remain afloat.

topics.title: ECOLOGY : FACTORS AFFECTING THE DISTRIBUTION OF ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q:
In an experiment to estimate the volume of air in a soil sample using a measuring cylinder, it was found that:
Initial volume of water = p cm3
Volume of soil before mixing with water = q cm3
Final volume of water after adding soil = r cm3
From the data above, which of the following deductions is correct?
explanation:
After mixing the air present in the soil will escape due to stirring of the soil in the water, the final volume will be less.
Therefore, r < p + q.

topics.title: SOIL : CHARACTERISTICS OF DIFFERENT TYPES OF SOIL

subject: Biology
q: An ecological succession often leads to
explanation: Ecological succession is the process of change in the species structure of an ecological community over time. It often leads to an increase in species diversity as the environment becomes more stable and can support a wider range of species.

topics.title: THE ECOLOGY OF POPULATIONS : ECOLOGICAL SUCCESSION

subject: Biology
q: Atmospheric nitrogen is converted to soil nitrogen for plant use by
explanation: Atmospheric nitrogen is converted to soil nitrogen for plant use by the process of nitrification and through lightning. Nitrification is a process by which specific bacteria convert different forms of nitrogen-containing compounds. Atmospheric nitrogen is converted into nitrates by lightning, which can then be used by plants.

subject: Biology
q:
I. High birth rate and high immigration rate.
II. Low birth rate and high immigration rate.
III. Low mortality rate and low emigration rate.
IV. High mortality rate and high emigration rate.
Which combination of the above can cause rapid overcrowding in climax biotic communities and human settlements?
explanation: High birth rate and low mortality rate can cause rapid overcrowding in climax biotic communities and human settlements. High birth rate means more individuals are being added to the population, and low mortality rate means fewer individuals are leaving or dying, leading to an increase in population size.

topics.title: THE ECOLOGY OF POPULATIONS : POPULATION DENSITY AND OVERCROWDING

subject: Biology
q:
Use the graph below to answer this question.


The soil becomes toxic to plants when the concentration of its essential elements is in the range labelled
explanation: The part labelled "IV" depict that the soil essential concentration element, has increased maximally with very low plant growth graphically.

subject: Biology
q:
Use the graph below to answer this question.

The range at which soil essential elements concentration is recorded for optimal plant growth is marked
explanation: III gives the best range for optimal growth.At the part labelled "III", the soil essential elements concentration, has increased maximally with a very low plant growth showing optimal growth.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following growth activities in plants is brought about by gibberellins?
explanation: Gibberellins are plant hormones that regulate growth and influence various developmental processes, including stem elongation, germination, dormancy, flowering, sex expression, enzyme induction, and leaf and fruit senescence.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following are adaptations of animals to aquatic habitats?
explanation: Aquatic animals have adapted to their environment in several ways. Gills are used for respiration in water, streamlined bodies help to reduce resistance when moving through water, and the lateral line system is a sense organ in aquatic organisms, especially fish, that detects movement and vibration in the surrounding water. Lungs and spiracles are adaptations for air-breathing animals, not aquatic ones.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : ADAPTIVE COLOURATION AND ITS FUNCTIONS

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following is an adaptation of forest species?
explanation: Buttress roots are large, wide roots on all sides of a shallowly rooted tree. Typically, they are found mainly in large trees located in the tropical rain forest and they provide structural support. Few stomata, thick bark, and reduced leaves are adaptations to dry conditions, not forest conditions.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : STRUCTURAL ADAPTATIONS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: In a food chain, each succeeding level in a forward direction represents
options: an increase in the number of individuals
a decrease in the number of individuals
an increase in the biomass of individuals
a gain in the total energy being transferred
explanation: In a food chain, each succeeding level in a forward direction represents a decrease in the number of individuals. This is due to the fact that as energy is transferred up the food chain, there is a loss of energy at each trophic level due to the energy used for metabolic processes and heat loss. As a result, there is less energy available to support the same number of individuals at higher trophic levels, leading to a decrease in the number of individuals.

topics.title: ECOLOGY : FOOD CHAINS FOOD WEBS AND TROPHIC LEVELS

subject: Biology
q: The disaster that would have the least destructive impact on animal life and balance in nature is
explanation: Grasshopper pests would have the least destructive impact on animal life and balance in nature. While they can cause significant damage to crops, their impact on animal life and the overall balance of nature is less severe than the other options. Chemical pollution and oil spillage can have long-term, widespread effects on multiple species and ecosystems, while forest fires can result in the loss of habitat for many species.

topics.title: HUMANS AND ENVIRONMENT : POLLUTION AND ITS CONTROL

subject: Biology
q: The legs and beak of an egret resemble those of the heron because they
explanation: The legs and beak of an egret resemble those of the heron because they occupy similar niches. In ecology, a niche refers to the role and position a species has in its environment, including what it eats and how it interacts with other species. Both egrets and herons are wading birds that feed on fish and other small aquatic animals, so they have similar adaptations, such as long legs for wading in water and long, pointed beaks for catching prey.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : ADAPTIVE COLOURATION AND ITS FUNCTIONS

subject: Biology
q: The factors that determines the distribution of vegetation zones are
explanation: The distribution of vegetation zones is determined by several factors including temperature, light, rain and humidity. These factors influence the types of plants that can survive and thrive in a particular area. For example, temperature affects the rate of photosynthesis and plant growth, light is necessary for photosynthesis, rain provides water which is essential for plant life, and humidity affects the rate of evaporation and transpiration.

topics.title: ECOLOGY : FACTORS AFFECTING THE DISTRIBUTION OF ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: A cross between an albino female and a genetically normal male will result in offspring that are
explanation: Albinism is a recessive trait, which means that an individual must inherit two copies of the albino gene (one from each parent) to express the trait. If a genetically normal male (who does not carry the albino gene) mates with an albino female (who carries two copies of the albino gene), all of their offspring will inherit one normal gene from the father and one albino gene from the mother. This means that all of the offspring will be carriers of the albino gene, but they will not express the trait because they also have a normal gene. Therefore, they will all be phenotypically normal.

topics.title: HEREDITY : INHERITANCE OF CHARACTERS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: The pollutants that contribute to the depletion of the ozone layer in the atmosphere are
explanation: Chlorofluorocarbons (CFCs) are the main pollutants that contribute to the depletion of the ozone layer in the atmosphere. CFCs are compounds that contain chlorine, fluorine, and carbon. They are used in air conditioning systems, refrigerators, and aerosol propellants. When CFCs are released into the atmosphere, they can rise to the stratosphere where they are broken down by solar radiation, releasing chlorine atoms. These chlorine atoms can catalyze the destruction of ozone, leading to the thinning of the ozone layer, also known as the ozone hole.

topics.title: HUMANS AND ENVIRONMENT : POLLUTION AND ITS CONTROL

subject: Biology
q: The surest way to combine the best qualities of both parents in the offspring is by
explanation: Cross-breeding is the surest way to combine the best qualities of both parents in the offspring. This is because cross-breeding involves the mating of two different species or breeds, which allows for the combination of the best traits of both parents. Inbreeding, selective breeding, and pure breeding do not necessarily combine the best qualities of both parents.

topics.title: HEREDITY : APPLICATION OF THE PRINCIPLES OF HEREDITY

subject: Biology
q: Blood grouping in human beings is derived from combinations of
explanation: Alleles combinations are responsible for blood groups. There are three common blood type alleles: A, B, and O. Humans all have two alleles, one inherited from each parent. The possible combinations of the three alleles are; OO; AO; BO; AB; AA; BB.

topics.title: HEREDITY : INHERITANCE OF CHARACTERS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: The older fossil-bearing rocks, in contrast to the more recent ones, are more likely to contain
explanation: The older fossil-bearing rocks, in contrast to the more recent ones, are more likely to contain invertebrates rather than birds. This is because invertebrates evolved before birds in the history of life on Earth. The other options are incorrect because they do not accurately represent the types of organisms that would be found in older versus more recent fossil-bearing rocks.

subject: Biology
q: In a group of male Agama lizards, the one with the brightest head colour is the
explanation: In a group of male Agama lizards, the one with the brightest head colour is usually the dominant one. This is because in many animal species, bright colours are often a sign of dominance and are used to attract mates and intimidate rivals. Therefore, the correct answer is 'dominant'.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : BEHAVIOURAL ADAPTATIONS IN SOCIAL ANIMALS

subject: Biology
q: Examples of water-borne and sex-linked diseases are
explanation: Water-borne diseases are diseases that are transmitted through contaminated water. Dracunculiasis, also known as Guinea worm disease, is a water-borne disease. Sex-linked diseases are genetic diseases that are determined by the sex chromosomes. Haemophilia is a sex-linked disease because it is usually passed from mother to son on the X chromosome. Therefore, the correct answer is 'dracunculiasis and haemophilia'.

subject: Biology
q: The mutation theory of organic evolution was propounded by
explanation: The mutation theory of organic evolution was propounded by Hugo de Vries. This theory, also known as the mutation theory of evolution, proposes that new species are formed from the sudden and drastic changes in the genes of an individual, which are then passed on to its offspring. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Hugo de Vries'.

subject: Biology
q: A certain savanna grasshopper changes colour from green during the rainy season to brown during the dry season and black after the dry season bush fires. The reason for these colour changes is that the
explanation: The grasshopper changes its color to blend in with its environment and avoid being noticed by predators. This is a survival strategy known as camouflage. During the rainy season, the environment is green, so the grasshopper is green. During the dry season, the environment turns brown, so the grasshopper turns brown. After bush fires, the environment is black, so the grasshopper turns black.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : ADAPTIVE COLOURATION AND ITS FUNCTIONS

subject: Biology
q: Complex social behaviour and organization are found mostly in
explanation: Mammals, particularly primates, are known for their complex social behavior and organization. They live in groups with defined roles and hierarchies, communicate in sophisticated ways, and exhibit a wide range of behaviors related to cooperation, competition, and social learning. While insects, birds, and reptiles can also exhibit social behavior, it is generally not as complex as that of mammals.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : BEHAVIOURAL ADAPTATIONS IN SOCIAL ANIMALS

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following structural features are adapted for uses other than water conservation?
explanation: Feathers in birds are adapted for uses other than water conservation. They are primarily used for flight and insulation, but also play roles in display during courtship and in protecting the bird's skin from damage. The other options listed - succulent stems, scales in animals, and spines in plants - are all adaptations primarily related to water conservation.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : STRUCTURAL ADAPTIONS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: Water fleas, wood lice and barnacles belong to the group
explanation: Water fleas, wood lice and barnacles belong to the group 'Crustacea'. Crustaceans are a large group of arthropods, which includes such familiar animals as crabs, lobsters, crayfish, shrimp, krill, wood lice, and barnacles.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following organisms does not exist as a single free living cell?
explanation: Volvox does not exist as a single free-living cell. It is a genus of chlorophytes, a type of green algae. It forms spherical colonies of up to 50,000 cells. They live in a variety of freshwater habitats, and were first reported by Antonie van Leeuwenhoek in 1700.

topics.title: LIVING ORGANISMS : CELL STRUCTURE AND FUNCTIONS OF CELL COMPONENTS

subject: Biology
q: An association between the root nodule of a leguminous plant and Rhizobium sp. is known as
explanation: The association between the root nodule of a leguminous plant and Rhizobium sp. is known as 'Symbiosis'. Symbiosis is a close and long-term biological interaction between two different biological organisms. In this case, Rhizobium bacteria in the soil infect the roots of leguminous plants, leading to the formation of root nodules where the bacteria fix atmospheric nitrogen into a form that the plant can use.

topics.title: ECOLOGY : SYMBIOTIC INTERACTIONS OF PLANTS AND ANIMALS

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following groups is the most advanced?
explanation: Gymnosperms are the most advanced among the given options. The order of advancement in plant groups is Thallophytes < Bryophytes < Pteridophytes < Gymnosperms. Gymnosperms are seed-producing plants, and their seeds are not enclosed within an ovary, unlike angiosperms. They are more advanced than the other options because they have a well-differentiated plant body and vascular tissues for the transport of water, minerals, and food.

subject: Biology
q: The mode of feeding in Amoeba and Hydra is
explanation: The mode of feeding in both Amoeba and Hydra is heterotrophic. Heterotrophic organisms are those that cannot synthesize their own food and depend on other organisms for their nutrition. Amoeba and Hydra both consume their food by engulfing it, a process known as phagocytosis.

subject: Biology
q: Viviparity occurs mainly in the
explanation: Viviparity, the mode of reproduction in which the embryo develops inside the body of the mother, occurs mainly in mammals. In this mode of reproduction, the young ones are born alive and not from eggs. This is a characteristic feature of mammals, although there are exceptions in other groups.

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer the question that follows.


In ferns, the structure above is equivalent to the
explanation: In mosses, the gametophyte generation is the dominant, photosynthetic phase. It begins with the protonema, a filamentous structure from which buds form. These buds develop into leafy shoots composed of stem-like caulidum, leaf-like phyllids, and rhizoids for anchorage. The gametophyte produces male (antheridia) and female (archegonia) reproductive organs at the shoot tips. Fertilization occurs when sperm swim to the archegonia, leading to the formation of a diploid sporophyte.

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer the question that follows.

The structures labeled II and III respectively are
explanation: The structures labeled II and III respectively are Male organs and rhizoid.

subject: Biology
q: Most monocots are easily recognized by their
explanation: Monocots, short for monocotyledons, are a type of flowering plant that typically have long, narrow, and sword-like leaves. This is a characteristic feature of many monocots, including grasses and grains. The leaves are usually parallel-veined, another distinguishing feature of monocots.

topics.title: INTERNAL STRUCTURE OF A FLOWERING PLANT : LEAF

subject: Biology
q: Amphibians are normally found
explanation: Amphibians are a group of cold-blooded vertebrates that includes frogs, toads, newts, and salamanders. They are adapted to live both in water and on moist land. They typically start their life cycle in water with gills and tails, like fish, and then undergo metamorphosis, developing lungs and legs for living on land. However, they still need a moist environment to survive, as their skin must stay wet.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : STRUCTURAL ADAPTATIONS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: The jointed structure in insects that bears organs which are sensitive to touch, smell and vibration is the
explanation: In insects, the antenna is a jointed structure that bears organs which are sensitive to touch, smell, and vibration. It is a sensory appendage that is attached to the head of adult insects. Antennae are used for the sense of smell and balance. They are jointed, and the number of segments can vary between species.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : STRUCTURAL ADAPTATIONS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: The veins of the leaf are formed by the
explanation: The veins of the leaf are formed by the vascular bundles. Vascular bundles are a part of the transport system in plants. They are strands of tissue that run the length of the plant, from the roots, through the stem and out into the leaves. They are responsible for the transport of water, minerals, and food throughout the plant.

topics.title: INTERNAL STRUCTURE OF A FLOWERING PLANT : LEAF

subject: Biology
q: When specimen X is mixed with few drops of iodine solution, the appearance of a blue-black colour confirms X is
explanation: When iodine solution is mixed with a specimen and a blue-black color appears, it confirms that the specimen contains starch. Iodine solution is used as a test for the presence of starch. Starch reacts with iodine to form a blue-black complex, hence the change in color.

subject: Biology
q: The enzyme invertase will hydrolyze sucrose to give
explanation: The enzyme invertase hydrolyzes sucrose to give glucose and fructose. Invertase is an enzyme that catalyzes the hydrolysis (breakdown with the addition of a water molecule) of sucrose, a disaccharide, into the monosaccharides glucose and fructose.

subject: Biology
q: Salts and water are absorbed in the roots and transported to the leaves by
explanation: Salts and water are absorbed in the roots and transported to the leaves by osmosis through the xylem tissues. Xylem is a type of tissue in vascular plants that transports water and some nutrients from the roots to the leaves. Osmosis is the process by which molecules of a solvent tend to pass from a less concentrated solution into a more concentrated one, equalizing the concentrations on each side of a biological membrane.

subject: Biology
q: The transportation of oxygen and carbon(IV) oxide in mammals is carried out by
explanation: The transportation of oxygen and carbon(IV) oxide in mammals is carried out by erythrocytes. Erythrocytes, also known as red blood cells, are the most common type of blood cell and the vertebrate's principal means of delivering oxygen to the body tissues via the blood flow through the circulatory system.

subject: Biology
q: The gas produced during tissues respiration can be identified by using
explanation: The gas produced during tissues respiration can be identified by using calcium hydroxide. Calcium hydroxide, also known as limewater, is used to test for the presence of carbon dioxide. In the presence of carbon dioxide, limewater turns milky or cloudy, which is a positive test.

subject: Biology
q: When yeast respires anaerobically it converts simple sugars to Carbon (IV) Oxide and
explanation: During anaerobic respiration, yeast converts simple sugars into carbon dioxide and alcohol. This process is known as fermentation. It is a type of anaerobic respiration, which occurs in the absence of oxygen. The alcohol produced is ethanol.

subject: Biology
q: Urea formation occurs in the
explanation: Urea formation occurs in the liver. This process is part of the urea cycle, where ammonia, a toxic substance, is converted into urea, a less toxic substance. The urea is then transported to the kidneys where it is excreted in urine.

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer the question that follows.


The structure above represents a
explanation: The structure above represents a cervical vertebra. It has a short neural spine.

topics.title: SUPPORT AND MOVEMENT : TYPES AND FUNCTIONS OF THE SKELETON

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer the question that follows.

Blood vessels usually pass through the structure labelled
explanation: Is vertebrarterial canal where blood vessels pass through.

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer the question that follows.


The part labeled IV is the
explanation: The Germinal disc is also known as the embryo and it is surrounded by the yolk.

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer the question that follows.

The structure that holds the yolk in position is labelled
explanation: The structure that holds the yolk in position is labelled III or Chalaza holds the yolk in position

subject: Biology
q: A seedling grown in the dark is likely to be
explanation: A seedling grown in the dark is likely to be etiolated. Etiolation is a process in flowering plants grown in partial or complete absence of light, which causes them to become pale, elongated, and weak due to rapid cell growth and elongation.

topics.title: GROWTH : GERMINATION OF SEEDS AND CONDITION NECESSARY FOR GERMINATION OF SEEDS

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer the question that follows.


The part labelled II is the
explanation: The Ear ossicles is located in the middle ear and they include malleus (hammer), stapes (stirrup) and incus (anvil).

topics.title: COORDINATION AND CONTROL : THE SENSE ORGANS

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer the question that follows.

The parts which function together to bring about hearing are labelled
explanation: The external auditory meatus, the hammer, anvil, stirrup and the cochlea aids in hearing.

topics.title: COORDINATION AND CONTROL : THE SENSE ORGANS

subject: Biology
q: The center for learning and memory in the human brain is the
explanation: The cerebrum is the largest part of the brain and is associated with higher brain function such as thought and action. It is divided into four sections, known as 'lobes': the frontal lobe, parietal lobe, occipital lobe, and temporal lobe. The temporal lobe is primarily involved in processing and interpreting sounds and also plays a key role in memory formation.

subject: Biology
q: The physical space occupied by an organism together with its functional role in the community can be described as
explanation: In ecology, a niche is the match of a species to a specific environmental condition. It describes how an organism or population responds to the distribution of resources and competitors (for example, by growing when resources are abundant, and when predators, parasites and pathogens are scarce) and how it in turn alters those same factors (for example, limiting access to resources by other organisms, acting as a food source for predators and a consumer of prey).

topics.title: ECOLOGY : FACTORS AFFECTING THE DISTRIBUTION OF ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: The hygrometer is used for measuring
explanation: A hygrometer is an instrument used to measure the amount of humidity and water vapor in the atmosphere, in soil, or in confined spaces. Humidity measurement instruments usually rely on measurements of some other quantity such as temperature, pressure, mass, a mechanical or electrical change in a substance as moisture is absorbed.

subject: Biology
q: The highest percentage of energy in an ecosystem occurs at level of the
explanation: In an ecosystem, the highest percentage of energy is found at the level of the producers. This is because producers, such as plants, are able to convert sunlight into usable energy through photosynthesis. This energy is then passed up the food chain to consumers and decomposers, but with each transfer, a significant amount of energy is lost, usually as heat. Therefore, the amount of energy decreases as you move up the food chain from producers to primary consumers, secondary consumers, and decomposers.

topics.title: ECOLOGY : ENERGY FLOW IN THE ECOSYSTEM

subject: Biology
q: Carbon (IV) oxide content of the atmosphere is least affected by
explanation: The Carbon (IV) oxide content of the atmosphere is least affected by plant and animal respiration. This is because the process of respiration in plants and animals is part of the natural carbon cycle. During respiration, Carbon (IV) oxide is released into the atmosphere, but it is also absorbed by plants during photosynthesis. Therefore, the net effect on the Carbon (IV) oxide content of the atmosphere is minimal. On the other hand, human activities such as burning of fossil fuels and deforestation significantly increase the Carbon (IV) oxide content of the atmosphere.

topics.title: HUMANS AND ENVIRONMENT : POLLUTION AND ITS CONTROL

subject: Biology
q: A freshwater pond may contain
options: pond skater, water lily, shark and mosquito larva
water lily, fish, water scorpion and dragonfly larva
tad pole, water boatman, leeches and crab
water beetle, shrimps, water snail and water bug
explanation: A freshwater pond may contain water lily, fish, water scorpion, and dragonfly larva. These are all organisms that are adapted to live in freshwater environments. Sharks and crabs are marine animals and are not typically found in freshwater environments. Similarly, shrimps are usually found in saltwater environments. Pond skaters, leeches, water beetles, water snails, and water bugs can also be found in freshwater environments, but the combination of organisms listed in option 2 is the most likely to be found together in a freshwater pond.

topics.title: NATURAL HABITAT : AQUATIC TERRESTRIAL AND ARBOREAL

subject: Biology
q: The epiphytic habitat can best be described as
explanation: Epiphytic habitat refers to the environment in which epiphytes live. Epiphytes are plants that grow on other plants, usually trees, without harming them. They are not parasitic but rely on their hosts for physical support. This type of habitat is best described as arboreal, which means 'pertaining to trees'.

topics.title: NATURAL HABITAT : AQUATIC TERRESTRIAL AND ARBOREAL

subject: Biology
q: The distribution of plants in a rain forest is governed mainly by
explanation: The distribution of plants in a rain forest is governed mainly by the rainfall pattern. In both temperate and tropical regions, the amount and distribution of rainfall, rather than temperature, determine the vegetation i.e whether it is a forest, a grassland or a desert.

topics.title: ECOLOGY : FACTORS AFFECTING THE DISTRIBUTION OF ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q:
The number of plant species obtained from a population study of a garden is as follows;
Guinea grass (15), Ipomea spp. (5) Sida spp(7) and Imperata spp. (23). What is the percentage of occurrence of Imperata spp.?
explanation: The percentage of occurrence of a species in a population can be calculated by dividing the number of individuals of that species by the total number of individuals in the population, and then multiplying the result by 100. In this case, the total number of plants is 15 (Guinea grass) + 5 (Ipomea spp.) + 7 (Sida spp.) + 23 (Imperata spp.) = 50. Therefore, the percentage of occurrence of Imperata spp. is (23/50) * 100 = 46%.

topics.title: THE ECOLOGY OF POPULATIONS : FACTORS AFFECTING POPULATION SIZES

subject: Biology
q: The greatest influence on a stable ecosystem in nature is exhibited by
explanation: The greatest influence on a stable ecosystem in nature is exhibited by man. This is because human activities such as deforestation, pollution, urbanization, etc., have a significant impact on the ecosystem. They can alter the balance of the ecosystem, leading to the extinction of certain species and the proliferation of others.

topics.title: ECOLOGY : FACTORS AFFECTING THE DISTRIBUTION OF ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: The factor that least affects food shortage in sub-saharan Africa is
explanation: The factor that least affects food shortage in sub-saharan Africa is mixed-cropping. Mixed cropping is a method of farming that involves planting two or more plants simultaneously in the same field, which can actually increase food production and contribute to food security. On the other hand, drought, flooding, and pests can significantly reduce food production.

topics.title: HUMANS AND ENVIRONMENT : CONSERVATION OF NATURAL RESOURCES

subject: Biology
q: In man, the ability to roll the tongue is a variation classified as
explanation: In man, the ability to roll the tongue is a variation classified as physiological. Physiological variations are differences in the function of an organism or the ability to perform a particular function. The ability to roll the tongue is a functional variation, not a structural, morphological, or anatomical one.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following statements is true of blood groups and blood transfusion?
options: Group A can donate to group A only
Group B can donate to group B only
explanation: In the context of blood transfusion, Group AB is considered the universal recipient because it can receive blood from donors of any ABO blood group (A, B, AB, or O). This is due to the fact that AB blood types do not have any antibodies against A or B antigens, thus they can receive blood from any group without causing an immune response.

topics.title: HEREDITY : INHERITANCE OF CHARACTERS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: A tall plant crossed with a dwarf one produces offspring of which half are tall and half are dwarf. What are the genotypes of the parents?
explanation: Hence we have : 2 Heterzygous tall and homozygous short.

topics.title: HEREDITY : PROBABILITY IN GENETICS AND SEX DETERMINATION

subject: Biology
q: Both recessive and dominant characters are found
options: at the same locus of a homologous chromosome
on the same chromatid in a chromosome
explanation: Both recessive and dominant characters are found at the same locus of a homologous chromosome. A locus is a specific location or position on a chromosome where a given gene is located. In a pair of homologous chromosomes, one from each parent, the same locus is the position for the same gene. This gene can be either dominant or recessive.

topics.title: HEREDITY : INHERITANCE OF CHARACTERS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following is likely to encourage inbreeding in plants
explanation: Hermaphrodite plants, which contain both male and female reproductive organs, are more likely to encourage inbreeding as they can self-fertilize.

subject: Biology
q: The probability of a baby being a boy or a girl depends on the contribution of the
explanation: The sex of a baby is determined by the sex chromosome contributed by the father's sex cell (sperm). If the sperm carries an X chromosome, the baby will be female (XX). If the sperm carries a Y chromosome, the baby will be male (XY).

topics.title: HEREDITY : PROBABILITY IN GENETICS AND SEX DETERMINATION

subject: Biology
q: The ability of an organism to live successfully in an environment is known as
explanation: Adaptation is the ability of an organism to change over time in response to the environment. This can be a physical change in the organism's body or a change in the organism's behavior. This ability helps the organism to survive and reproduce in its environment.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : ADAPTIVE COLOURATION AND ITS FUNCTIONS

subject: Biology
q: For heterotrophic organisms, competition is least caused by inadequacy of
explanation: Heterotrophic organisms are those that cannot manufacture their own food and need to obtain it from other organisms. This includes animals and fungi. They compete for resources such as nutrients, space, and mates. However, light is primarily a concern for autotrophic organisms (like plants) that use light for photosynthesis, not heterotrophs. Therefore, competition among heterotrophs is least caused by inadequacy of light.

subject: Biology
q: The most important adaptation of xerophytes is the ability of the protoplasm to
options: store sugar and minerals in the vacuoles
explanation: Xerophytes are plants that are adapted to survive in dry environments with little water. The most important adaptation of xerophytes is their ability to resist being damaged by water loss. This is achieved through various physiological and structural adaptations such as having a thick cuticle, reduced leaf area, and the presence of sunken stomata.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : STRUCTURAL ADAPTATIONS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: The stem of a typical aquatic plant usually has many
explanation: Aquatic plants, also known as hydrophytes, have adaptations that enable them to survive in water or in soil that is periodically underwater. One of these adaptations is the presence of air cavities, also known as aerenchyma, in their stems. These air cavities allow for the transportation of gases between the plant parts that are above water and those that are submerged.

topics.title: INTERNAL STRUCTURE OF A FLOWERING PLANT : STEM

subject: Biology
q: A green snake in green grass is able to escape notice from predators because of its
explanation: A green snake in green grass is able to escape notice from predators because of its cryptic colouration. Cryptic colouration, also known as camouflage, is a defensive adaptation that allows an organism to blend in with its surroundings. In this case, the green colour of the snake matches the green colour of the grass, making it difficult for predators to spot.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : ADAPTIVE COLOURATION AND ITS FUNCTIONS

subject: Biology
q: The role of the male adult honey bee is to
explanation: The primary role of the male adult honey bee, also known as a drone, is to mate with the queen. They do not have the ability to sting, and they do not participate in nectar and pollen gathering. A drone's primary role is to mate with a virgin queen. He has a barbed sex organ, which leads to his death after mating.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : BEHAVIOURAL ADAPTATIONS IN SOCIAL ANIMALS

subject: Biology
q: Darwin is considered the first scientist who correctly explained the theory of
explanation: Charles Darwin is most celebrated for his theory of evolution by natural selection, which explains how populations of organisms change over time through the process of descent with modification. This theory specifically focuses on organic or biological evolution, as opposed to other concepts like use and disuse, special creation, or spontaneous generation.

subject: Biology
q: An amoeba and an unlaid chicken egg are
explanation: An amoeba and an unlaid chicken egg are both single cells. An amoeba is a single-celled organism that moves by changing shape. It is one of the simplest creatures that exists. An egg, before it is laid, is also a single cell. It is the largest single cell that can be seen without the aid of a microscope.

subject: Biology
q: In corms, food is usually stored in the
explanation: In corms, food is usually stored in the stems. Corms are short, vertical, swollen underground plant stems that serve as storage organs used by some plants to survive winter or other adverse conditions such as summer drought and heat.

topics.title: INTERNAL STRUCTURE OF A FLOWERING PLANT : STEM

subject: Biology
q: The animals that move by means of Flagella include
explanation: Flagella are whip-like structures that allow cells to move. They are found in both prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells. Chlamydomonas and Euglena are examples of organisms that use flagella for movement.

subject: Biology
q: The structures found only in plant cell are
explanation: The cell wall and chloroplast are structures found only in plant cells. The cell wall provides structural support and protection, while the chloroplast is involved in photosynthesis, the process by which plants convert light energy into chemical energy.

topics.title: LIVING ORGANISMS : CELL STRUCTURE AND FUNCTIONS OF CELL COMPONENTS

subject: Biology
q: A flower that has both stamens and pistil is said to be
explanation: A flower that has both stamens (male reproductive organs) and pistils (female reproductive organs) is said to be perfect. This means it has the ability to self-pollinate, although cross-pollination may still occur.

subject: Biology
q: When oil is poured into the breeding site of mosquitoes, it
explanation: When oil is poured into the breeding site of mosquitoes, it suffocates the pupae. This is because the oil forms a thin layer on the surface of the water, preventing oxygen from reaching the mosquito larvae and pupae that breathe through spiracles located on their abdomens. This lack of oxygen suffocates the pupae.

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer this question.


The correct evolutionary sequence of the organisms represented is
explanation: The correct evolutionary sequence of the organisms represented is IV → II → III → I.

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer this question.

Ovoviparity is the type of fertilization exhibited by the organism labelled
explanation: Ovoviparity is exhibited by toads , there is no placenta connection and the young are nourished by egg yolk.

subject: Biology
q: In the diagram, the hawk is
explanation: A tertiary consumer is a carnivore at the topmost level in a food chain that feeds on other carnivores.

topics.title: ECOLOGY : FOOD CHAINS FOOD WEBS AND TROPHIC LEVELS

subject: Biology
q: Examples of competitors are
explanation: Grasshopper and mouse competitors because they both feed on vegetation in the food web.

subject: Biology
q: One of the adaptations to life on trees by a monkey is its possession of digits which are
explanation: One of the adaptations to life on trees by a monkey is its possession of opposable digits. Opposable digits, usually thumbs, allow monkeys to grasp and manipulate objects, including tree branches, effectively. This is a crucial adaptation for life in the trees, as it enables them to move around efficiently and securely.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : STRUCTURAL ADAPTATIONS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: The community of plants in which the same species occur from year to year is the
explanation: The community of plants in which the same species occur from year to year is the climax vegetation. Climax vegetation is the final stage of succession, remaining relatively unchanged until destroyed by an event such as fire or human interference. It is a stable community that is in equilibrium with the environment.

topics.title: THE ECOLOGY OF POPULATIONS : ECOLOGICAL SUCCESSION

subject: Biology
q: The most common means of transmitting the Acquired Immune Deficiency Syndrome (AIDS) is
explanation: The most common means of transmitting the Acquired Immune Deficiency Syndrome (AIDS) is through sexual intercourse. AIDS is primarily transmitted through unprotected sexual intercourse with an infected person. It can also be transmitted through sharing of needles, blood transfusion, and from an infected mother to her child during childbirth or breastfeeding, but these are less common.

subject: Biology
q: Plants tend to prevent overcrowding by means of efficient
explanation: Plants tend to prevent overcrowding by means of efficient seed dispersal. Seed dispersal is the movement or transport of seeds away from the parent plant. Plants have very limited mobility and consequently rely upon a variety of dispersal vectors to transport their propagules, including both abiotic vectors such as the wind and living (biotic) vectors like birds. Seed dispersal is likely to have several benefits for plant species. First, seed survival is often higher away from the parent plant. This higher survival may result from the actions of density-dependent mortality agents, which often kill more seeds at high densities.

topics.title: THE ECOLOGY OF POPULATIONS : POPULATION DENSITY AND OVERCROWDING

subject: Biology
q: The physical and climatic factors of a region primarily determine the
options: types of plants found in the area
nature of the soil in that region
explanation: The physical and climatic factors of a region primarily determine the community of organisms in the region. The physical factors like temperature, humidity, rainfall and soil type in a region determine what types of plants and animals can survive there. These organisms together form a community. For example, cacti, snakes and scorpions are found in deserts due to the hot and dry climate, while polar bears and seals are found in polar regions due to the cold climate.

topics.title: ECOLOGY : FACTORS AFFECTING THE DISTRIBUTION OF ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: A step in the movement of energy through an ecosystem is a description that best fits the term
explanation: A trophic level refers to a step in the movement of energy through an ecosystem. Energy moves up the food chain by passing through different trophic levels. For example, plants take up energy from the sun and are eaten by herbivores, which are then eaten by carnivores. Each of these stages represents a different trophic level.

topics.title: ECOLOGY : ENERGY FLOW IN THE ECOSYSTEM

subject: Biology
q: In a field experiment, the frequency of water leaf was observed to be 48 after 20 tosses of a 2m2 quadrant. What is the density of the plant in the field?
explanation: The density of a population in ecology is calculated as the number of individuals per unit area or volume. In this case, the frequency of water leaf was observed to be 48 after 20 tosses of a 2m2 quadrant. Therefore, the density of the plant in the field is 48/40 = 1.2 per m2.

topics.title: THE ECOLOGY OF POPULATIONS : POPULATION DENSITY AND OVERCROWDING

subject: Biology
q: The supporting tissue of xylem is most poorly developed in
explanation: The supporting tissue of xylem is most poorly developed in submerged water plants. This is because these plants live in water and therefore do not need to transport water from the roots to other parts of the plant as land plants do. The xylem, which is responsible for this transportation, is thus not as developed.

topics.title: INTERNAL STRUCTURE OF A FLOWERING PLANT : STEM

subject: Biology
q: The addition of lime to clayey soil serves to
explanation: The addition of lime to clayey soil serves to prevent water-logging. Clayey soil has small, tightly packed particles that can hold water, but this can also lead to water-logging. Lime helps to break up these particles, improving drainage and preventing water-logging.

subject: Biology
q: The excessive use of agro-chemicals could lead to the pollution of
explanation: The excessive use of agro-chemicals could lead to the pollution of fresh water. Agro-chemicals, such as fertilizers and pesticides, can run off from agricultural land into rivers and lakes, causing water pollution. This can harm aquatic life and make the water unsafe for human consumption.

topics.title: HUMANS AND ENVIRONMENT : POLLUTION AND ITS CONTROL

subject: Biology
q: In a population study using the transect method, a student is likely to record the highest number of species in
explanation: In a population study using the transect method, a student is likely to record the highest number of species in a guinea savanna.

topics.title: ECOLOGY : FACTORS AFFECTING THE DISTRIBUTION OF ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: In his theory of evolution, Darwin implied that
options: the struggle for existence among living organisms is sporadic
the most successful organisms are those that best adapt to their environment
organs of the body which are not regularly used by an organism will disappear
any traits acquired by an organism during its lifetime can be passed on to its offspring
explanation: Darwin's theory of evolution, also known as Darwinism, implies that the most successful organisms are those that best adapt to their environment. This is the principle of natural selection, where individuals with traits that increase their probability of survival will have more opportunities to reproduce, and their offspring will also benefit from these advantageous traits.

subject: Biology
q: The carnassial teeth of a carnivorous animal consist of the
options: last upper premolar and the first lower molar
last upper molar and the last lower molar
first upper premolar and the first lower molar
first upper molar and the first lower molar
explanation: The carnassial teeth of a carnivorous animal consist of the last upper premolar and the first lower molar. Carnassial teeth are modified molars (and in the case of carnivorans, premolars) which are adapted in certain mammals to allow for the shearing (rather than tearing) of flesh to permit the more efficient consumption of meat.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : STRUCTURAL ADAPTATIONS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: A feature of the caste systems of bees and termites is that
options: the kings are bigger than the queens
nuptial flight is performed by all members
explanation: In the caste systems of bees and termites, the workers are typically sterile. This is a feature of eusociality, a high level of organization of animal sociality, where the workers forego their own reproduction to care for the offspring of others (usually the queen).

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : BEHAVIOURAL ADAPTATIONS IN SOCIAL ANIMALS

subject: Biology
q: The structure that is common in the embryos of mammals, amphibians, birds, fishes and reptiles and which is an evidence of their common ancestry is the
explanation: The gill-slits are a structure that is common in the embryos of mammals, amphibians, birds, fishes and reptiles. This is an evidence of their common ancestry. Gill-slits are the rudimentary structures that are present in the embryos of these animals and are indicative of their aquatic ancestry. They later develop into different structures in different animals as they evolve.

subject: Biology
q: Animals aestivate when the environment in which they live becomes
explanation: Animals aestivate when the environment in which they live becomes hot and dry. Aestivation, or estivation, is a state of animal dormancy, similar to hibernation, characterized by inactivity and a lowered metabolic rate, that is entered in response to high temperatures and arid conditions. It takes place during times of heat and dryness, the hot dry season, which are often the summer months.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : BEHAVIOURAL ADAPTATIONS IN SOCIAL ANIMALS

subject: Biology
q: Birds which are large with long straight pointed beaks, long necks and long legs are likely to be
explanation: Birds which are large with long straight pointed beaks, long necks and long legs are likely to be fish catchers. These physical characteristics are adaptations that help the bird to catch fish. The long straight pointed beak is used to spear the fish, the long neck helps in reaching into the water to catch the fish and the long legs are used for wading in the water.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : STRUCTURAL ADAPTATIONS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: Examples of organisms in which extracellular digestion occurs are
explanation: Extracellular digestion is the process where digestion takes place outside the cells in a digestive cavity. Fungi, such as Rhizopus, perform extracellular digestion by secreting enzymes into their environment. Houseflies also perform extracellular digestion by regurgitating digestive enzymes onto their food and then absorbing the nutrients. Hydra perform extracellular digestion by releasing enzymes into their gastrovascular cavity.

subject: Biology
q: The mammalian erythrocytes differ from the leucocytes at maturity because the erythrocytes are
explanation: Mammalian erythrocytes, also known as red blood cells, are discoid (disk-shaped) and enucleated (lack a nucleus at maturity). This is in contrast to leucocytes, or white blood cells, which are nucleated and can change shape.

topics.title: INTERNAL STRUCTURE OF A MAMMAL : MAMMALIAN INTERNAL ORGANS

subject: Biology
q: The presence of endoskeleton is characteristic of
explanation: Endoskeleton is an internal support structure of an animal, composed of mineralized tissue. Endoskeleton develops within the skin or in the deeper body tissues. The vertebrates (animals with a backbone, including mammals, birds, reptiles, amphibians, and fishes) possess an endoskeleton.

topics.title: STRUCTURAL/BEHAVIOURAL ADAPTATIONS OF VERTEBRATES : BONY FISH

subject: Biology
q: The capture and digestion of insects by a pitcher plant is a special form of nutrition termed ......
explanation: The capture and digestion of insects by a pitcher plant is a special form of nutrition termed heterotrophic. Heterotrophic nutrition is when an organism relies on organic substances for its nutrition because it cannot synthesize its own food. In this case, the pitcher plant, which is typically a photosynthetic plant, supplements its nutrition by capturing and digesting insects.

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram to answer this question.

Which of the following statements is correct about the experiment?
options: The flask must be of the conical type
Caustic soda can be replaced with distilled water
The enclosed leaf will lose its green colour
The leaves outside the flask serve as control
explanation: The leaves outside the flask serve as control is correct about the experiment

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram to answer this question.
The experimental set-up can be used to demonstrate that
options: photosynthesis occurs in the leaves of plants
explanation: The experimental set-up can be used to demonstrate that carbon(IV)oxide is necessary for photosynthesis.

subject: Biology
q: When a marine fish was taken from the ocean and put in a tank of fresh water, it died after a short period because
options: the tank was too small compared to the large ocean
the body cells of the fish swelled and burst as a result of the hypotonic fresh water
the body cells of the fish shrank as their sap was hypertonic to the fresh water
there was no food in the tank, so the fish starved.
explanation: When a marine fish is taken from the ocean and put in a tank of fresh water, it dies after a short period because the body cells of the fish swelled and burst as a result of the hypotonic fresh water. This is due to osmosis, a process where water moves from an area of lower solute concentration (the fresh water) to an area of higher solute concentration (the body cells of the fish). The cells swell and can burst due to the influx of water.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : ADAPTIVE COLOURATION AND ITS FUNCTIONS

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following pairs of organs is located in the anterior half of the mammalian body cavity?
explanation: The pair of organs located in the anterior half of the mammalian body cavity are the lungs and heart. The anterior half of the body cavity, also known as the thoracic cavity, houses the heart and lungs, among other structures.

topics.title: INTERNAL STRUCTURE OF A MAMMAL : MAMMALIAN INTERNAL ORGANS

subject: Biology
q: The mode of nutrition exhibited by a tapeworm is
explanation: A tapeworm is a type of parasite that lives in the intestines of its host. Parasitic nutrition is when an organism lives on or in its host and causes harm to it. The tapeworm absorbs nutrients directly from the host's gut, hence it exhibits parasitic nutrition.

subject: Biology
q: The organ located within the duodenal loop in the mammal is the
explanation: The pancreas is located within the loop of the duodenum. The duodenum is the first part of the small intestine, and the pancreas is a gland organ that is part of the digestive and endocrine systems. It releases digestive enzymes into the duodenum to help with digestion and also produces insulin and other hormones.

topics.title: INTERNAL STRUCTURE OF A MAMMAL : MAMMALIAN INTERNAL ORGANS

subject: Biology
q: In which of the following groups of fruits is the pericarp inseparable from the seed coat?
explanation: In a caryopsis, the pericarp (the part of a fruit formed from the ovary wall) is inseparable from the seed coat. This is typical of grains like corn, wheat, and rice. The other options, Nut, Follicle, and Cypsela, do not have this characteristic.

topics.title: INTERNAL STRUCTURE OF A FLOWERING PLANT : SEED

subject: Biology
q: A person that is obese must avoid meals containing
explanation: Obesity is a condition characterized by excessive body fat. It is often associated with a high intake of fats and sugars. Margarine and butter are high in fats and should be avoided by an obese person to prevent further weight gain. Carrots and oranges are fruits and vegetables that are low in fats and high in fiber, which is beneficial for weight loss. Beef and beans are sources of protein, which is essential for body functions and not necessarily linked to obesity. Rice and yam are carbohydrates, which provide energy for the body and are not directly linked to obesity.

subject: Biology
q: Tissue respiration is important for the
options: release of energy for the body use
explanation: Tissue respiration, also known as cellular respiration, is the process by which cells in the body convert glucose and oxygen into energy, carbon dioxide, and water. This energy is used to power various cellular processes. The absorption of oxygen into the alveoli and the release of carbon dioxide into the lungs are part of the respiratory process, not tissue respiration. The exhalation of carbon dioxide from the lungs is also part of the respiratory process, not tissue respiration.

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer this question.


The function of absorption is performed by the structure labelled
explanation: It's function is absorption of nutrients. The part labelled II is the root hair. Root hairs increase the root's surface area, aiding in water and nutrient absorption, anchoring the plant in soil, and facilitating symbiotic relationships with soil microbes.

topics.title: INTERNAL STRUCTURE OF A FLOWERING PLANT : ROOT

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer this question.

The structure labelled I represents the
explanation: The pericycle is a cylinder of parenchyma or sclerenchyma cells that lies just inside the endodermis and is the outer most part of the stele of plants. It strengthens the stem and provides protection for the vascular bundles.

topics.title: INTERNAL STRUCTURE OF A FLOWERING PLANT : ROOT

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer this question.


The gland usually found in the position labelled I is the
explanation: It is the major endocrine gland, a pea-sized body attached to the base of the brain that is important in controlling growth and development and the functioning of the other endocrine glands.

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer this question.

The hormone secreted at IV serves to
options: raise the level of calcium ions in the blood
explanation: IV is a sex hormone that facilitate the development of facial hairs.

subject: Biology
q: To select and retain the desirable trait of large body size which a farmer has observed in his herd, the farmer needs to
options: feed the animals in the herd with more food
cross-breed his animals with a different herd
explanation: The farmer needs to inbreed the animals in his herd. Inbreeding is the mating of organisms closely related by ancestry. This method is often used to retain desirable traits in a herd. By inbreeding, the farmer can increase the chances of the offspring inheriting the desirable trait of large body size.

topics.title: HEREDITY : APPLICATION OF THE PRINCIPLES OF HEREDITY

subject: Biology
q: In a population of living things, the parameters of size, height, weight and colour are examples of
explanation: Size, height, weight, and colour are examples of continuous variations. Continuous variations are those that have a range of phenotypes. They are usually quantitative and can be measured, and are controlled by many genes (polygenic), such as height, weight, and skin colour in humans.

subject: Biology
q: If XN is the dominant allele for normal vision and Xn the recessive allele for colour-blindness, a boy with the genotype YXn will
explanation: A boy with the genotype YXn will be colour-blind. In this case, XN is the dominant allele for normal vision and Xn is the recessive allele for colour-blindness. Since males have only one X chromosome, they will express the trait associated with the allele they inherit, regardless of whether it is dominant or recessive. In this case, the boy has inherited the Xn allele, so he will be colour-blind.

topics.title: HEREDITY : PROBABILITY IN GENETICS AND SEX DETERMINATION

subject: Biology
q: The first four children of a couple were all girls. The probability that the fifth will also be a girl is
explanation: The gender of a baby is determined by the sex chromosomes that it inherits from its parents. A baby girl is formed when two X chromosomes are inherited (one from the mother and one from the father), while a baby boy is formed when an X and a Y chromosome are inherited (X from the mother and Y from the father). Since the mother can only pass on an X chromosome, the gender of the baby is determined by the father. If the father passes on an X chromosome, the baby will be a girl, and if he passes on a Y chromosome, the baby will be a boy. Therefore, the probability that a baby will be a girl is always 1/2, regardless of the genders of the previous children.

topics.title: HEREDITY : PROBABILITY IN GENETICS AND SEX DETERMINATION

subject: Biology
q: Genetic counseling is important when a marriage is planned between a
options: Rh- woman and Rh+ man
Rh- woman and Rh- man
Rh+ woman and Rh+ man
Rh+ woman and Rh - man
explanation: Rh factor is a protein found on red blood cells. People who have the Rh factor are Rh positive (Rh+) and those who do not are Rh negative (Rh-). Problems can occur during pregnancy if the mother is Rh- and the father is Rh+, because the baby could inherit the Rh+ factor from the father. This can lead to Rh disease, a condition where the mother's immune system attacks the baby's red blood cells. Genetic counseling is important in such cases to understand the risks and manage the pregnancy appropriately.

topics.title: HEREDITY : APPLICATION OF THE PRINCIPLES OF HEREDITY

subject: Biology
q: What proportion of the offspring of a cross between two heterozygous parents will exhibit the recessive condition phenotypically?
explanation:
1 HH - Homozygous (Dominant)
2 Hh - Heterozygous
1 hh - Homozygous (recessive)
Total = 4
pr(Recessive)
=1
4

In a cross between two heterozygous parents (Aa x Aa), the Punnett square shows that 1/4 of the offspring will be homozygous dominant (AA), 1/2 will be heterozygous (Aa), and 1/4 will be homozygous recessive (aa). The homozygous recessive condition is the one that exhibits the recessive phenotype, so the proportion of offspring that will exhibit the recessive condition phenotypically is 1/4.

topics.title: HEREDITY : INHERITANCE OF CHARACTERS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: In the egg of a bird, the embryo is located in the
explanation: In the egg of a bird, the embryo is located in the embryo disc. The embryo disc, also known as the blastodisc, is the fertilized portion of the egg where the embryo begins to form. The chalaza anchors the yolk in the center of the egg, the yolk provides nutrients to the developing embryo, and the albumen provides additional nutrients and water.

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer this question.


The structures that are common to both plant and animal cells are labelled
explanation: Animal cells and plant cells have features in common such as a nucleus, cytoplasm, cell membrane, mitochondria and ribosomes. Plant and algal cells also have a cell wall, and often have chloroplasts and a permanent vacuole.

topics.title: LIVING ORGANISMS : CELL STRUCTURE AND FUNCTIONS OF CELL COMPONENTS

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer this question.

Food is stored in the structure labelled
explanation: The structure labelle IV is the pyrenoid. Pyrenoid are dense storage structures present in the chloroplast of green algae, They store starch.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : STRUCTURAL ADAPTATIONS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: One distinctive feature in the life history of liverworts is that they exhibit
explanation: Liverworts exhibit alternation of generations. This is a type of life cycle found in plants and algae, where they alternate between two generations: a sexual phase or generation (the gametophyte) and an asexual phase or generation (the sporophyte). Vegetative, asexual, and sexual reproduction are all methods of reproduction, but they are not distinctive features of liverworts.

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer this question.


A noticeable adaptation of the animal to its aquatic habitat is the possession of
explanation: It is the possession of webbed digits, used to aid movement in water.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : ADAPTIVE COLOURATION AND ITS FUNCTIONS

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer this question.


The processes of water loss and intake indicated by the arrows labelled I and II are respectively
explanation: Frogs lose water through the skin by evaporation and they absorb water by osmosis.

subject: Biology
q: The umbrella-shaped fruiting body of a fully developed mushroom is the
explanation: The umbrella-shaped fruiting body of a fully developed mushroom is the pileus. The pileus is the technical term for the cap, or top, of a mushroom. The mycelium is the vegetative part of a fungus, consisting of a network of fine white filaments. The basidium is the microscopic, spore-producing structure found on the underside of the mushroom cap, and the stipe is the stalk that supports the cap.

subject: Biology
q: The similarity among organisms belonging to the same group will be least within each
explanation: The similarity among organisms belonging to the same group will be least within each kingdom. The reason is that a kingdom is the highest and broadest classification level in the biological taxonomy hierarchy. It includes a vast variety of organisms that share only a few similarities. For example, the animal kingdom includes organisms as diverse as sponges, worms, insects, fish, and mammals.

subject: Biology
q: Hermaphroditic reproduction can be found among the
explanation: Hermaphroditic reproduction, where an individual organism possesses both male and female reproductive organs, can be found among the annelids and molluscs. Annelids, like earthworms, and many molluscs, like snails, are known to be hermaphrodites.

subject: Biology
q: An insect whose economic importance is both harmful and beneficial is the
explanation:
The insect whose economic importance is both harmful and beneficial is:
Tsetsefly

Tsetse flies are known for transmitting diseases such as sleeping sickness in humans and nagana in livestock, which are harmful. However, they also play a role in pollination and nutrient cycling in certain ecosystems, which can be beneficial.

Note:
Why the other options are not the most appropriate:
Blackfly: Blackflies can be harmful as they are vectors of diseases such as river blindness (onchocerciasis) in humans and livestock. They are generally not considered beneficial in economic terms.
Mosquito: Mosquitoes are primarily seen as harmful due to their role as vectors of diseases like malaria, dengue fever, and Zika virus. While they are also prey for some species and play a role in aquatic ecosystems, their negative impacts on human health outweigh their benefits.
Butterfly: Butterflies, as mentioned earlier, are primarily beneficial due to their role in pollination and ecosystem health. They do not cause significant economic harm compared to their ecological benefits.

Therefore, while all these insects may have dual roles in ecosystems (beneficial and harmful), the tsetsefly is specifically noted for its significant dual economic impact-both as a disease vector and as a pollinator in certain ecosystems.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : BEHAVIOURAL ADAPTATIONS IN SOCIAL ANIMALS

subject: Biology
q: The cell component that is present in a prokaryotic cell is the
explanation: Prokaryotic cells, such as bacteria, do not have a nucleus or other membrane-bound organelles. Instead, the genetic material in prokaryotic cells is contained in a central region of the cell called the nucleoid. However, they do contain ribosomes, which are not bound by a membrane. Ribosomes are responsible for protein synthesis in all cells, including prokaryotic cells.

topics.title: LIVING ORGANISMS : CELL STRUCTURE AND FUNCTIONS OF CELL COMPONENTS

subject: Biology
q: The stunted growth of a leguminous plant suffering from nitrogen deficiency may be corrected by inoculating the soil with
explanation: Rhizobium is a type of bacteria that forms symbiotic relationships with leguminous plants. These bacteria are able to fix atmospheric nitrogen into a form that the plant can use for growth. When a leguminous plant is suffering from nitrogen deficiency, inoculating the soil with Rhizobium can help to correct the deficiency and promote the plant's growth.

subject: Biology
q: If water that has been coloured red is poured at the base of a wilting plant, it will appear as a red stain in the cell of the
explanation: The xylem is a type of tissue in plants that transports water and dissolved nutrients from the roots to the rest of the plant. It is part of the plant's vascular system. If water that has been coloured red is poured at the base of a wilting plant, it will be absorbed by the roots and transported up through the xylem to the rest of the plant. Therefore, it will appear as a red stain in the cells of the xylem.

subject: Biology

the part played by the pleural cavity and diaphragm in respiration
that a large amount of oxygen is absorbed by the lungs
the presence of carbon(IV)oxide in exhaled air
explanation: It is to show that in mammals gaseous exchange takes place in the lungs.

subject: Biology
q: The dark reaction of photosynthesis involves the
options: reduction of carbon (IV) oxide to organic compounds
release of oxygen and the splitting of water
photolysis of water and the production of starch
explanation: The dark reaction of photosynthesis, also known as the Calvin cycle, involves the reduction of carbon dioxide (CO2) to produce organic compounds, specifically glucose. This process does not directly depend on light, but relies on the ATP and NADPH synthesized during the light-dependent reactions of photosynthesis.

subject: Biology

explanation: Transpiration is defined as the removal of excess water from plants to the atmosphere in the form of water vapour. The first jar has not water drops at the underside. This is because there is are no leaves. Hence, no transpiration. But the second jar has water drops on the underside. This is because of the presence of leaves which are responsible transpiration.

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer this question.


In the experiment, the layer of oil serves to prevent water loss by
explanation: In the experiment, the layer of oil serves to prevent water loss by evaporation

subject: Biology
q: A flower that has both stamens and pistil is said to be
explanation: A flower that has both stamens (male reproductive organ) and pistils (female reproductive organ) is said to be perfect. Staminate refers to a flower that only has stamens, while pistillate refers to a flower that only has pistils. Imperfect flowers are those that have either stamens or pistils, but not both.

topics.title: INTERNAL STRUCTURE OF A FLOWERING PLANT : LEAF

subject: Biology
q: The part of the mammalian digestive system where absorption of nutrients takes place is the
explanation: The ileum is the final part of the small intestine in mammals, and it is where most of the absorption of nutrients takes place. The colon is involved in the absorption of water and electrolytes, the oesophagus is a muscular tube that delivers food to the stomach, and the duodenum is the first part of the small intestine where the breakdown of food continues after it leaves the stomach.

subject: Biology
q: The best method of propagating sugarcane is by
explanation: Sugarcane is typically propagated by stem cuttings. This is because sugarcane seeds are not always viable or true to type. Stem cuttings, on the other hand, are pieces of the sugarcane stalk that are planted in the ground to grow a new plant. This method ensures that the new plant will have the same characteristics as the parent plant.

subject: Biology
q: In mammalian males, the excretory and reproductive systems share the
explanation: In mammalian males, the excretory and reproductive systems share the urethra. The urethra is a tube that carries urine from the bladder to the outside of the body, and in males, it also carries semen. The ureter and vas deferens are parts of the excretory and reproductive systems respectively, but they do not serve a shared function. The testes are solely part of the reproductive system.

subject: Biology
q: Metabolic production of urea is carried out in the
explanation: The metabolic production of urea is carried out in the liver. Urea is a waste product that is produced when the liver breaks down proteins or amino acids, and ammonia. The kidneys then filter the urea from the blood and excrete it in urine. The urinary bladder, pancreas, and malpighian tubule do not produce urea.

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer this question.


The structure can be found in the
explanation: nerve cells are found in the brain , spinal cord or nerve fibres

q:
