subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer the question that follows.


A function of the part labeled II is to
options: equalize pressure on both sides of the tympanum
help the animal maintain balance or posture
explanation:
Equalize pressure on both sides of the tympanum. The part labeled II is the eustachian tube.
The Eustachian tube in the ear functions to equalize air pressure between the middle ear and the atmosphere, facilitates drainage of mucus, allows ventilation for the middle ear, and protects against pathogens, thus maintaining ear health and function.

topics.title: COORDINATION AND CONTROL : THE SENSE ORGANS

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following is a homeostatic response in humans?
options: Withdrawing and hand from a hot object
The mouth getting watery when food is sighted
explanation: Homeostasis is the ability of an organism to maintain a stable, constant internal environment, despite changes in external conditions. Shivering in a cold environment is a homeostatic response in humans. When the body temperature drops, the muscles involuntarily contract and relax rapidly (shiver) to generate heat and restore the body temperature to its normal range.

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer the question that follows.



The optimal temperature for breeding cockroaches is
explanation: At 24°C, the quantity of cockroaches is maximal showing that the temperature is most suitable.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : BEHAVIOURAL ADAPTATIONS IN SOCIAL ANIMALS

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer the question that follows.


At which temperature will cockroaches not survive after 10days?
explanation: 15°C. Cockroaches, being ectothermic, thrive in warmer temperatures but struggle to survive prolonged exposure to temperatures around 15 degrees Celsius due to their cold sensitivity and reduced metabolic activity, which can lead to impaired functioning and potential death.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : BEHAVIOURAL ADAPTATIONS IN SOCIAL ANIMALS

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer the question that follows.



Evaporation and transpiration are respectively represented by the components labelled
explanation: Evaporation and transpiration are respectively represented by the components labelled I and II

topics.title: ECOLOGY : ENERGY FLOW IN THE ECOSYSTEM

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer the question that follows.


The main reservoir of water in the cycle is the
explanation: Ocean is the main reservoir of water in the water cycle.

topics.title: ECOLOGY : ENERGY FLOW IN THE ECOSYSTEM

subject: Biology
q: The Mambilla plateau is a unique Nigerian biome located in
explanation: The Mambilla Plateau is a unique Nigerian biome located in Taraba State. A biome is a large community of plants and animals that occupies a distinct region. The Mambilla Plateau, with its unique climate and vegetation, is one such distinct region in Nigeria.

topics.title: LOCAL(NIGERIAN) BIOMES : HIGHLANDS OF MONTANE FORESTS AND GRASSLANDS

subject: Biology
q: In Nigeria, the Guinea savanna belt borders the
explanation: In Nigeria, the Guinea savanna belt borders the rainforests and the Sudan savanna. The Guinea savanna is a type of grassland that is found between the rainforest and the Sudan savanna. It is characterized by tall grasses and scattered trees.

subject: Biology
q:
The population density of Tridax in an abandoned square farmland of side 200m
was found to be 5plants per m2. The population size of the plant on the farm is
explanation:
L = 200m
Population density = Population ÷ Area = 5 plants m-2
A = L2 = 2002 = 40,000
Population = 5 × 40,000 = 200,000.

topics.title: THE ECOLOGY OF POPULATIONS : POPULATION DENSITY AND OVERCROWDING

subject: Biology
q: Secondary succession is much faster than primary succession because
explanation: Secondary succession is much faster than primary succession because soil is already present. Primary succession begins in lifeless areas where there is no soil. In contrast, secondary succession begins in areas where a community that previously existed has been removed; it is typified by smaller scale disturbances that do not eliminate all life and nutrients from the environment.

topics.title: THE ECOLOGY OF POPULATIONS : ECOLOGICAL SUCCESSION

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following is used to test for the presence of lime in a soil sample?
explanation: Lime in soil can be tested using Hydrochloric acid (HCl). When HCl is added to a soil sample containing lime, it reacts to produce carbon dioxide gas which can be observed as effervescence or bubbling. This is due to the reaction between the acid and the calcium carbonate (lime) in the soil.

topics.title: SOIL : CHARACTERISTICS OF DIFFERENT TYPES OF SOIL

subject: Biology
q: The importance of practicing crop rotation in agriculture is to
explanation: Crop rotation is the practice of growing different types of crops in the same area in sequenced seasons. It is done so that the soil of farms is not used for only one set of nutrients. It helps in reducing soil erosion and increases soil fertility and crop yield. Growing the same crop in the same place for many years in a row gradually depletes the soil of certain nutrients and crop rotation is a way to help maintain soil fertility.

subject: Biology
q: The recycling method of solid waste disposal is unsuitable for
explanation: The recycling method of solid waste disposal is unstable for organic matter. Recycling is unsuitable for organic matter because it decomposes, causing odors and attracting pests, and can contaminate recyclable materials. Organic waste is biodegradable and better suited for composting, which converts it into nutrient-rich compost. Recycling facilities are not equipped to handle organic matter, and improper disposal can increase methane emissions. Composting or anaerobic digestion are more appropriate and environmentally friendly methods for managing organic waste.

topics.title: HUMANS AND ENVIRONMENT : POLLUTION AND ITS CONTROL

subject: Biology
q: A non-renewable alternative source of energy is
explanation: A non-renewable energy source is one that cannot be replenished in a short period of time. Nuclear energy is a non-renewable energy source because it is derived from uranium, a material that is not replenished in nature. Wind generators, solar panels, and hydroelectric power are all renewable energy sources because they are derived from natural resources that are constantly replenished.

topics.title: HUMANS AND ENVIRONMENT : CONSERVATION OF NATURAL RESOURCES

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following is the best explanation for a child who is phenotypically short and born of two tall parents?
options: the father possesses a gene for shortness
the mother possesses a gene for shortness
explanation: In genetics, height is considered a polygenic trait, meaning it is controlled by more than one gene. If both parents are tall but carry a recessive gene for shortness, they can produce a short child. This is because the child could inherit the recessive gene from both parents, resulting in a short phenotype. Therefore, the best explanation for a child who is phenotypically short and born of two tall parents is that both parents possess genes for shortness.

topics.title: HEREDITY : INHERITANCE OF CHARACTERS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: A yellow maize is crossed with a white maize and the first filial generation produce yellow maize only. The white trait is said to exhibit
explanation: In genetics, when a trait is not expressed in the presence of another trait, it is said to be recessive. In the given question, when a yellow maize is crossed with a white maize and the first filial generation produces yellow maize only, it means that the yellow trait is dominant and the white trait is recessive. Therefore, the white trait is said to exhibit recessiveness.

topics.title: HEREDITY : INHERITANCE OF CHARACTERS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: The association in which one member benefits and the other is relatively unaffected by the interaction is termed
explanation: The association where one organism benefits and the other is neither harmed nor benefits is called commensalism. In this type of relationship, one organism benefits from the other without affecting it. This is different from symbiosis where both organisms benefit, parasitism where one organism benefits at the expense of the other, and mutualism where both organisms benefit but are dependent on each other.

topics.title: ECOLOGY : SYMBIOTIC INTERACTIONS OF PLANTS AND ANIMALS

subject: Biology
q: When a peacock displays its colourful feathers, it is
explanation: When a peacock displays its colourful feathers, it is courting a female. This is a type of behavior known as a courtship display, which is common in many species. The male peacock uses its colourful feathers to attract a mate. This is not a sign of readiness for a fight, nor is it a method of protecting itself or its mate from predators.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : BEHAVIOURAL ADAPTATIONS IN SOCIAL ANIMALS

subject: Biology
q: When an animal has a dark-coloured dorsal surface and a light-coloured ventral surface, this is an adaptation called
explanation: When an animal has a dark coloured dorsal surface and a light coloured ventral surface, this is an adaptation called counter shading.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : ADAPTIVE COLOURATION AND ITS FUNCTIONS

subject: Biology
q: The only caste in the termite colony whose members can feed themselves are the
explanation: In a termite colony, the workers are the ones responsible for gathering food and feeding the rest of the colony. The other castes, such as the reproductives (queen and king), soldiers, and nymphs, rely on the workers for nourishment. Therefore, the workers are the only caste that can feed themselves.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : BEHAVIOURAL ADAPTATIONS IN SOCIAL ANIMALS

subject: Biology
q:
An evidence of a common ancestry for fishes, amphibians, reptiles, birds and
mammals is the
options: possession of wings by birds and bats
presence of gill clefts in vertebrate embryos
possession of scales by fishes and reptiles
explanation: The presence of gill clefts in vertebrate embryos is a common feature among fishes, amphibians, reptiles, birds, and mammals. This suggests a common ancestry among these groups. Gill clefts are part of the pharyngeal arches, which are a series of outgrowths in the neck region of vertebrate embryos. In fishes, these develop into gills, while in terrestrial vertebrates, they give rise to various structures in the head and neck.

subject: Biology
q: Night-blindness results from a deficiency of
explanation: Night blindness, also known as nyctalopia, is a condition that results from a deficiency of vitamin A. This vitamin is essential for the synthesis of rhodopsin, a pigment in the retina that allows for low-light vision. Without sufficient vitamin A, the eyes cannot produce enough rhodopsin, leading to difficulty seeing in low light or at night.

subject: Biology
q: The product of excretion common to the mammalian kidney, lung and skin is
explanation: The product of excretion common to the mammalian kidney, lung, and skin is water. Excretion is the process by which metabolic waste and other non-useful materials are eliminated from an organism. In mammals, the kidney, lungs, and skin are involved in this process. The kidney excretes urea, salts, and water. The lungs excrete carbon dioxide and water. The skin excretes sweat which contains water, salts, and a small amount of urea.

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer this question.


Sexual reproduction is illustrated in
explanation: I. The part labeled I illustrate sexual reproduction in paramecium. Sexual reproduction in Paramecium involves two cells of different mating types coming together and forming a conjugation bridge. They exchange haploid micronuclei, which fuse within each cell to form diploid micronuclei. These undergo mitosis, leading to new macronuclei formation. After separation, each Paramecium cell possesses a new macronucleus, ensuring genetic diversity.

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer this question.

A plant that exhibits the type of reproduction shown in IV is
explanation: In Bryophyllum, reproduction takes place through the asexual mode called Vegetative Propagation where new plants are produced from vegetative parts, leaves, stems or roots. Buds are produced in the notches along the leaf margin of Bryophyllum.

subject: Biology
q: An increase in air pressure in the lungs is due to the
options: increase in the volume of the thoracic cavity
explanation: An increase in air pressure in the lungs is due to the relaxation of the diaphragm. The diaphragm is a dome-shaped muscle that separates the thoracic cavity from the abdominal cavity. When the diaphragm relaxes, it moves upward, reducing the volume of the thoracic cavity and increasing the pressure in the lungs, forcing air out.

topics.title: RESPIRATION : THE MECHANISM OF GASEOUS EXCHANGE IN PLANTS AND MAMMALS

subject: Biology
q: One basic similarity between nervous and endocrine system is that they both
explanation: Both the nervous and endocrine systems involve the use of chemical substances. The nervous system uses neurotransmitters to transmit signals between neurons, while the endocrine system uses hormones to regulate various bodily functions. The other options are incorrect because they do not accurately describe similarities between the two systems.

topics.title: COORDINATION AND CONTROL : HORMONAL CONTROL~~COORDINATION AND CONTROL : NERVOUS COORDINATION

subject: Biology
q: The organ situated in the pericardial cavity of a mammal is the
explanation: The pericardial cavity is the potential space formed between the two layers of serous pericardium around the heart. Therefore, the organ situated in the pericardial cavity of a mammal is the heart.

topics.title: INTERNAL STRUCTURE OF A MAMMAL : MAMMALIAN INTERNAL ORGANS

subject: Biology
q: A unique characteristic of the cervical vertebrae is the presence of
explanation: The cervical vertebrae are the seven spinal bones that are located in the neck between the base of the skull and the thoracic vertebrae. A unique characteristic of the cervical vertebrae is the presence of the vertebrarterial canal. This canal is also known as the transverse foramen, and it is the hole in each of the transverse processes that gives passage to the vertebral artery and vein and a plexus of sympathetic nerves.

topics.title: SUPPORT AND MOVEMENT : TYPES AND FUNCTIONS OF THE SKELETON

subject: Biology
q: In rabbits, the chamber of the heart that receives oxygenated blood from the lungs is the
explanation: In the heart of a rabbit, similar to other mammals, the left auricle (also known as the left atrium) is the chamber that receives oxygenated blood from the lungs. The blood is then passed to the left ventricle which pumps it to the rest of the body.

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer this question.


The part labelled IV is for
explanation: It is for protection or cushion.The part labeled IV is known as amnion . it is filled with amniotic fluid which serves as cushion and protects the developing embryo from shaking and jarring.

topics.title: LIVING ORGANISMS : CELL STRUCTURE AND FUNCTIONS OF CELL COMPONENTS

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer this question.

The organ through which the embryo receives food water and oxygen is labelled
explanation: II is placenta. It carries oxygen, food and water from the mother's blood to the embryo's blood. It also removes excretory waste from the embryo's blood and put it in the mother's blood.

subject: Biology
q: The formation of water in the tissue respiration results from the
explanation: The formation of water in tissue respiration results from the reduction of oxygen by hydrogen. This process is part of the electron transport chain in cellular respiration, where hydrogen ions (protons) and electrons are transferred to oxygen, the final electron acceptor, forming water.

subject: Biology
q: Water is necessary for a germinating seed because it
explanation: Water is necessary for a germinating seed because it activates the enzymes. Enzymes are biological catalysts that speed up chemical reactions in cells. In the case of germination, water activates enzymes that break down stored food reserves in the seed, providing energy and materials for growth.

topics.title: GROWTH : GERMINATION OF SEEDS AND CONDITION NECESSARY FOR GERMINATION OF SEEDS

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following produces both hormones and enzymes?
explanation: The pancreas produces both hormones and enzymes. It is an organ of the digestive system and endocrine system of vertebrates. In humans, the pancreas produces insulin and glucagon hormones which regulate blood sugar levels. It also produces digestive enzymes which break down food in the small intestine.

subject: Biology
q: Mammals are capable of producing hypertonic urine mainly because of reabsorption in the
explanation: Mammals are capable of producing hypertonic urine mainly due to the reabsorption in the Loop of Henle. The Loop of Henle is a part of the nephron in the kidney. It plays a crucial role in concentrating the urine by reabsorbing water and various ions from the urine. This process helps in maintaining the balance of water and salts in the body.

subject: Biology
q: The mammalian vein which starts with and ends in a capillary network is the
explanation: The hepatic portal vein is the mammalian vein which starts with and ends in a capillary network. It carries blood from the gastrointestinal tract and spleen to the liver. This is unique because most veins carry blood directly back to the heart, but the hepatic portal vein is different because it carries blood to the liver for processing and detoxification before it returns to the heart.

subject: Biology
q: In the transverse section of a dicot stem, the region lying between the endodermis and the vascular bundle is the
explanation: In the transverse section of a dicot stem, the region lying between the endodermis and the vascular bundle is the pericycle. The pericycle is a layer of cells that surrounds the vascular bundle. It is involved in the formation of lateral roots and contributes to the secondary growth of the plant.

topics.title: INTERNAL STRUCTURE OF A FLOWERING PLANT : STEM

subject: Biology
q: Hassan and Hussain are identical twins but Hassan grows taller and fatter than Hussain. This is probably because
options: Hassan inherits genes for tallness and fatness from the father
Hussain is endowed with genes for shortness and thinness
explanation: Identical twins share the same genetic material, so differences in their physical characteristics are likely due to environmental factors rather than genetic ones. This is because the environment can influence how genes are expressed. For example, nutrition and exercise can affect a person's height and weight.

topics.title: HEREDITY : APPLICATION OF THE PRINCIPLES OF HEREDITY

subject: Biology
q: The two normal types of sex chromosomes are
explanation: The two normal types of sex chromosomes are XX and XY. XX represents female and XY represents male in most mammals. This is a fundamental concept in genetics.

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer this question.


The diagram represents the structure of
explanation: DNA means deoxyribonucleic acid. DNA is a double-stranded molecule shaped like a twisted ladder, known as a double helix. This is depicted in the question diagram. It consists of two antiparallel strands made of alternating sugar (deoxyribose) and phosphate groups, forming the backbone. Specific bases (adenine, thymine, cytosine, guanine) pair across the strands: adenine with thymine (A-T) and cytosine with guanine (C-G), held together by hydrogen bonds. This structure encodes genetic information, crucial for the development, functioning, and reproduction of living organisms.

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer this question.

The part labelled I represents the
explanation:
It represents base pairs. Base pairs in DNA are complementary pairs of nucleotide bases that adhere to specific rules: adenine (A) pairs with thymine (T) via two hydrogen bonds, and cytosine (C) pairs with guanine (G) via three hydrogen bonds. These pairings form the rungs of the DNA double helix, providing stability and encoding genetic information essential for cellular functions and inheritance.
It represents base pairs.

subject: Biology
q: One advantage of variation in a species population is that individuals
explanation: Variation in a species population is advantageous because it allows individuals to better adapt to changes. This is because different traits can provide advantages in different environments. If the environment changes, the population is more likely to have individuals with traits that are advantageous in the new environment, increasing the likelihood of the species' survival.

topics.title: VARIATION IN POPULATION : APPLICATION OF VARIATION

subject: Biology
q: Mendel's second law of inheritance states that
explanation: Mendel's second law of inheritance, also known as the law of independent assortment, states that the alleles for different traits segregate, or assort, independently of one another during the formation of gametes. This means that the allele a gamete receives for one gene does not influence the allele received for another gene.

topics.title: HEREDITY : INHERITANCE OF CHARACTERS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer this question


The change in the length of the necks or the giraffes shown was brought about by
explanation: Natural selection is the differential survival and reproduction (i.e., fitness) of individuals that differ in phenotype. It is a key mechanism of evolution.

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer this question

The variation illustrated is
explanation: It is morphological. They are morphological because they are noticeable physical appearance .

subject: Biology
q: The intensity of competition among animals in a given area will increase with
options: an increase in the frequency of disease outbreaks
explanation: The intensity of competition among animals in a given area will increase with an increase in population size. This is because as the population size increases, the resources available per individual decrease, leading to increased competition for these resources.

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer this question


The economic importance of the larva is that it
options: resembles the plant on which it lives
explanation: The larva feeds on the leaves of the crop. A butterfly may be capable of laying up to 500 eggs. On average however only about 100 will be laid, as many females die before they are able to lay all their eggs. Perhaps 95 of those eggs will hatch. 85 of the resulting caterpillars are likely to be killed by birds, wasps, spiders or parasitoids, leaving just 10 to reach pupation. Studies have found that over half of all wild pupae will be eaten, be killed by parasitoids, or die from desiccation, fungal attack, or other causes.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : ADAPTIVE COLOURATION AND ITS FUNCTIONS

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer this question

The type of adaptation shown by the larva is
explanation: Disruptive coloration in an animal, a colour pattern that is thought to disrupt the perceived contour of the body or parts of the body, thereby making the animal more difficult to see.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : ADAPTIVE COLOURATION AND ITS FUNCTIONS

subject: Biology
q: The part of the mammalian skin involved in temperature regulation is the
explanation: The part of the mammalian skin involved in temperature regulation is the sweat gland. Sweat glands produce sweat, which is a fluid that helps to cool the body when it evaporates from the skin surface. This is a key mechanism for thermoregulation, or maintaining a stable internal body temperature.

subject: Biology
q: The role of the Golgi complex in a eukaryotic cell is to
options: conduct ions in and out of the cell
transport genetic materials out of the cell
transport organic materials in and out of the cell
explanation: The Golgi complex, also known as the Golgi apparatus, is an organelle found in most eukaryotic cells. Its primary function is to process and package the macromolecules such as proteins and lipids that are synthesized by the cell. It is involved in the transport of lipids around the cell, and the creation of lysosomes. Hence, the option 'transport organic materials in and out of the cell' is the correct answer.

topics.title: LIVING ORGANISMS : CELL STRUCTURE AND FUNCTIONS OF CELL COMPONENTS

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer this question.


The maxillary palp is labelled
explanation: The part labelled IV is the maxiliary palp. The maxillary palp in insects is a sensory appendage located on the maxilla, consisting of several segments covered with sensory hairs (sensilla). It detects chemical and mechanical stimuli, aiding in feeding behavior by assessing food suitability and locating prey or hosts. This structure varies across species, reflecting adaptations to different ecological roles and feeding strategies in insects

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : STRUCTURAL ADAPTATIONS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer this question.

The structure labelled I is sensitive to
explanation: Insects' antennae are primarily sensitive to chemical stimuli, aiding in their sense of smell and taste, as well as touch stimuli, helping them navigate their environment and detect obstacle

topics.title: COORDINATION AND CONTROL : THE SENSE ORGANS

subject: Biology
q: The dominant phase in the life cycle of a bryophyte is the
explanation: In the life cycle of a bryophyte, the gametophyte is the dominant phase. Bryophytes are non-vascular plants, including mosses, liverworts, and hornworts. They reproduce via spores rather than seeds and their life cycle is characterized by an alternation of generations. The gametophyte stage is the sexual phase in the life cycle of plants and algae. It is haploid, meaning it has half the number of chromosomes as the diploid sporophyte stage. In bryophytes, the gametophyte stage is the most visible and is the stage that carries out most of the plant's photosynthesis.

subject: Biology
q: The evidence that supports the advancement of ferns over mosses is derived from
explanation: The advancement of ferns over mosses is supported by comparative anatomy. Comparative anatomy is the study of similarities and differences in the anatomy of different species. It is closely related to evolutionary biology and phylogeny (the evolution of species). The comparison of body structures in different species to understand the adaptive changes they have undergone to survive in different environments is a key principle in comparative anatomy. In this case, the structure of ferns shows more complexity and advancement over mosses.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following vertebrates has the most simple structured heart?
explanation: Fish have the most simple structured heart among the given options. The heart of a fish consists of two main parts: an atrium and a ventricle, making it a two-chambered heart. This is simpler in structure compared to the hearts of reptiles, mammals, and amphibians. Reptiles (except crocodiles), and amphibians have a three-chambered heart, and mammals have a four-chambered heart.

topics.title: STRUCTURAL/BEHAVIOURAL ADAPTATIONS OF VERTEBRATES : BONY FISH

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer this question


A unique characteristic that distinguishes the class of vertebrates represented from other vertebrates is the presence of
explanation: A unique characteristic that distinguishes the class of vertebrates represented from other vertebrates is the presence of a covering of feathers.

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer this question

The structures used for offense and defence are labelled
explanation: The structures used for offense and defence are labelled I and II i.e the beak and the feet.

subject: Biology
q: One primitive feature of the coelenterates is the possession of
explanation: One primitive feature of the coelenterates (also known as cnidarians, which include jellyfish, sea anemones, and corals) is the possession of radial symmetry. Radial symmetry is a type of symmetry where any longitudinal cut through the organism's central axis will give two identical halves. This is a primitive feature because it is seen in less complex organisms. Bilateral symmetry, where only one longitudinal cut can give two identical halves, is seen in more complex organisms.

subject: Biology
q: A characteristic that best exemplifies the evolutionary advancement of mammals over other vertebrates is the
explanation: The viviparous mode of reproduction is a characteristic that best exemplifies the evolutionary advancement of mammals over other vertebrates. Viviparity is the condition of giving birth to live young ones (instead of laying eggs) which develop within the mother's body. This is a feature that is predominantly found in mammals and is a significant evolutionary advancement over other vertebrates.

subject: Biology
q: An organism which exhibits extracellular digestion is
explanation: Rhizopus is an organism which exhibits extracellular digestion. Extracellular digestion is a process where food is broken down outside the cells in a digestive cavity and then absorbed into the body. Rhizopus, a type of fungus, secretes enzymes into the surrounding environment to break down complex organic materials into simpler substances which are then absorbed for nutrition.

subject: Biology
q: The causative agent of typhoid fever is
explanation: The causative agent of typhoid fever is Salmonella. More specifically, it is caused by the bacterium Salmonella typhi. This bacterium is a pathogenic microorganism that causes typhoid fever, a serious and potentially fatal disease in humans.

subject: Biology
q: Grasses recover quickly from bush fires in the savanna because of their
explanation: Grasses recover quickly from bush fires in the savanna because of their perennating organs. Perennating organs are structures in plants that enable them to survive adverse conditions such as fires, droughts, and cold. In grasses, these are often underground stems or roots that can quickly regenerate the above-ground parts of the plant after a fire.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : ADAPTIVE COLOURATION AND ITS FUNCTIONS

subject: Biology
q: Climax communities in a biotic succession are usually characterized by
options: a constant change in the appearance of the communities
a stable composition of plant and animals
rapid changes in the plant and animal species
explanation: Climax communities in a biotic succession are usually characterized by a stable composition of plant and animals. A climax community is the final stage of succession, remaining relatively unchanged until destroyed by an event such as fire or human interference. It is a stable and mature community in a successive sequence that has reached equilibrium after having evolved through stages and adapted to its environment.

topics.title: THE ECOLOGY OF POPULATIONS : ECOLOGICAL SUCCESSION

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer the question


Which of the following can be deduced from the distribution?
options: Species II is found only in locations where it rains all year round
Species III increases in number as rainfall increases
The distribution of species IV is not dependent on rainfall
Species I thrives under conditions of moderate to high rainfall
explanation: Species I thrives under conditions of moderate to high rainfall.

topics.title: ECOLOGY : FACTORS AFFECTING THE DISTRIBUTION OF ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer the question

The species whose distribution is not affected by rainfall is
explanation: The distribution of SpII is not affected by rainfall.

topics.title: ECOLOGY : FACTORS AFFECTING THE DISTRIBUTION OF ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: The soil type that contains nutrients which are not readily available for plants is
explanation: Clayey soil is known for its poor drainage and compact nature, which makes it difficult for plant roots to access the nutrients within the soil. The other soil types listed (alluvial, sandy, and loamy) generally have better drainage and are more conducive to plant growth.

topics.title: SOIL : CHARACTERISTICS OF DIFFERENT TYPES OF SOIL

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram to answer the question that follows.


The importance of the association to the fungus is that it obtains
options: water and mineral salts from the algae
explanation: It obtains oxygen and carbohydrates from the algae. In lichens, fungi and algae form a symbiotic relationship. The fungus provides structure and protection, retaining water and nutrients, while the algae perform photosynthesis, producing organic compounds (oxygen and carbohydrates) that nourish both organisms. This mutualistic relationship benefits both the fungi and the algae.

topics.title: ECOLOGY : SYMBIOTIC INTERACTIONS OF PLANTS AND ANIMALS

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram to answer the question that follows.

The association illustrated demonstrates
explanation: It demonstrates mutualism. Mutualism is the way two organisms of different species exist in a relationship in which each individual benefits from the activity of the other. Similar interactions within a species are known as co-operation.

topics.title: ECOLOGY : SYMBIOTIC INTERACTIONS OF PLANTS AND ANIMALS

subject: Biology
q: A population that doubles in size at constant intervals is an indication of
explanation: Exponential growth refers to the phenomenon where the population size doubles at constant intervals. This is because the growth rate is proportional to the current population. In other words, as the population increases, the rate of increase also increases.

topics.title: THE ECOLOGY OF POPULATIONS : FACTORS AFFECTING POPULATION SIZES

subject: Biology
q: The main purpose of establishing shelter belts in the Sahel region is to
explanation: Shelter belts are barriers of trees and shrubs that are established to protect against wind and soil erosion. In the Sahel region, they are primarily used to check desert encroachment, which is the spread of desert conditions into areas that were previously fertile.

topics.title: HUMANS AND ENVIRONMENT : CONSERVATION OF NATURAL RESOURCES

subject: Biology
q: The organs that will be most useful to giant African rats in finding their way in underground habitats are the
explanation: Vibrissae, or whiskers, are highly sensitive to touch, allowing animals to detect changes in their environment. This would be particularly useful for giant African rats in navigating their underground habitats, where visibility is limited.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : BEHAVIOURAL ADAPTATIONS IN SOCIAL ANIMALS

subject: Biology
q: The increasing order of the particle size in the following soil types is
explanation: The particle size of soil types increases in the order of clay, silt, sand, and gravel. Clay particles are the smallest, followed by silt, then sand, and finally gravel which has the largest particles. This is because clay particles are less than 0.002 mm, silt particles are 0.002 mm to 0.05 mm, sand particles are 0.05 mm to 2 mm, and gravel particles are greater than 2 mm in diameter.

topics.title: SOIL : CHARACTERISTICS OF DIFFERENT TYPES OF SOIL

subject: Biology
q: The causative agent of bird flu is a
explanation: Bird flu, also known as avian influenza, is caused by a virus. Influenza viruses are a type of RNA virus that infects birds and can also infect humans and other animals. The virus is highly contagious among birds and can be deadly to them.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following is an example of parasitism?
options: Fungi growing on a dead tree branch
A squirrel living in an abandoned nest of a bird
Cattle egrets taking ticks from the body of cattle
explanation: Parasitism is a type of symbiotic relationship where one organism, the parasite, benefits at the expense of the other, the host. Mistletoe growing on an orange tree is an example of parasitism because the mistletoe (parasite) absorbs water and nutrients from the orange tree (host), which can harm or even kill the tree.

topics.title: ECOLOGY : SYMBIOTIC INTERACTIONS OF PLANTS AND ANIMALS

subject: Biology
q: One adaptation shown by hydrophytes in fresh water habitats is the
options: poor development of roots and xylem tissues
explanation: Hydrophytes are plants that have adapted to living in aquatic environments. They often have poorly developed roots as they are not needed for water absorption and xylem tissues (which are responsible for the transport of water in plants) are also less developed because water can be absorbed directly from their surroundings.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : STRUCTURAL ADAPTATIONS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: A crucible of 5gm weighed 10gm after filling with fresh soil. It is then heated in an oven at 100oC for 1 hour. After cooling in a desiccator, the weight was 8gm. The percentage of water in the soil is
explanation:
Weight of wet soil = 10gm - 5gm = 5gm
Weight of dry soil = 8gm - 5gm = 3 gm Percentage of water lost in the soil = 3gm/5gm × 100% = 60%
Percentage left in the soil will be 100% - 60% = 40%

topics.title: SOIL : CHARACTERISTICS OF DIFFERENT TYPES OF SOIL

subject: Biology
q: The highest level of ecological organization is the
explanation: The highest level of ecological organization is the biosphere. The biosphere is the global sum of all ecosystems. It can also be termed as the zone of life on Earth, a closed system (apart from solar and cosmic radiation and heat from the interior of the Earth), and largely self-regulating.

subject: Biology
q: The mangrove swamp in Nigeria is restricted to the
explanation: Mangrove swamps are found in tropical and subtropical tidal areas. In Nigeria, they are found in the coastal region, which is part of the tropical rainforest, not in the savanna regions. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Tropical rainforest'.

subject: Biology
q: A biotic factor which affects the distribution and abundance of organisms in a terrestrial habitat is
explanation: Biotic factors are the living components of an ecosystem. They are the interactions between organisms. In this case, 'competition' is a biotic factor. Competition in biology is a type of interaction between species where the fitness of one is lowered by the presence of another. This can affect the distribution and abundance of organisms in a terrestrial habitat.

topics.title: ECOLOGY : FACTORS AFFECTING THE DISTRIBUTION OF ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: In which of the following Nigerian states can montane vegetation be found?
explanation: Montane vegetation is found in mountainous areas. In Nigeria, such vegetation can be found in the Plateau state, which is known for its cooler temperatures due to its high altitude. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Plateau'.

topics.title: LOCAL(NIGERIAN) BIOMES : HIGHLANDS OF MONTANE FORESTS AND GRASSLANDS

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer this question


The type of protective adaptation exhibited by the animal is
explanation:
Countershading, or Thayer's Law, is a form of camouflage. Countershading is the pattern of animal coloration in which an animal's pigmentation is darker on the upper side and lighter on the underside of the body. This pattern is found in many species of mammals, reptiles, birds, and fish.


Countershading is a protective adaptation whereby an animal possess a darker colour in its dorsal side and a lighter colour in its ventral side. This helps the animal to camouflage and escape predation. This is exhibited by the animal in the above diagram.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : ADAPTIVE COLOURATION AND ITS FUNCTIONS

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer this question

The structure labelled I is
explanation: It is tactile because of its sensitive to touch. Tactile sensation in rats is mainly mediated through their whiskers, which are essential for navigation, object identification, and social interaction. Each whisker is anchored in a follicle rich in blood vessels and nerve endings, with mechanoreceptors detecting movements. Sensory information from whiskers is processed in the somatosensory cortex, specifically in regions called "barrels," forming a detailed map for tactile perception. This system enables rats to explore and interact with their environment effectively.

topics.title: COORDINATION AND CONTROL : THE SENSE ORGANS

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following factors can bring about competition in a population?
explanation: Competition in a population can be brought about by various factors, one of which is scarcity of resources. Drought, which is a prolonged period of abnormally low rainfall leading to a shortage of water, can cause scarcity of resources. This scarcity can lead to competition among individuals in a population as they struggle to access the limited resources. The other options, dispersion, emigration, and mortality, do not directly cause competition.

subject: Biology
q: The theory which supports the view that the large muscles developed by an athlete will be passed on to the offspring was proposed by
explanation: The theory that the large muscles developed by an athlete will be passed on to the offspring was proposed by Lamarck. This is known as Lamarck's theory of inheritance of acquired characteristics. According to this theory, an organism can change during its lifetime in response to its environment, and those changes are passed on to its offspring. This theory has been largely discredited in modern biology. The other options, Darwin, Mendel, and Pasteur, proposed different theories.

subject: Biology
q: In lizards, the lowering of the gular fold is used to
explanation: In lizards, the lowering of the gular fold is used to attract mates. The gular fold is a part of a lizard's throat that can be expanded and contracted. During courtship, a male lizard may lower its gular fold to display its bright throat colors to attract a female. The other options, catch insects, defend their territory, and frighten enemies, are not the primary uses of the gular fold.

subject: Biology
q: An example of a fish that aestivates is
explanation: Aestivation is a state of animal dormancy, similar to hibernation, characterized by inactivity and a lowered metabolic rate, that is entered in response to high temperatures and arid conditions. The African lungfish is an example.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : BEHAVIOURAL ADAPTATIONS IN SOCIAL ANIMALS

subject: Biology
q: In mammals, the organ directly on top of the kidney is the
explanation: The adrenal glands (also known as suprarenal glands) are endocrine glands that produce a variety of hormones including adrenaline and the steroids aldosterone and cortisol. They are found above the kidneys. Each gland has an outer cortex which produces steroid hormones and an inner medulla.

topics.title: INTERNAL STRUCTURE OF A MAMMAL : MAMMALIAN INTERNAL ORGANS

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer this question


The diagram shows that the organisms are
explanation: They are oviparous. Oviparous animals are animals that lay eggs, with little or no other embryonic development within the mother. This is the reproductive method of most fish, amphibians, reptiles, all birds, the monotremes, and most insects, molluscs, and arachnids.

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer this question

The breeding posture illustrated in the diagram is known as
explanation: Amplexus is the mating position of frogs and toads, in which the male clasps the female about the back.

subject: Biology
q: The photosynthetic pigments include
explanation: Photosynthetic pigments are molecules which are responsible for capturing light energy, which is then used to convert carbon dioxide and water into glucose. The main pigments are chlorophylls and carotenoids. Chlorophylls absorb blue and red light and reflect green light, which is why we see plants as green. Carotenoids absorb blue and green light and reflect yellow, orange and red light.

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer this question


Insulin is produced by the endocrine organ labelled
explanation: Insulin is produced by the endocrine organ labelled III, Insulin is produced by the pancreas

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer this question

The function of the structure labelled I is to
options: stimulate the development of secondary sexual characters
maintain proper glucose concentration in the blood
regulate the activities of other endocrine glands
explanation: It is to regulate the activities of other endocrine glands. The part labelled I is the pituitary gland known as th master gland , it control all other endocrine glands in the body

subject: Biology
q: Monocots stems differ from dicot stems in that monocots have
explanation: Monocots stems differ from dicot stems in that monocots have no cambium. The cambium is a layer of tissue in plants that provides undifferentiated cells for plant growth. It is responsible for secondary growth in plants, which leads to an increase in stem thickness. Monocots, however, do not have this secondary growth and therefore lack cambium.

topics.title: INTERNAL STRUCTURE OF A FLOWERING PLANT : STEM

subject: Biology
q: The opening and closing of the stoma are regulated by
explanation: The opening and closing of the stoma are regulated by osmosis. Osmosis is the process by which water molecules move from an area of high concentration to an area of low concentration through a semi-permeable membrane. In the case of stomata, the guard cells surrounding the stomata swell or shrink due to osmosis, causing the stomata to open or close.

subject: Biology
q: Stunted growth and poor root development are a result of a deficiency in
explanation: Stunted growth and poor root development are a result of a deficiency in phosphorus. Phosphorus is a vital nutrient for plants, playing a key role in energy transfer and storage, as well as in photosynthesis, respiration, and cell division. A deficiency in phosphorus can lead to stunted growth and poor root development.

subject: Biology
q: The pancreas secretes enzymes for the digestion of
explanation: The pancreas secretes enzymes that aid in the digestion of fats, proteins, and carbohydrates. These enzymes include lipase (for fats), protease (for proteins), and amylase (for carbohydrates). Therefore, the correct answer is 'fats, proteins and carbohydrates'.

subject: Biology
q: Pineapple is an example of
explanation: A pineapple is an example of a composite fruit, also known as a multiple fruit. This type of fruit develops from the merging of several ovaries that were separate in a single flower. Therefore, the correct answer is 'a composite fruit'.

topics.title: GROWTH : GERMINATION OF SEEDS AND CONDITION NECESSARY FOR GERMINATION OF SEEDS

subject: Biology
q: The correct sequence of the movement of urea during urine formation is
options: glomerulus → Bowman's capsule → convoluted tubule → Henle's loop → collecting tubule
glomerulus → Bowman's capsule → convoluted tubule → convoluted tubule → collecting tubule
convoluted tubule → glomerulus → Henle's loop → Bowman's capsule → collecting tubule
convoluted tubule → Bowman's capsule → Henle's loop → glomerulus → collecting tubule
explanation: The correct sequence of the movement of urea during urine formation is from the glomerulus to the Bowman's capsule, then to the convoluted tubule, Henle's loop, and finally the collecting tubule. The glomerulus filters the blood, and the filtrate, which contains urea, is collected in the Bowman's capsule. The filtrate then moves through the convoluted tubule and Henle's loop where reabsorption of water and solutes occurs. The remaining filtrate, now urine, moves to the collecting tubule. Therefore, the correct answer is 'glomerulus → Bowman's capsule → convoluted tubule → Henle's loop → collecting tubule'.

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer the question


Oxygenated blood is pumped to the entire body from the part labelled
explanation: The part labelled IV is known as left verticles. Oxygenated blood is pumped from the left ventricle of the heart into the aorta. The aorta then distributes this blood throughout the body via its branches, ensuring that all tissues and organs receive oxygen and nutrients essential for their function.

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer the question

The part labelled I is the
explanation: It is the pulmonary artery. The part labelled I is the pulmonary artery. The pulmonary artery carries deoxygenated blood from the right ventricle to the lungs, where it splits into the left and right pulmonary arteries. Its primary function is to transport blood to the lungs for oxygenation.

subject: Biology
q: The waste product of plants used in the conversion of hide to leather is
explanation: The waste product of plants used in the conversion of hide to leather is tannin. Tannin is a type of polyphenolic compound found in various plant tissues. It is used in the leather industry for tanning hides and skins, a process that converts the raw hide into a material that will not decompose and is suitable for a variety of end applications.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following uses diffusion as the principal method of gaseous exchange?
explanation: Earthworms use diffusion as the principal method of gaseous exchange. They breathe through their skin, where oxygen from the environment diffuses directly into their bloodstream and carbon dioxide diffuses out. This process is facilitated by the moist mucus that covers the earthworm's skin.

topics.title: RESPIRATION : THE MECHANISM OF GASEOUS EXCHANGE IN PLANTS AND MAMMALS

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer this question


The experiment demonstrates
explanation:
It demonstrates geotropism. It is a turning or growth movement by a plant or fungus in response to gravity. It is a general feature of all higher and many lower plants as well as other organisms.
Geotropism is the directional growth of parts of plant in response to gravity. This experiment demonstrate geotropism showing that the root undergoes positive geotropism (towards gravity) and the stem undergoes negative geotropism (against gravity)

topics.title: SUPPORT AND MOVEMENT : TROPIC TACTIC NASTIC AND SLEEP MOVEMENTS IN PLANTS

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer this question

The part marked I will contain a high concentration of
explanation: The part marked I will contain a high concentration of auxin. causes the fast growth of shoot.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following has the most primitive respiratory system?
explanation: Snails have the most primitive respiratory system among the options provided. They breathe using a pallial lung, which is a type of lung that is developed from the mantle cavity. This is a simple and primitive system compared to the more complex respiratory systems of fish (gills), insects (tracheal system), and mice (lungs).

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer this question


The part labelled IV is responsible for
explanation: Osmoregulation is the active regulation of the osmotic pressure of an organism's fluids to maintain the homeostasis of the organism's water content; that is, it keeps the organism's fluids from becoming too diluted or too concentrated. The part labelled IV is the contractile vacuole. In a paramecium, osmoregulation is primarily managed by the contractile vacuole. The contractile vacuole is a specialized organelle responsible for regulating water intake and expulsion to maintain proper osmotic balance within the cell. It works by accumulating excess water that enters the cell through osmosis and then periodically expelling this water to prevent the cell from bursting due to osmotic pressure.

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer this question

The organelle responsible for sexual reproduction is
explanation: III. The parts labelled II and III are the macronucleus and micronucleus respectively. Paramecium typically has one macronucleus and one or more micronuclei. The macronucleus is responsible for general cell functions, while micronuclei are involved in genetic exchange during conjugation, a form of sexual reproduction in Paramecium. Therefore, the organelle responsible for sexual reproduction is III

subject: Biology
q: A water medium is necessary for fertilization in
explanation: Ferns are a type of plant that require a water medium for fertilization. This is because their sperm cells are flagellated and need water to swim to the egg cells for fertilization to occur. Fungi, conifers, and angiosperms do not require a water medium for fertilization.

subject: Biology
q: The chromosomes of members of the kingdom Monera are within the
explanation: Members of the kingdom Monera, which includes bacteria, do not have a true nucleus. Instead, their genetic material, or chromosomes, are found within the cytoplasm of the cell. This is a key characteristic that distinguishes them from eukaryotic organisms, which have a nucleus that houses their genetic material.

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram to answer the question that follows.


The organelle responsible for heredity is
explanation:
III is the chromosome. The part labelled III is the chromosome.
Chromosomes play a crucial role in heredity by carrying genetic information from one generation to the next. Chromosomes are responsible for heredity by:

- Carrying genes that code for specific traits
- Replicating and passing on genetic information during cell division
- Pairing and recombining genetic material to increase diversity
- Determining the expression of traits and characteristics

This process ensures that the genetic information is transmitted from one generation to the next, making chromosomes the fundamental units of heredity.

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram to answer the question that follows.

The part labelled I is the
explanation:
plasmallema. The part labeled I is the plasmallema. The plasmalemma (cell membrane) is a thin, semi-permeable membrane that:

- Separates the cell from its environment
- Protects the cell from harm
- Regulates the movement of materials in and out
- Receives and transmits cell signals
- Facilitates cell adhesion

It is composed of a phospholipid bilayer with embedded proteins, and its functions are essential for maintaining cellular integrity and homeostasis.

topics.title: LIVING ORGANISMS : CELL STRUCTURE AND FUNCTIONS OF CELL COMPONENTS

subject: Biology
q: The process of shedding the exoskeleton of an arthropod is known as
explanation: Ecdysis is the process of shedding the exoskeleton in arthropods. This is a necessary process as the exoskeleton is rigid and cannot grow with the organism. Therefore, the organism must periodically shed its exoskeleton and form a new one that is larger to accommodate its growth. Metamorphosis refers to the process of transformation from an immature form to an adult form in two or more distinct stages. Instar formation refers to the stages between each moulting in the life cycle of insects and other arthropods. Tagmosis is the fusion of segments into functional units.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : STRUCTURAL ADAPTATIONS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following is common to the mosquito housefly and blackfly?
options: Their adults have two pairs of wings
explanation: Mosquitoes, houseflies, and blackflies all undergo complete metamorphosis. This means they go through four distinct stages of life: egg, larva, pupa, and adult. Not all of these insects have aquatic immature stages, are parasites of man, or have two pairs of wings.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : STRUCTURAL ADAPTATIONS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer the question


The process illustrated is
explanation: The process illustrated in the diagram is sexual reproduction in Rhizopus.

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer the question

The structure labelled I is the
explanation:
It is the zygospore. The part labeled I is the zygospore. The zygospore of Rhizopus:

- Is a large, spherical or oval-shaped cell with a thick, multilayered wall
- Contains multiple nuclei and abundant cytoplasm
- Results from sexual reproduction, formed by fusion of two haploid nuclei
- Serves as a survival structure, tolerating adverse conditions
- Remains dormant until favorable conditions return
- Germinates to produce a new haploid generation, restarting the life cycle.

In short, the zygospore is a resilient, protective structure ensuring Rhizopus' survival and propagation.

subject: Biology
q: An accurate identification of a rapist can be carried out by conducting a
explanation: DNA analysis is the most accurate method for identifying a rapist. This is because each person's DNA is unique to them (except in the case of identical twins). A DNA sample can be obtained from body fluids, hair, or skin cells left at the crime scene. RNA analysis, blood group tests, and behavioural traits tests are not as accurate or reliable.

topics.title: HEREDITY : APPLICATION OF THE PRINCIPLES OF HEREDITY

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer the question


If the dogs are offspring of a monohybrid cross and the gene G for grey head is dominant over its allele g, the individual whose genotype is likely to be gg is
explanation: If the dogs are offspring of a monohybrid cross and the gene G for grey head is dominant over its allele g, the individual whose genotype is likely to be gg is IV.

topics.title: HEREDITY : INHERITANCE OF CHARACTERS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer the question

Which of the following will be true of dog II which lost its tail in an accident if it mates with dog III?
options: None of its offspring will be born without a tail
3/4 of its offspring will be born without tails
all of its offspring will be born without tails
1/4 of its offspring will be born without tails
explanation: None of its offspring will be born without a tail.

topics.title: HEREDITY : INHERITANCE OF CHARACTERS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following is true of cloning?
options: It is welcomed as an ethically and morally sound science
Only one cell of the original organism is needed to initiate the process
The clone is similar to but not exactly like the original organism
It involves the asexual multiplication of the tissues of the original organism
explanation: The clone is similar to but not exactly like the original organism.

subject: Biology
q: An example of a sex-linked trait is the
options: ability to grow long hair in females
explanation: Sex-linked traits are those that are associated with genes found on sex chromosomes. Haemophilia is a sex-linked trait because the gene that causes it is located on the X chromosome, which corresponds to option 4. The other options are incorrect because they are not associated with genes on the sex chromosomes.

subject: Biology
q: In terms of the number of individuals, which of the following taxa is most inclusive?
explanation: In terms of the number of individuals, the taxon that is most inclusive is the Class. The hierarchy of biological classification from most inclusive to least inclusive is: Domain, Kingdom, Phylum, Class, Order, Family, Genus, Species. Therefore, a Class includes more individuals than an Order, Family, or Species.

subject: Biology
q: A characteristic that can possibly be shared by both living and non-living organisms is
explanation: Both living and non-living organisms can increase in size. For example, a rock (non-living) can increase in size through accumulation of other minerals, and a tree (living) grows larger as it matures.

subject: Biology
q: The cell of an onion bulb can be differentiated from a cheek cell by the presence of
explanation: The cell of an onion bulb can be differentiated from a cheek cell by the presence of a cell wall. The cell wall is a rigid layer that is found outside the cell membrane and provides additional support and protection. Animal cells, such as cheek cells, do not have a cell wall.

topics.title: LIVING ORGANISMS : CELL STRUCTURE AND FUNCTIONS OF CELL COMPONENTS

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer the question


Which of the organisms is characterized by the possession of a chitinous exoskeleton?
explanation: I is characterized by the possession of a chitinous exoskeleton. I is an arthropod which possesses chitin.

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer the question

The type of reproduction illustrated in IV is
explanation: Budding is a form of asexual reproduction in which a new organism develops from an outgrowth or bud due to cell division at one particular site.

subject: Biology
q: In plants, the structure that performs a similar function with the testis in mammals is the
explanation: In plants, the structure that performs a similar function with the testis in mammals is the anther. The anther is part of the stamen (male reproductive organ in flowering plants) and is responsible for the production of pollen, which is equivalent to sperm production in mammals.

subject: Biology
q: The bacteria type that are arranged in chains are the
explanation: The bacteria type that are arranged in chains are the Streptococci. The term 'Streptococci' is derived from Greek words 'streptos' meaning chain and 'kokkos' meaning berry. This is because these bacteria are usually found in chains or pairs, after division along a single axis.

subject: Biology
q: The most abundant group of organisms in the animal kingdom is
explanation: Insects are the most diverse and abundant group of organisms in the animal kingdom, with more than a million described species and potentially representing over 90% of the differing animal life forms on Earth.

subject: Biology
q: Radial symmetry is a feature common to the
explanation: Radial symmetry is the arrangement of body parts around a central axis, like spokes on a wheel. This type of symmetry is found in simple organisms such as the sea anemone, a member of the phylum Cnidaria (Coelenterata).

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following is used mainly for balancing in fish?
explanation: The dorsal fin helps a fish to keep its balance in the water. Without a dorsal fin, a fish would roll over onto its side.

topics.title: STRUCTURAL/BEHAVIOURAL ADAPTATIONS OF VERTEBRATES : BONY FISH

subject: Biology
q: The beak of a duck is structurally adapted for
explanation: The beak of a duck is broad and flat, and is adapted for scooping and sieving food from the water. This is because ducks are often 'dabbling ducks' - they feed in the water by tipping forward and grazing on underwater plants.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : STRUCTURAL ADAPTATIONS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: The most important characteristics that makes reptiles to conquer terrestrial habitats is the possession of
explanation: The most important characteristic that allows reptiles to conquer terrestrial habitats is the possession of an amniotic egg. The amniotic egg provides a complete environment for the developing embryo, including a shell for protection and membranes for nourishment and waste removal. This allows reptiles to lay their eggs on land, away from the water, which gives them a significant advantage in colonizing a wide range of terrestrial habitats.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : STRUCTURAL ADAPTATIONS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: In a dicotyledonous stem, the zone between the epidermis and the pericycle is the
explanation: In a dicotyledonous stem, the zone between the epidermis (outer layer) and the pericycle (a layer of cells surrounding the vascular tissue) is the cortex. The cortex is a tissue of the stem or root of a plant, lying between the epidermis and the vascular tissue. It is mostly made up of relatively unspecialized cells, with large intercellular spaces.

topics.title: INTERNAL STRUCTURE OF A FLOWERING PLANT : STEM

subject: Biology
q: The order of passage of food in the digestive system is
explanation: The order of passage of food in the digestive system is from the ileum to the caecum, then to the large intestine, and finally to the rectum. The ileum is the final part of the small intestine where most of the absorption of nutrients occurs. The caecum is a pouch at the beginning of the large intestine that receives chyme from the ileum, and connects to the colon. The large intestine absorbs water and electrolytes from the remaining indigestible food matter, and then passes it to the rectum for expulsion from the body.

subject: Biology
q: Insectivorous plants trap and kill their prey to derive
explanation: Insectivorous plants trap and kill their prey to derive nitrogen. Nitrogen is a vital nutrient for plants, but it is scarce in the soil where insectivorous plants grow. Therefore, these plants have evolved to derive the necessary nitrogen by trapping and digesting insects.

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer this question


Water reabsorption takes place in
explanation: The part labelled III is the digestive system and IV is the excretory system.

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer this question

The vessel that carries blood from I to II is the
explanation: Pulmonary artery carries deoxygenated blood to the lungs to get oxygenated .

subject: Biology
q: In the alimentary system of a bird, the function of teeth is carried out by the
explanation: In the alimentary system of a bird, the function of teeth is carried out by the gizzard. Birds do not have teeth to grind their food. Instead, they have a specialized stomach, known as a gizzard, where the grinding of food occurs. The gizzard contains small stones, which the bird swallows, that aid in the mechanical breakdown of food.

subject: Biology
q: What will happen when two equal sized pieces of unripe pawpaw labelled X and Y are dropped into equal volumes of concentrated salt solution and distilled water respectively?
explanation: When a piece of unripe pawpaw is dropped into distilled water, it will become turgid. This is due to the process of osmosis, where water molecules move from an area of lower solute concentration (the distilled water) to an area of higher solute concentration (the pawpaw), causing the pawpaw to swell or become turgid.

subject: Biology
q: Exhaled air differs from inhaled air in that it
explanation: Exhaled air usually has more water vapour than inhaled air. This is because the respiratory system adds moisture (water vapour) to the air during the process of respiration, as the air passes through the nasal passages and the lungs.

topics.title: RESPIRATION : THE MECHANISM OF GASEOUS EXCHANGE IN PLANTS AND MAMMALS

subject: Biology
q: In the mammalian kidney, the Bowman's capsule is located in the
explanation: In the mammalian kidney, the Bowman's capsule is located in the cortex. The cortex is the outer part of the kidney, and it contains the renal corpuscles, which are made up of the Bowman's capsule and the glomerulus.

subject: Biology
q: A plant parenchyma cell also acts as a supporting tissue when it
explanation: A plant parenchyma cell acts as a supporting tissue when it becomes turgid. Turgidity refers to the state of being swollen due to high fluid content. In the context of plant cells, turgidity is achieved when water enters the cell, causing it to swell and become rigid. This rigidity provides support to the plant.

topics.title: SUPPORT AND MOVEMENT : SUPPORTING TISSUES IN ANIMALS

explanation: Wind, It is pollinated because it pollen grains are small, smooth, non-sticky and light, and also it anther are attached to the flower in such a way that they readily swing in the air to release the pollen grains.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : STRUCTURAL ADAPTATIONS IN ORGANISMS

q:
Use the diagram below to answer this question

The male inflorescence is labelled
explanation: The part labelled I is the male inflorescence. To identify the male inflorescence (tassel) in a maize plant, look for the large, cylindrical structure at the top of the plant. It consists of spike-like structures with yellowish or light green color, containing clusters of flowers that produce pollen for fertilization. So option I is correct.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : STRUCTURAL ADAPTATIONS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: During ovulation, an egg is released from the
explanation: During ovulation, an egg is released from the graafian follicle. The graafian follicle is a mature follicle in the ovary that contains the developing ovum (egg). When the follicle ruptures, it releases the egg, which is then ready for fertilization.

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer this question


The part labelled I represents the
explanation: lag phase. The part labelled I is the lag phase.In a growth curve, the lag phase is the initial phase where bacteria or other microorganisms adapt to their new environment. During this phase, there is little to no increase in cell number as the organisms are adjusting to the growth medium, synthesizing enzymes, and preparing for active growth. The length of the lag phase can vary depending on factors such as the organism's metabolic state and the composition of the growth medium. Once the lag phase ends, the organisms enter the exponential (log) phase where they grow and divide at their maximum rate.

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer this question

The part labelled IV indicates that the yeast population has
explanation: It indicates reached its maximum size. The plateau or stationary phase is labelled IV in the graph. in a growth curve occurs when microorganisms stabilize in number due to balanced growth and death rates. This phase follows the exponential growth phase and is characterized by metabolic adjustments and survival strategies in response to limited resources or environmental stressors.

topics.title: THE ECOLOGY OF POPULATIONS : FACTORS AFFECTING POPULATION SIZES

subject: Biology
q: The transmission of impulses along a nerve fibre is characterized by
explanation: The transmission of impulses along a nerve fibre is characterized by electrical and ionic changes. When a nerve impulse is transmitted, there is a change in the electrical potential of the nerve fiber due to the movement of ions (such as sodium and potassium) across the nerve membrane. This is known as an action potential.

subject: Biology
q: The major consequence of bush burning in an ecosystem is
options: the loss of water absorbing ability of the soil
explanation: Bush burning in an ecosystem leads to the loss of biological diversity. This is because the fire destroys the habitats of various organisms, leading to a decrease in the number of species in that area. The other options are also consequences of bush burning, but the major consequence is the loss of biological diversity.

topics.title: HUMANS AND ENVIRONMENT : CONSERVATION OF NATURAL RESOURCES

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following associations is an example of mutualism?
explanation: Hydra viridis and zoochlorellae exhibit a mutualistic relationship. Mutualism is a type of symbiotic relationship where both organisms benefit. In this case, the Hydra viridis provides the zoochlorellae with protection and nutrients, while the zoochlorellae provide the Hydra viridis with oxygen and food through photosynthesis.

topics.title: ECOLOGY : SYMBIOTIC INTERACTIONS OF PLANTS AND ANIMALS

subject: Biology
q: In a typical freshwater habitat, the edge of the stream or pond constitutes the
explanation: In a typical freshwater habitat, the edge of the stream or pond constitutes the littoral zone. The littoral zone is the part of a body of water that is close to the shore. In contrast, the tidal zone is the area that is above water at low tide and under water at high tide, the intertidal zone is the area between the high tide and low tide, and the euphotic zone is the layer of water that sunlight can penetrate, allowing photosynthesis to occur.

topics.title: NATURAL HABITAT : AQUATIC TERRESTRIAL AND ARBOREAL

subject: Biology
q: The main ecological problem facing intertidal organisms is
explanation: The main ecological problem facing intertidal organisms is desiccation. Intertidal organisms live in the area between high tide and low tide, and are therefore exposed to air and sunlight at low tide. This can cause them to dry out, or desiccate, which is a major challenge for these organisms. None of the other options (floatation, salinity, humidity) pose as significant a problem as desiccation.

topics.title: ECOLOGY : FACTORS AFFECTING THE DISTRIBUTION OF ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: Stomata of some plants are sunken and protected by hairs. These are features of
explanation: Stomata of some plants are sunken and protected by hairs. These are features of xerophytes. Xerophytes are plants that are adapted to live in dry conditions, such as deserts. The sunken stomata and protective hairs help to reduce water loss from the plant, enabling it to survive in arid environments. The other options (mesophytes, epiphytes, hydrophytes) do not typically have these adaptations.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : STRUCTURAL ADAPTATIONS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: An ecological factor that will have the most limiting effect on the abundance of phytoplankton in a turbid pond is
explanation: An ecological factor that will have the most limiting effect on the abundance of phytoplankton in a turbid pond is light. Phytoplankton are photosynthetic organisms that require light to produce food. In a turbid, or murky, pond, light penetration is reduced, limiting the amount of light available for photosynthesis and therefore limiting the abundance of phytoplankton. The other options (pH, oxygen, temperature) can also affect phytoplankton abundance, but not as significantly as light in a turbid pond.

topics.title: ECOLOGY : FACTORS AFFECTING THE DISTRIBUTION OF ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q:

options: The population of species II is growing at a slow rate
The population of species III has reached its carrying capacity
The population of species I has become extinct
The population of species I is always higher than species II
explanation: The population of species I has become extinct.

topics.title: THE ECOLOGY OF POPULATIONS : FACTORS AFFECTING POPULATION SIZES

explanation: The population size of species II on the 20th day is 200.

topics.title: THE ECOLOGY OF POPULATIONS : FACTORS AFFECTING POPULATION SIZES

subject: Biology
q:
In an experiment to determine the percentage of air in a soil sample, the following readings were recorded:
Volume of water in a measuring cylinder = 500cm3
Volume of soil added to water = 350cm3
Volume of water and soil after stirring = 800cm3
The percentage of air in the soil sample is
explanation: The volume of the soil is 350cm^3 and the volume of the water and soil after stirring is 800cm^3. The volume of the air in the soil is 800cm^3 - 500cm^3 = 300cm^3. Therefore, the percentage of air in the soil is (300cm^3 / 350cm^3) * 100% = 14.28%, which corresponds to option 3.

topics.title: SOIL : CHARACTERISTICS OF DIFFERENT TYPES OF SOIL

subject: Biology
q: A boy who is fond of swimming in a pond finds himself passing urine with traces of blood. He is likely to have contracted
explanation: Schistosomiasis, also known as snail fever and bilharzia, is a disease caused by parasitic flatworms called schistosomes. The urinary tract or the intestines may be infected. Symptoms include abdominal pain, diarrhea, bloody stool, or blood in the urine. Those who have been infected for a long time may experience liver damage, kidney failure, infertility, or bladder cancer. In children, it may cause poor growth and learning difficulty.

subject: Biology
q: The easiest way to establish the level of pollution in a local stream is to measure the level of
explanation: The level of dissolved oxygen in water is directly related to the health and quality of the aquatic environment. A decrease in the level of dissolved oxygen can indicate a high level of pollution, as many pollutants can consume oxygen as they decompose. Therefore, measuring the level of oxygen in a stream can provide a good indication of its pollution level.

topics.title: HUMANS AND ENVIRONMENT : POLLUTION AND ITS CONTROL

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following is a major cause of variation among organisms?
explanation: Sexual reproduction is a major cause of variation among organisms. This is because during sexual reproduction, genes from two parents combine in unique ways during meiosis and fertilization. This leads to offspring that are genetically different from their parents and each other, causing variation.

subject: Biology
q: The Rhesus factor of blood was first identified in a category of
explanation: The Rhesus factor of blood was first identified in a category of monkeys. The Rhesus factor is a type of antigen present on the surface of red blood cells. It was first discovered in Rhesus monkeys, which is where it gets its name.
