explanation: The part labeled IV in the digram is the Endoplasmic reticulum.

topics.title: LIVING ORGANISMS : CELL STRUCTURE AND FUNCTIONS OF CELL COMPONENTS

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following is most advanced in the evolutionary trend of animals?
explanation: In the evolutionary trend of animals, the cockroach is the most advanced. This is because it belongs to the class Insecta, which is higher in the animal kingdom hierarchy than the classes of the other options. Liverfluke belongs to the class Trematoda, earthworm to the class Oligochaeta, and snail to the class Gastropoda. Insects are more complex and have more advanced features such as a highly developed nervous system and the ability to fly.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following is the lowest category of classification?
explanation: In the biological classification system, species is the lowest category. This system, also known as the Linnaean system, classifies living organisms into a hierarchy of taxonomic ranks. From highest to lowest, these ranks are: Domain, Kingdom, Phylum, Class, Order, Family, Genus, and Species. Therefore, species is the lowest category of classification.

subject: Biology
q: Plants that show secondary growth are usually found among the
explanation: Secondary growth, which involves the thickening of the plant body, is usually found among the dicotyledons. This is because dicotyledons have a vascular cambium, a type of meristematic tissue that contributes to secondary growth. Thallophytes, pteridophytes, and monocotyledons typically do not show secondary growth.

topics.title: INTERNAL STRUCTURE OF A FLOWERING PLANT : STEM

subject: Biology
q: The fungi are a distinct group of eukaryotes mainly because they have
explanation: Fungi are a distinct group of eukaryotes mainly because they lack chlorophyll. Chlorophyll is the pigment that gives plants their green color and is used in photosynthesis to convert light energy into chemical energy. Fungi, unlike plants, do not perform photosynthesis and therefore do not have chlorophyll. While fungi do produce spores, have many fruiting bodies, and can reproduce both sexually and asexually, these characteristics are not unique to fungi and are shared by other groups of organisms.

subject: Biology
q: An arthropod that is destructive at the early stage of its life cycle is
explanation: Butterfly is the arthropod that is destructive at the early stage.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : BEHAVIOURAL ADAPTATIONS IN SOCIAL ANIMALS

subject: Biology
q: An animal body that can be cut along its axis in any plane to give two identical parts is said to be
explanation: An animal body that can be cut along its axis in any plane to give two identical parts is said to be radially symmetrical. This means that the animal's body has a symmetry around a central axis, similar to the spokes on a wheel. Examples of animals with radial symmetry include starfish and jellyfish. Bilateral symmetry, on the other hand, means that an animal can be divided into mirror-image halves only along one plane, which is typically the midline. Asymmetrical animals cannot be divided into mirror-image halves along any plane, and symmetrical is a general term that could refer to either radial or bilateral symmetry.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following possesses mammary gland?
explanation: Mammary glands are the organs that produce milk for the sustenance of a newborn. These are characteristic of mammals, including whales. Dogfish, sharks, and catfish are types of fish, and fish do not possess mammary glands.

subject: Biology
q: The feature that links birds to reptiles in evolution is the possession of
explanation: Birds and reptiles are linked in evolution by the possession of scales. While birds are covered in feathers, they have scales on their feet and legs. Reptiles are covered in scales. Feathers, beaks, and skeletons are not unique to both birds and reptiles.

subject: Biology
q: Counter-shading is an adaptive feature that enables animals to
explanation: Counter-shading is a form of camouflage that animals use to remain undetected. It involves having a darker color on the top side and a lighter color on the underside. This makes the animal harder to see from both above and below, helping it avoid predators.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : ADAPTIVE COLOURATION AND ITS FUNCTIONS

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following plant structures lacks a waterproof cuticle?
explanation: The root of a plant lacks a waterproof cuticle. The cuticle is a waxy, water-repelling layer found on the epidermal cells of leaves and stems but not roots. It helps to prevent water loss from the plant. Roots, on the other hand, are designed to absorb water and therefore do not have this waterproof layer.

topics.title: INTERNAL STRUCTURE OF A FLOWERING PLANT : ROOT

subject: Biology
q: In the mammalian male reproductive system, the part that serves as a passage for both urine and semen is the
explanation: The urethra in the male reproductive system serves as a passage for both urine and semen. The urethra runs through the penis and carries semen as well as urine out of the body. The ureter is a tube that carries urine from the kidney to the bladder, the bladder stores urine until it is released, and the seminal vesicle produces a significant portion of the fluid that makes up semen.

subject: Biology
q:
In plants, which of the following is required in minute quantities for
growth?
explanation: Copper is required in minute quantities for plant growth. It is a micronutrient that plays a number of important roles in plants, including enzyme activation and photosynthesis. Potassium, phosphorus, and sodium, on the other hand, are macronutrients, which are required by plants in larger amounts.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following organisms is both parasitic and autotrophic?
explanation: Loranthus is a type of parasitic plant that is also capable of photosynthesis, making it both parasitic and autotrophic. Parasitism is a relationship between species, where one organism, the parasite, lives on or in another organism, the host, causing it some harm. Autotrophs are organisms that can produce their own food from the substances available in their surroundings using light (photosynthesis) or chemical energy (chemosynthesis).

topics.title: ECOLOGY : SYMBIOTIC INTERACTIONS OF PLANTS AND ANIMALS

subject: Biology
q: A function of hydrochloric acid produced in the human stomach during digestion is to
explanation: The hydrochloric acid in the stomach stops the action of ptyalin. Ptyalin is an enzyme in saliva that breaks down starch into maltose. When the food reaches the stomach, the acidic environment inactivates the ptyalin, stopping further breakdown of the starch.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following is a polysaccharide?
explanation: Cellulose is a polysaccharide. Polysaccharides are carbohydrates with high molecular weight made up of long chains of monosaccharide units bound together by glycosidic linkages. Cellulose is a complex carbohydrate, or polysaccharide, consisting of 3,000 or more glucose units. The other options, Glucose, Sucrose, and Maltose, are all simple sugars or disaccharides, not polysaccharides.

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer the question that follows.


Transportation in the xylem is represented by
explanation: IV, the xylem transport or conducts water from the root to other parts of the plant.

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer the question that follows.

The arrow labeled II represents the
explanation:
II is the release of a by-product of photosynthesis. This by-product is Oxygen. Also the arrow on the diagram shows that II is released not taken in.

III is the intake of a gas needed for photosynthesis. This gas is carbon (IV)oxide.

subject: Biology
q: In the kidney of animals, the site of ultra-filtration is the
explanation: In the kidney of animals, the site of ultra-filtration is the Bowman's capsule. Ultra-filtration is the process by which blood is filtered in the kidneys to remove waste products and excess substances. This process occurs in the Bowman's capsule, which is a cup-like sac at the beginning of the tubular component of a nephron in the mammalian kidney that performs the first step in the filtration of blood to form urine.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following is involved in secondary thickening in plants?
explanation: Secondary thickening in plants involves the Vascular cambium and cork cambium. Secondary thickening is the process by which stems and roots of plants increase in thickness. This is achieved through the activity of the vascular cambium, which produces secondary xylem and phloem, and the cork cambium, which produces the protective outer layer of the plant.

topics.title: INTERNAL STRUCTURE OF A FLOWERING PLANT : STEM

subject: Biology
q: An example of a fruit that develops from a single carpel is
explanation: If the fruit develops from a single carpel or fused carpels of a single ovary, it is known as a simple fruit as seen in nuts and beans.

topics.title: INTERNAL STRUCTURE OF A FLOWERING PLANT : LEAF

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer the question that follows.


The developing embryo is usually contained in the part labelled
explanation: The part abelled II is known as the uterus or womb , the developing embrayo grows in the uterus

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer the question that follows.

The function of the part labeled III is to
explanation: The function of the part labeled III is to produce egg cells. Ovaries produces eggs

subject: Biology
q: Plant growth can be artificially stimulated by the addition of
explanation: Gibberellins are plant hormones that regulate growth and influence various developmental processes, including stem elongation, germination, dormancy, flowering, sex expression, enzyme induction, and leaf and fruit senescence. Therefore, plant growth can be artificially stimulated by the addition of gibberellin.

subject: Biology
q: The autonomic nervous system consists of neurons that control the
explanation: The autonomic nervous system is a control system that acts largely unconsciously and regulates bodily functions such as the heart rate, digestion, respiratory rate, pupillary response, urination, and sexual arousal. Therefore, it controls the heartbeat.

subject: Biology
q: Plants of temperate origin can be grown in tropical areas in the vegetation zones of the
explanation: Montane forests are found in cooler-climate mountainous areas. Because of the cooler climate, they are able to support plant species from temperate latitudes, which are not adapted to the heat and humidity of the tropics. Therefore, plants of temperate origin can be grown in tropical areas in the vegetation zones of the montane forest.

topics.title: LOCAL(NIGERIAN) BIOMES : HIGHLANDS OF MONTANE FORESTS AND GRASSLANDS

subject: Biology
q: The water cycle is maintained mainly by
options: evaporation and condensation of water in the environment
explanation: The water cycle is maintained mainly by both evaporation and condensation of water in the environment. Evaporation is the process by which water changes from a liquid to a gas or vapor. This is the primary path for water to move from the liquid state back to the water cycle as atmospheric water vapor. Condensation is the process by which water vapor in the air is changed into liquid water. Condensation is crucial to the water cycle because it is responsible for the formation of clouds. These clouds may produce precipitation, which is the primary route for water to return to the Earth's surface within the water cycle.

topics.title: ECOLOGY : ENERGY FLOW IN THE ECOSYSTEM

subject: Biology
q: Organisms living in an estuarine habitat are adapted to
options: survive only in water with low salinity
feed only on phytoplankton and dead organic matter
explanation: Estuarine habitats are where rivers meet the sea and the water can vary from fresh to brackish to salt water. Organisms living in these habitats are adapted to withstand wide fluctuations in salinity. This is because the salinity of estuaries changes with the tides, and organisms living in these habitats need to be able to adapt to these changes.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : ADAPTIVE COLOURATION AND ITS FUNCTIONS

subject: Biology
q: The presence of slit roots, pneumatophores, sunken stomata and salt glands are adaptive features of plants found in the
explanation: The presence of stilt roots, pneumatophores, sunken stomata, and salt glands are adaptive features of plants found in the mangrove swamps. Stilt roots provide support and stability to the plants in the soft muddy soil. Pneumatophores are specialized root structures that stick up out of the soil like snorkels, allowing the plant to breathe even when its main roots are submerged. Sunken stomata and salt glands help the plant to deal with the high salinity of the water in the mangrove swamps. So, the correct answer is option 2.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : STRUCTURAL ADAPTATIONS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q:
Which of the following animals can exist solely on the water they get from food
and metabolic reactions?
explanation: Desert dwellers, such as kangaroo rats, can survive without drinking water. They get the water they need from the food they eat and the metabolic reactions in their bodies. Metabolic water is water created inside a living organism through their metabolism, by oxidizing energy-containing substances in their food.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : ADAPTIVE COLOURATION AND ITS FUNCTIONS

subject: Biology
q: The most likely first colonizers of a bare rock are
explanation: Lichens are the most likely first colonizers of a bare rock. They are pioneer species, which are the first to colonize previously disrupted or damaged ecosystems, beginning a chain of ecological succession that ultimately leads to a more biodiverse steady-state ecosystem. Lichens can survive in harsh conditions and can start the process of soil formation by breaking down the rocks.

topics.title: THE ECOLOGY OF POPULATIONS : ECOLOGICAL SUCCESSION

subject: Biology
q: The carrying capacity of a habitat is reached when the population growth begins to
explanation: The carrying capacity of a habitat is reached when the population growth begins to remain steady. Carrying capacity refers to the maximum number of individuals of a particular species that a particular environment can support. When the carrying capacity is reached, the population size becomes stable, and the growth rate is zero.

topics.title: THE ECOLOGY OF POPULATIONS : FACTORS AFFECTING POPULATION SIZES

subject: Biology
q: The abiotic factors that control human population include
explanation: The abiotic factors that control human population include space and rainfall. Space refers to the physical environment and geographical location where a population resides. Rainfall affects the availability of water, which is essential for human survival and agriculture. Disease and famine, flooding and earthquake, and temperature and disease are biotic factors and natural disasters, not abiotic factors.

topics.title: THE ECOLOGY OF POPULATIONS : FACTORS AFFECTING POPULATION SIZES

subject: Biology
q: An indigenous method of renewing and maintaining soil fertility is by
explanation: Crop rotation is the practice of growing different types of crops in the same area in sequenced seasons to help restore soil nutrients. Shifting cultivation, also known as slash and burn agriculture, is a method where an area of vegetation is cut down and burned before new seeds are sown.

subject: Biology
q: The diseases caused by water-borne pathogens include
explanation: Both typhoid and cholera are diseases caused by bacteria that can be transmitted through contaminated water.

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer the question that follows.


The graph illustrates
options: the highest frequency for height of 2 metres
explanation: The graph illustrates a continuously varying character.

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer the question that follows.

The largest number of cassava plants has an approximate height of
explanation: The largest number of cassava plants has an approximate height of 1.6m(from the diagram).

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following is true in blood transfusion?
options: A person of blood group AB can donate blood only to another person of blood group AB
Person of blood groups A and B can donate or receive blood from each other
A person of blood group AB can receive blood only persons of blood group A or B
A person of blood group O can donate only to a person of blood group O
explanation: A person of blood group AB is a universal recipient, meaning they can receive blood from any group (A, B, AB, or O), but they can only donate blood to individuals of the same group AB. This is because AB blood group individuals have both A and B antigens on their red blood cells, but their plasma does not contain any antibodies against A or B blood groups. Therefore, they can receive blood from any group without causing an immune response.

subject: Biology
q:
A yellow maize is planted and all the fruits obtained are of yellow seeds. When they are cross-bred, yellow seeds and white seeds are obtained in a ratio
3:1. The yellow seed is said to be
explanation: In Mendelian genetics, a dominant trait is one that is phenotypically expressed in heterozygotes. In the given scenario, the yellow seed color is dominant because it appears in the F1 generation after cross-breeding. The 3:1 ratio of yellow to white seeds in the F2 generation is a classic Mendelian ratio, indicating that yellow is the dominant trait and white is the recessive trait.

topics.title: HEREDITY : INHERITANCE OF CHARACTERS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q:
When a colour-blind man marries a carrier woman. What is the probability
of their offspring being colour blind?
explanation: Colour blindness is a sex-linked recessive trait. This means that a male offspring only needs to inherit one copy of the colour-blind gene from his mother to be colour blind. If a colour-blind man (XcY) marries a carrier woman (XXc), there is a 50% chance that a male offspring will inherit the colour-blind gene from his mother and be colour blind (XcY). The other 50% of the time, the male offspring will inherit the normal gene from his mother and will not be colour blind (XY).

topics.title: HEREDITY : PROBABILITY IN GENETICS AND SEX DETERMINATION

subject: Biology
q: The correct base pairing for DNA is
explanation: In DNA, adenine (A) pairs with thymine (T) and guanine (G) pairs with cytosine (C). This is known as complementary base pairing and is a fundamental principle in the structure of DNA.

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer the question that follows.


The type of interaction shown is referred to as
explanation: Interspecific competition occurs between individuals of different species.

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer the question that follows.

Which of the following statements is true of the interaction?
options: P. aurelia is better adapted for obtaining food than P.caudatum.
P. caudatum is better adapted for obtaining food than P. aurelia.
explanation: P. aurelia is better adapted for obtaining food than P.caudatum.

subject: Biology
q: The short thick beak in birds is an adaptation for
explanation: Birds with short, thick beaks are typically adapted for crushing seeds. The shape and strength of the beak allow the bird to break open hard seed shells.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : STRUCTURAL ADAPTIONS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: The basking of agama lizards in the sun is to
options: raise their body temperature to become active
explanation: The basking of agama lizards in the sun is to raise their body temperature to become active. Agama lizards are ectothermic animals, which means they rely on external sources of heat to regulate their body temperature. Basking in the sun allows them to absorb heat and increase their body temperature, which enhances their metabolic activities and makes them more active.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : BEHAVIOURAL ADAPTATIONS IN SOCIAL ANIMALS

subject: Biology
q: The significance of a very large number of termites involved in nuptial swarming is to
options: search for a favourable place to breed
ensure that every individual gets a mate
explanation: The significance of a large number of termites involved in nuptial swarming is to ensure their perpetuation despite predatory pressure. Nuptial swarming is a reproductive strategy used by termites where a large number of winged termites (alates) leave their nest to mate and start new colonies. The large number of termites involved in this process ensures that even if many are eaten by predators, enough will survive to continue the species.

topics.title: ECOLOGY : FACTORS AFFECTING THE DISTRIBUTION OF ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: The use and disuse of body parts and the inheritance of acquired traits were used to explain
explanation: The use and disuse of body parts and the inheritance of acquired traits were used to explain Lamarck's Theory. This theory, also known as Lamarckism, proposed that an organism can change during its lifetime in response to its environment, and those changes are passed on to its offspring. It is named after the French biologist Jean-Baptiste Lamarck, who incorporated the action of soft inheritance into his evolutionary theories.

subject: Biology
q: From his study of Galapagos finches, Darwin derived his Theory of evolution from
explanation: From his study of Galapagos finches, Darwin derived his Theory of evolution from comparative anatomy.

subject: Biology
q: Which subject are you doing?
explanation: The question is asking about the subject being done. Since the questions are from a Biology exam, the correct answer is 'Biology'.

subject: Biology
q: The process in which complex substances are broken down into simpler ones is referred to as
explanation: The process in which complex substances are broken down into simpler ones is referred to as 'catabolism'. This is a type of metabolic process that breaks down molecules into smaller units to release energy. 'Metabolism' is a broad term that includes all chemical reactions that occur within an organism, including both anabolism (building up) and catabolism (breaking down). 'Tropism' is a biological phenomenon indicating growth or turning movement of a biological organism in response to an environmental stimulus. 'Anabolism' is the process in which simpler substances are converted into complex ones.

subject: Biology
q: The organ which is sensitive to light in Euglena is the
explanation: The organ which is sensitive to light in Euglena is the 'eyespot'. The eyespot, or stigma, is a photoreceptive organelle found in the flagellate protists such as Euglena. It allows the cell to sense light direction and intensity and respond by swimming either towards the light (positive phototaxis) or away from it (negative phototaxis). 'Chloroplast' is an organelle within the cells of plants and certain algae that is the site of photosynthesis, which is the process by which light energy is converted to chemical energy, resulting in the production of oxygen and energy-rich organic compounds. 'Gullet' is the passage by which food passes from the mouth to the stomach. 'Flagellum' is a lash-like appendage that protrudes from the cell body of certain bacteria and eukaryotic cells.

topics.title: COORDINATION AND CONTROL : THE SENSE ORGANS

subject: Biology
q: The organelles present in cells that are actively respiring and photosynthesizing are
explanation: Mitochondria and chloroplasts are the organelles present in cells that are actively respiring and photosynthesizing. Mitochondria are the powerhouses of the cell, where respiration occurs to produce energy. Chloroplasts are found in plant cells and are where photosynthesis occurs to convert light energy into chemical energy.

topics.title: LIVING ORGANISMS : CELL STRUCTURE AND FUNCTIONS OF CELL COMPONENTS

subject: Biology
q: Taenia solium can be found in
explanation: Taenia solium, also known as the pork tapeworm, can be found in pigs. Humans can get infected by eating undercooked pork that contains the larvae of the tapeworm.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : BEHAVIOURAL ADAPTATIONS IN SOCIAL ANIMALS

q:

explanation: The clitellum is an organ used for sexual reproduction of some annelids

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : STRUCTURAL ADAPTATIONS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer the question that follows

The organism is found in soils rich in
explanation: The organism is found in soils rich in humus. Earthworms are found in moist soil rich in humus.

topics.title: SOIL : CHARACTERISTICS OF DIFFERENT TYPES OF SOIL

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following describes a characteristic of arthropods?
options: The body is not divided into a number of segments
The organism finds it easy to grow freely
The organism has a pair of jointed appendages
explanation: Arthropods are characterized by having bodies covered by chitin. Chitin is a tough, protective, semitransparent substance, primarily a nitrogen-containing polysaccharide, forming the principal component of arthropod exoskeletons and the cell walls of certain fungi.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following distinguishes a butterfly from a moth?
options: The abdomen of moth is fatter than that of butterfly
The wings of butterfly rest horizontally but those of moth rest vertically
explanation: Butterflies and moths both possess a pair of wings; while butterflies are diurnal insects, most moths are nocturnal. They differ from butterflies as they have fatter and more hairy bodies. The antennae of most moths are pointed at their tips while those of most butterflies have knobs at their tips. At rest, the wings of most moths are spread out horizontally while those of most butterflies are held vertically. The fore and hind wings of a moth are connected to each other while those of a butterfly are not. The pupae of a moth are generally enclosed in well-developed cocoons while those of butterflies are not. Most butterflies are bright-coloured while moths tend to have dull colouring

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following types of feathers is used for flight in birds?
explanation: Birds have different types of feathers, each serving a specific function. The quill or flight feathers are the long, stiff feathers that are used for flight and steering. They are attached to the wing and tail. Covert feathers are small feathers that cover the bases of the larger flight feathers. Down feathers are the soft, fluffy feathers located beneath the outer feathers that provide insulation. Filoplume feathers are hair-like feathers that have sensory functions.

subject: Biology
q: The plants that grow in deserts or very dry areas are referred to as
explanation: Plants that grow in deserts or very dry areas are referred to as xerophytes. Xerophytes have adapted to survive in an environment with little water. They have features such as thick cuticles, reduced leaf areas, sunken stomata and the presence of a large number of stomata on their stems to reduce water loss. Epiphytes are plants that grow on other plants but are not parasitic, such as the numerous ferns, bromeliads, air plants, and orchids growing on tree trunks in rainforests. Mesophytes are plants that require a more or less balanced supply of water. They neither very dry not very wet conditions. Hydrophytes are plants that have adapted to living in aquatic environments.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : ADAPTIVE COLOURATION AND ITS FUNCTIONS

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following is the simplest living organism?
explanation: The simplest living organism is a virus. A virus is a small infectious agent that replicates only inside the living cells of an organism. Viruses can infect all types of life forms, from animals and plants to microorganisms, including bacteria and archaea.

topics.title: LIVING ORGANISMS : CELL STRUCTURE AND FUNCTIONS OF CELL COMPONENTS

subject: Biology
q: Proboscis is a structure that is mostly found in
explanation: A proboscis is an elongated appendage from the head of an animal. The most well-known example of a proboscis in insects is the feeding tube used by nectar-feeding insects such as butterflies and moths.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : STRUCTURAL ADAPTATIONS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: The structural adaptation of desert plants for water conservation is
explanation: Desert plants have adapted to the extremes of heat and aridity by using both physical and behavioral mechanisms. One of the physical mechanisms is having spiny leaves which reduce the surface area that would otherwise be exposed to the hot sun and thus reduce water loss.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : ADAPTIVE COLOURATION AND ITS FUNCTIONS

subject: Biology
q: The long and sharp clawed feet of birds is an adaptation for
explanation: The long and sharp clawed feet of birds are an adaptation for grasping prey. This is especially true for birds of prey, such as hawks and eagles, which use their powerful talons to catch and hold onto their prey. The claws are not primarily used for tearing flesh, crushing seeds, or scooping mud.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : STRUCTURAL ADAPTATIONS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: During the manufacture of food by plants, which of the following organisms use energy from the sun?
explanation: Anabaena is a type of cyanobacteria that uses energy from the sun to manufacture food through the process of photosynthesis. Cyanobacteria are a group of photosynthetic bacteria, some of which are nitrogen-fixing, that live in a wide variety of moist soils and water either freely or in a symbiotic relationship with plants or lichen.

subject: Biology
q: Movement of minerals and chemical compounds within a plant occurs during
explanation: Movement of minerals and chemical compounds within a plant occurs during translocation. Translocation is the process by which organic substances move through the phloem of a plant. This process allows the plant to distribute the nutrients it produces during photosynthesis to all parts of the plant. Transpiration, diffusion, and osmosis are all involved in the movement of water and other substances in plants, but they do not specifically involve the movement of minerals and chemical compounds.

subject: Biology
q: The enzyme that is present in the saliva is
explanation: The enzyme present in saliva is Ptyalin, also known as salivary amylase. It begins the process of digestion of food by breaking down starch into maltose, a smaller carbohydrate.

subject: Biology
q: Plants that have special devices for trapping and digesting insects are
explanation: Carnivorous plants have special devices for trapping and digesting insects. They live in environments where the soil is low in nutrients, especially nitrogen, such as acidic bogs and rock outcroppings. Examples of carnivorous plants include the Venus flytrap, pitcher plant, and sundew.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : STRUCTURAL ADAPTATIONS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: The process of transforming the chemical energy of cellular fuels into the high energy bonds of ATP in plants is
explanation: The process of transforming the chemical energy of cellular fuels into the high energy bonds of ATP in plants is Respiration. ATP (Adenosine triphosphate) is a complex organic chemical that provides energy to drive many processes in living cells, such as muscle contraction, nerve impulse propagation, and chemical synthesis. Respiration is the process by which cells obtain energy from glucose. During respiration, cells break down simple food molecules, such as sugar, and release the energy they contain.

subject: Biology
q: Fungi are referred to as heterotrophs because they
explanation: Fungi are referred to as heterotrophs because they lack chlorophyll. Heterotrophs are organisms that cannot manufacture their own food and instead obtain their food from other organisms. Because fungi lack chlorophyll, they cannot perform photosynthesis and therefore must obtain nutrients from other sources.

subject: Biology
q: An example of a parasitic protozoan is
explanation: Plasmodium is an example of a parasitic protozoan. It is the causative agent of malaria, a disease transmitted to humans through the bite of an infected mosquito. The other options, Euglena, Chlamydomonas, and Paramecium, are not parasitic protozoans.

subject: Biology
q: Which blood cells are involved in the immune response of vertebrates?
explanation: Lymphocytes are the blood cells involved in the immune response of vertebrates. They are a type of white blood cell that is part of the immune system. There are two main types of lymphocytes: B cells, which produce antibodies that are used to attack invading bacteria, viruses, and toxins; and T cells, which attack the body's own cells that have been taken over by viruses or become cancerous.

subject: Biology
q: The blood circulatory system of vertebrates consists of
explanation: The blood circulatory system of vertebrates consists of the heart, arteries, capillaries, and veins. The heart pumps blood, arteries carry blood away from the heart, capillaries are where the exchange of nutrients and waste occurs, and veins carry blood back to the heart.

subject: Biology
q: A plant tissue that carries water and mineral salts is the
explanation: Xylem is a type of tissue in vascular plants that transports water and dissolved minerals from the roots to the rest of the plant. It is also involved in replacing water lost during transpiration and photosynthesis.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following helps in the clotting of blood?
explanation: Platelets, also called thrombocytes, are blood cells that cause blood clots and other necessary growth healing functions. Platelets are the smallest of our blood cells, with a diameter of 2–3 µm and no nucleus. Their main function is to contribute to hemostasis: the process of stopping bleeding at the site of interrupted endothelium.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following forms about 55% of the volumes of the blood in man?
explanation: Plasma makes up about 55% of the blood's volume. Blood plasma is the liquid component of blood, in which the blood cells are suspended. It is a yellowish liquid that carries nutrients, hormones, and proteins throughout the body.

subject: Biology
q: The part of the mammalian skin involved in excretion is the
explanation: The sweat gland in the mammalian skin is involved in excretion. It helps to regulate body temperature by secreting sweat, which evaporates and cools the body. It also helps to excrete waste products such as urea and salts.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following is a waste product of an insect?
explanation: Uric acid is a waste product of insects. Insects, like many other animals, produce waste products as a result of their metabolic processes. One of these waste products is uric acid, which is excreted from the body.

subject: Biology
q: The main structure in vertebrates that supports and protects the body is the
explanation: The skeleton is the main structure in vertebrates that supports and protects the body. It provides a framework that supports the body and allows for movement. It also protects the internal organs from injury.

topics.title: SUPPORT AND MOVEMENT : TYPES AND FUNCTIONS OF THE SKELETON

subject: Biology
q: The chitin in the exoskeleton of many arthropods is strengthened by
explanation: The chitin in the exoskeleton of many arthropods is strengthened by calcium compounds. Chitin is a complex carbohydrate that provides rigidity and strength to the exoskeleton, and the addition of calcium compounds further hardens and strengthens this structure.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : STRUCTURAL ADAPTATIONS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: The transfer of pollen grains from the anther to a receptive stigma is
explanation: The transfer of pollen grains from the anther to a receptive stigma is called pollination. This is a crucial step in the reproductive process of flowering plants, allowing for the fertilization of the ovule and the production of seeds.

subject: Biology
q: The male reproductive organ of a flower is the
explanation: The stamen is the male reproductive organ of a flower. It consists of an anther, where pollen is produced, and a filament, which supports the anther.

subject: Biology
q: The gland that is found just below the hypothalamus is the
explanation: The pituitary gland is a small pea-sized gland that plays a major role in regulating vital body functions and general wellbeing. It is referred to as the body's 'master gland' because it controls the activity of most other hormone-secreting glands.

subject: Biology
q: The most important plant hormone is
explanation: Auxins are a class of plant hormones (or plant growth regulators) with some morphogen-like characteristics. Auxins have a cardinal role in coordination of many growth and behavioral processes in the plant's life cycle and are essential for plant body development.

subject: Biology
q: The sensory cell that responds to dim light is referred to as the
explanation: The sensory cells in the retina of the eye that are responsible for vision in dim light are called rods. They are more sensitive to light than cones, the other type of photoreceptor, which are responsible for color vision and detail.

topics.title: COORDINATION AND CONTROL : THE SENSE ORGANS

subject: Biology
q: The absence of anti-diuretic hormone in humans results in
explanation: The anti-diuretic hormone (ADH) helps the kidneys manage the amount of water in your body. The absence of ADH causes a condition called diabetes insipidus, which causes frequent urination, leading to increased dehydration.

subject: Biology
q: Oestrogen is a hormone that is synthesized in the
explanation: Oestrogen is a hormone that is synthesized in the ovaries. It is responsible for the development and regulation of the female reproductive system and secondary sex characteristics.

subject: Biology
q: The eye defect caused by the development of cloudy areas in the lenses is
explanation: Cataract is an eye condition that occurs when the lens of the eye becomes cloudy, leading to blurry or less colorful vision. It is caused by the clumping of proteins in the eye's lens. Astigmatism, Presbyopia, and Glaucoma are other eye conditions but they are not caused by the development of cloudy areas in the lenses.

topics.title: COORDINATION AND CONTROL : THE SENSE ORGANS

subject: Biology
q: A pollutant that is biodegradable is
explanation: Biodegradable pollutants are substances that can be broken down by natural processes, such as the action of bacteria. Sewage is an example of a biodegradable pollutant because it can be broken down by bacteria and other microorganisms. Cellophane, Crude oil, and Heavy metals are not easily broken down by natural processes, hence they are not biodegradable.

topics.title: HUMANS AND ENVIRONMENT : POLLUTION AND ITS CONTROL

subject: Biology
q: A tropical disease caused by Trypanosoma is
explanation: Sleeping sickness, also known as African trypanosomiasis, is a tropical disease caused by the Trypanosoma parasite. This parasite is transmitted to humans through the bite of a tsetse fly. Yellow fever, Malaria, and River blindness are other tropical diseases but they are not caused by Trypanosoma.

subject: Biology
q: The solid part of the ecosystem is referred to as
explanation: The solid part of the ecosystem is referred to as the Lithosphere. The lithosphere is the rigid, outermost shell of a terrestrial-type planet or natural satellite that is defined by its rigid mechanical properties. It encompasses the crust and the portion of the upper mantle that behaves elastically on time scales of thousands of years or greater.

topics.title: ECOLOGY : ENERGY FLOW IN THE ECOSYSTEM

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following is caused by Treponema palladium ?
explanation: Syphilis is caused by the bacterium Treponema pallidum . This bacterium is a spirochete, a class of long, slender bacteria that have a distinctive helical shape.

subject: Biology
q: To which blood group do universal recipients belong?
explanation: Universal recipients belong to the AB blood group. This is because they have both A and B antigens on the surface of their red blood cells, and their blood serum does not contain any antibodies against these antigens. Therefore, they can receive blood from donors of any blood type: A, B, AB, or O.

topics.title: HEREDITY : INHERITANCE OF CHARACTERS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: The clumping together of red blood cells is
explanation: The clumping together of red blood cells is known as Agglutination. This usually occurs when there is a reaction between an antigen and its corresponding antibody.

subject: Biology
q: Physiological adaptation to very dry condition in animals demonstrates
explanation: Physiological adaptation to very dry conditions in animals demonstrates Aestivation. Aestivation, also known as estivation, is a state of animal dormancy, similar to hibernation, characterized by inactivity and a lowered metabolic rate, that is entered in response to high temperatures and arid conditions.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : BEHAVIOURAL ADAPTATIONS IN SOCIAL ANIMALS

subject: Biology
q: One adaptation of Cactus opuntia to conserve water is the reduction of
explanation: One adaptation of Cactus opuntia to conserve water is the reduction of Leaves to spine. This is because the leaves of cacti are modified into spines, which are less likely to lose water than regular leaves. The spines also provide shade and protect the cactus from animals.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : ADAPTIVE COLOURATION AND ITS FUNCTIONS

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following structures is adapted for feeding in a bird of prey?
explanation: Birds of prey, also known as raptors, have a hooked beak and sharp claws, which are also known as talons. These adaptations help them to catch, kill and eat their prey. The hooked beak is used to tear the flesh of the prey, while the sharp claws are used to catch and hold the prey. It illustrates the beak and claw adaption of some bird of prey

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : STRUCTURAL ADAPTATIONS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: The special pigment for colour change in chameleon is
explanation: Chromatophores are special pigment cells that are found in a wide range of animals including reptiles, fish, and cephalopods. They are responsible for skin color changes in animals such as chameleons. These cells can change the color of their skin by adjusting the positions of pigments within them, allowing the animal to blend in with its environment, communicate with others, or display emotions.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : ADAPTIVE COLOURATION AND ITS FUNCTIONS

subject: Biology
q: The behavioural adaptation in social insects could best be described as
explanation: The behavioural adaptation in social insects like ants, bees, wasps, and termites is best described as symbiosis. Symbiosis is a close and long-term biological interaction between two different biological organisms. In the context of social insects, this refers to the cooperative and mutually beneficial relationship within a colony, where different members perform different roles (like workers, soldiers, and reproducers) for the benefit of the whole colony.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : BEHAVIOURAL ADAPTATIONS IN SOCIAL ANIMALS

subject: Biology
q: The lowest level of organization in living organisms is
explanation: The lowest level of organization in living organisms is the cell. This is a basic concept in biology. Organisms are made up of cells, which form tissues, tissues form organs, and organs form systems.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following is the most complex according to their cellular level of organization?
explanation: The heart is the most complex according to their cellular level of organization. The heart is an organ, which is made up of tissues, and tissues are made up of cells. The other options, Hair (made up of cells), Euglena (a single-celled organism), and Hydra (a multicellular organism but less complex than an organ like the heart), are less complex in their cellular organization.

explanation: II and III are the hooks and suckers with it use to attach firmly to the intestinal wall of its host.

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer the question that follows.

The young proglottid is represented by
explanation: The young proglottid is represented by IV. A young proglottid in a tapeworm is a small, developing segment located near the neck of the worm. It has immature reproductive organs and is characterized by high cellular activity and growth. As it matures, it moves toward the posterior end, eventually developing fully functional reproductive organs and becoming gravid with eggs.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following organisms is multicellular?
explanation: Spirogyra is multicellular while amoeba, euglena and chlamydomonas are unicellular.

subject: Biology
q: In bryophytes, sex organs are produced in the
explanation: In bryophytes, sex organs are produced in the gametophyte. Bryophytes are non-vascular plants, such as mosses and liverworts. They have a life cycle that alternates between a haploid gametophyte stage and a diploid sporophyte stage. The gametophyte stage is dominant and is the stage during which the sex organs, the archegonia (female) and antheridia (male), are produced.

subject: Biology
q: Seed plants are the most dominant vegetation on land because of
explanation: Seed plants are the most dominant vegetation on land because of their efficient seed dispersal. Seed plants, which include gymnosperms and angiosperms, have developed a variety of mechanisms for seed dispersal, such as wind, water, and animals. This allows them to colonize a wide range of habitats. While the ability to photosynthesize, availability of water, and motile gametes are important for plant life, they are not the primary reasons why seed plants are the most dominant vegetation on land.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : STRUCTURAL ADAPTATIONS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following is an aboreal organism?
explanation: An arboreal organism is an organism that lives in or among trees. Birds are a perfect example of arboreal organisms as they spend most of their time in trees.

topics.title: NATURAL HABITAT : AQUATIC TERRESTRIAL AND ARBOREAL

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer the question that follows.


The part labelled I is the
explanation: The part labelled I in the diagram is the root hair.

topics.title: INTERNAL STRUCTURE OF A FLOWERING PLANT : ROOT

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer the question that follows.

The diagram is the transverse section of a
explanation:
Dicotyledonous root. Dicot Root:
- Central star-shaped xylem.
- Phloem between xylem arms.
- No central pith.
- Endodermis and pericycle clearly defined.
Monocot Root:
- Ring of vascular bundles.
- Central pith present.
- Endodermis and pericycle surrounding the ring of bundles.

topics.title: INTERNAL STRUCTURE OF A FLOWERING PLANT : STEM

subject: Biology
q:
i2/1, c0/0, pm3/2, m3/3
The dental formula above represents that of
explanation: The dental formula is that of a herbivore.it is due to the absence of canine

subject: Biology
q: A circulatory system is very essential in mammals but not in smaller organisms like Amoeba because
options: diffusion is sufficient to transport materials in Amoeba
explanation: In smaller organisms like Amoeba, diffusion is sufficient to transport materials. This is because they are single-celled organisms and the distance over which substances need to be transported is small. Therefore, a circulatory system is not necessary. In contrast, mammals are multicellular organisms with specialized cells and tissues, so a circulatory system is needed to transport substances to and from these cells.

subject: Biology
q: In vascular plants, the sieve tubes and companion cells are present in the
explanation: In vascular plants, the sieve tubes and companion cells are present in the phloem. The phloem is a type of tissue in vascular plants that transports nutrients, such as sugars, from the leaves (where they are produced via photosynthesis) to other parts of the plant. Sieve tubes and companion cells are two types of cells that make up the phloem.

subject: Biology
q: The stomata of leaves are similar in function to the
explanation: The stomata of leaves are similar in function to the spiracles of insects. Both stomata and spiracles are involved in gas exchange. Stomata, found on the surface of leaves, allow for the exchange of gases (carbon dioxide and oxygen) between the plant and the atmosphere. Similarly, spiracles, found in insects, allow for the exchange of gases (oxygen and carbon dioxide) between the insect and the atmosphere.

topics.title: INTERNAL STRUCTURE OF A FLOWERING PLANT : LEAF

subject: Biology
q: The use of moist skin for respiration in amphibians is known as
explanation: The use of moist skin for respiration in amphibians is known as cutaneous respiration. Cutaneous respiration is a form of respiration where gas exchange occurs across the skin or outer integument of an organism rather than gills or lungs. Amphibians, like frogs, often use this method of respiration, in addition to their lungs, to obtain oxygen.

topics.title: RESPIRATION : THE MECHANISM OF GASEOUS EXCHANGE IN PLANTS AND MAMMALS

subject: Biology
q: Water in plants is removed as water vapour through the process of
explanation:
Water in plants is removed as water vapour through the process of transpiration.
Transpiration is the process by which moisture is carried through plants from roots to small pores on the underside of leaves, where it changes to vapor and is released to the atmosphere. Transpiration is essentially evaporation of water from plant leaves

subject: Biology
q: An example of an organ of perennation in plants is
explanation: An organ of perennation in plants is a structure that allows a plant to survive an unfavorable season or period (such as winter or drought) and grow again when conditions improve. A rhizome, which is an underground stem, is an example of an organ of perennation. It can store food and water, and produce new shoots to allow the plant to grow again when conditions are favorable.

topics.title: GROWTH : GERMINATION OF SEEDS AND CONDITION NECESSARY FOR GERMINATION OF SEEDS

subject: Biology
q: Alternation of generation is a feature shown in
explanation: Alternation of generations is a feature shown in mosses. This is a type of life cycle found in terrestrial plants and some algae in which subsequent generations of individuals alternate between haploid and diploid organisms. In mosses, the haploid gametophyte is the dominant generation, while the diploid sporophyte is nutritionally dependent on the gametophyte.

subject: Biology
q:
I. Growth is mainly apical
II. Growth is specific with definite shape

III. Growth is throughout life
Which of the above correctly describes the growth pattern in plants?
explanation: The growth pattern in plants is characterized two statements. I. Growth is mainly apical: In plants, growth is primarily concentrated at the tips of roots and shoots. This is known as apical growth. III. Growth is throughout life: Unlike animals, plants continue to grow throughout their life. This is due to the presence of meristematic tissue in their tips.

subject: Biology
q: Coordination and regulation of body activities in mammals are achieved by the
explanation: Coordination and regulation of body activities in mammals are achieved by both the nervous system (nerves) and the endocrine system (hormones). The nervous system provides fast, short-term coordination through electrical signals (nerve impulses), while the endocrine system provides slower, long-term coordination through chemicals (hormones) released into the bloodstream.

topics.title: COORDINATION AND CONTROL : HORMONAL CONTROL~~COORDINATION AND CONTROL : NERVOUS COORDINATION

subject: Biology
q: The cerebellum of the brain controls
explanation: The cerebellum, a part of the brain, is primarily responsible for regulating and coordinating muscular activity. It plays a crucial role in maintaining posture and balance, ensuring that movements are smooth and coordinated.

subject: Biology
q: The part of the brain responsible for peristalsis is the
explanation: The medulla oblongata is a part of the brain that is responsible for regulating several basic functions of the autonomic nervous system, including peristalsis. Peristalsis is the involuntary constriction and relaxation of the muscles of the intestine or another canal, creating wave-like movements that push the contents of the canal forward.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following instruments is used for measuring atmospheric pressure?
explanation: A barometer is an instrument used to measure atmospheric pressure. It can measure the pressure exerted by the atmosphere by balancing it against the force of a column of liquid (usually mercury). The height of the column of liquid is a measure of the atmospheric pressure.

subject: Biology
q: The influence of soil on organisms in a habitat is referred to as
explanation: The influence of soil on organisms in a habitat is referred to as edaphic. Edaphic refers to soil-related factors, including its physical, chemical, and biological properties. These factors can influence the types of plants that can grow in a particular habitat, which in turn affects the types of animals that can live there.

topics.title: ECOLOGY : FACTORS AFFECTING THE DISTRIBUTION OF ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: The genetic make-up of an organism is described as
explanation: The genetic make-up of an organism is described as its genotype. The genotype is the part of the genetic makeup of a cell, and therefore of any individual, which determines one of its characteristics (phenotype). It is one of its genes or a set of genes that is carried by an individual. It does not refer to the entire genetic makeup of an individual, which would include all of its genes, its chromosomes, or its alleles.

subject: Biology
q: The major limiting factor of productivity in the aquatic habitat include the following except?
explanation:
Water is not a limiting factor in aquatic habitats, as it is the medium in which aquatic organisms live. In fact, water is a necessary component of aquatic habitats, and its absence would make life impossible.

The other options are all limiting factors in aquatic habitats:

Food: The availability of food can limit the growth and survival of aquatic organisms.

Temperature: Temperature affects the metabolism and survival of aquatic organisms, and extreme temperatures can be limiting.

Sunlight: Sunlight is essential for photosynthesis, and its availability can limit the growth and survival of aquatic plants and algae.

Therefore, the correct answer is water

topics.title: ECOLOGY : FACTORS AFFECTING THE DISTRIBUTION OF ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following group of organisms feeds directly on green plants?
explanation: Primary consumers are the group of organisms that feed directly on green plants. In the food chain, primary consumers are typically herbivores that eat plants and other producers. Secondary consumers, on the other hand, typically eat primary consumers, while decomposers break down dead or decaying organisms. Producers, such as green plants, produce their own food through photosynthesis.

topics.title: ECOLOGY : FOOD CHAINS FOOD WEBS AND TROPHIC LEVELS

subject: Biology
q: A characteristic feature of tropical rainforest is that it
options: has total annual rainfall of less than 50cm
explanation: Tropical rainforests are known for their biodiversity, which means they contain a large number of different plant species. This is due to the warm, wet climate that allows for a wide variety of plants to grow.

subject: Biology
q: The study of how and why population size change over time is
explanation: Population dynamics is the branch of life sciences that studies the size and age composition of populations as dynamic systems, and the biological and environmental processes driving them (such as birth and death rates, and by immigration and emigration).

topics.title: THE ECOLOGY OF POPULATIONS : FACTORS AFFECTING POPULATION SIZES

subject: Biology
q: A severe and long dry season is a characteristic feature of
explanation: The Sahel savanna is known for its long, severe dry seasons. The Sahel is a transitional ecoregion of semi-arid grasslands, savannas, steppes, and thorn shrublands lying between the wooded Sudan savanna to the south and the Sahara desert to the north.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following is a nitrogen-fixing blue-green algae of soil?
explanation: Anabaena is a genus of filamentous cyanobacteria, or blue-green algae, that is known for its nitrogen-fixing abilities. Nitrogen fixation is a process by which nitrogen in the atmosphere is converted into a form that can be used by plants. Nitrosomonas, Clostridium, and Rhizobium, while they are all involved in the nitrogen cycle, they are not blue-green algae.

subject: Biology
q: The soil with the highest water-retaining capacity is
explanation: Clayey soil has the highest water-retaining capacity among the options given. This is due to its fine texture and compactness, which allows it to hold water better than sandy, loamy, or stoney soils. Sandy soil has the least water-retaining capacity because of its coarse texture and large particles, which allow water to drain quickly. Loamy soil has a balance of sand, silt, and clay, and while it retains water well, it does not retain as much as clayey soil. Stoney soil, as the name suggests, contains a lot of stones, which do not retain water well.

topics.title: SOIL : CHARACTERISTICS OF DIFFERENT TYPES OF SOIL

subject: Biology
q: The causative agent of poliomyelitis is
explanation: Poliomyelitis, also known as polio, is caused by the poliovirus. A virus is a small infectious agent that can only replicate inside the living cells of an organism. Fungi, protozoans, and bacteria are all different types of microorganisms, but they do not cause poliomyelitis.

subject: Biology
q: One of the ways of controlling noise pollution in urban areas is
options: that fuel should be completely combusted by engines
by planting trees on both sides of the road
by siting industries away from residential areas
explanation: Noise pollution in urban areas can be controlled by siting industries away from residential areas. This is because industries are a major source of noise pollution due to the heavy machinery and processes they use. By siting them away from residential areas, the impact of this noise on people's daily lives can be reduced.

topics.title: HUMANS AND ENVIRONMENT : POLLUTION AND ITS CONTROL

subject: Biology
q: A constituent of the exhaust fumes from electricity generating sets which causes serious air pollution is
explanation: Carbon (II) oxide, also known as carbon monoxide, is a major pollutant that is released from the exhaust fumes of electricity generating sets. It is a colorless, odorless, and poisonous gas that is produced from the incomplete combustion of carbon-containing fuels. When inhaled, it can interfere with the blood's ability to carry oxygen, leading to serious health effects.

topics.title: HUMANS AND ENVIRONMENT : POLLUTION AND ITS CONTROL

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following is true of small pox?
options: It can effectively he controlled with antibiotics
It can effectively be controlled by vaccination
explanation: Smallpox can effectively be controlled by vaccination. Smallpox is a viral disease, and antibiotics, which are used to treat bacterial infections, are ineffective against it. Vaccination works by stimulating the body's immune system to recognize and fight the smallpox virus, thereby preventing the disease.

subject: Biology
q: A pollutant that is mostly associated with acid rain is
explanation: Acid rain is primarily caused by emissions of sulfur dioxide and nitrogen oxide from various human activities such as industrial processes and vehicle exhaust. These gases react in the atmosphere with water, oxygen, and other chemicals to form various acidic compounds. Nitrogen(IV)oxide is one of these gases, hence it is associated with acid rain.

topics.title: HUMANS AND ENVIRONMENT : POLLUTION AND ITS CONTROL

subject: Biology
q: When the adults have reached a certain degree of weakness, the process of binary fission is replaced by conjugation in
explanation: Conjugation is a form of sexual reproduction in unicellular organisms. In Paramecium, when the adults have reached a certain degree of weakness, the process of binary fission (asexual reproduction) is replaced by conjugation. During conjugation, two paramecia come together and exchange genetic material, then separate and divide to form new individuals.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : STRUCTURAL ADAPTATIONS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: Whorls, arches, loops and compounds are types of variation in
explanation: Whorls, arches, loops and compounds are types of patterns found in fingerprints. These patterns are unique to each individual and are used for identification purposes. The study of these patterns is called dermatoglyphics.

subject: Biology
q: A couple has 10 children, all female. Which of the following best explains the situation?
options: The woman is not capable of producing male children
The sex determination was by the man's Y chromosome
The sex determination was by the man's X chromosome
explanation: The sex of a child is determined by the type of sperm that fertilizes the woman's egg. A sperm can either carry an X or a Y chromosome. If the sperm carrying an X chromosome fertilizes the egg, the child will be female (XX). If the sperm carrying a Y chromosome fertilizes the egg, the child will be male (XY). Therefore, if all the children are female, it means that all the fertilizing sperms carried X chromosomes.

subject: Biology
q: A biological agent with antiviral property is
explanation: Interferons are proteins produced by the body's immune system in response to viruses. They have antiviral properties and work by interfering with the replication of viruses. Enzymes, antibiotics, and disinfectants do not have antiviral properties. Antibiotics are used to treat bacterial infections, not viral infections. Disinfectants are used to kill or inactivate microorganisms on inanimate objects and surfaces.

subject: Biology
q: One of the advantages of outbreeding is
explanation: Outbreeding, or outcrossing, is the practice of introducing unrelated genetic material into a breeding line. This has the advantage of increasing genetic diversity, which can lead to greater disease resistance. Disease resistance is a desirable trait because it can increase survival and reproduction rates. Fast growth, tall height, and pests tolerance are not necessarily advantages of outbreeding.

topics.title: HEREDITY : APPLICATION OF THE PRINCIPLES OF HEREDITY

subject: Biology
q: An individual with blood group AB can receive blood from those in blood group(s)
explanation: An individual with blood group AB is a universal recipient. This means they can receive blood from individuals of any blood group: A, B, AB, or O. This is because their blood does not have any antibodies against A, B or O antigens.

subject: Biology
q: The stream-lined shape of fishes is an adaptation for
explanation: The streamlined shape of fishes is an adaptation for easy movement. This shape reduces the resistance of water, allowing the fish to move quickly and efficiently through their aquatic environment.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : ADAPTIVE COLOURATION AND ITS FUNCTIONS

subject: Biology
q: An example of a poikilothermic organism is a
explanation: A poikilothermic organism, also known as a cold-blooded organism, is one that does not have an internal mechanism to regulate its body temperature. Instead, its body temperature changes with the environment. Examples of poikilothermic organisms include reptiles, amphibians, and fish. Among the options given, the lizard is a poikilothermic organism.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : BEHAVIOURAL ADAPTATIONS IN SOCIAL ANIMALS

subject: Biology
q: All living organisms are constantly involved in a struggle for existence. This was proposed by
explanation: The concept of 'struggle for existence' was proposed by Charles Darwin. It is a part of his theory of natural selection, where he proposed that all living organisms are constantly involved in a struggle for existence, and only the fittest survive this struggle.

subject: Biology
q: Adaptive radiation is illustrated in
explanation: Adaptive radiation is illustrated in wings in modified insect mouthparts. Adaptive radiation is an evolutionary process where organisms diversify rapidly into a multitude of new forms, particularly when a change in the environment makes new resources available, creates new challenges, or opens new environmental niches.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following has the most primitive respiratory system?
explanation: Among the given options, snails have the most primitive respiratory system. Snails breathe through a pallial lung, which is a type of lung that is developed for breathing air. This is a primitive form of respiration compared to the more complex respiratory systems of insects, fish, and mice.

subject: Biology
q: One adaptation shown by hydrophytes in fresh water habitats is the
options: poor development of roots and xylem tissues
explanation: Hydrophytes are plants that have adapted to living in aquatic environments. They have poorly developed root xylem tissues because they do not need to absorb water from the soil like terrestrial plants. Instead, they absorb water directly from their surroundings.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : STRUCTURAL ADAPTATIONS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following uses diffusion as the principal method of gaseous exchange?
explanation: Earthworms use diffusion as the principal method of gaseous exchange. They do not have specialized respiratory organs like lungs or gills. Instead, gases (oxygen and carbon dioxide) are exchanged across the moist body surface by diffusion.

subject: Biology
q: The theory which supports the view that the large muscles developed by an athlete will be passed on to the offspring was proposed by
explanation: The theory that the large muscles developed by an athlete will be passed on to the offspring was proposed by Lamarck. This is known as Lamarckism or Lamarckian inheritance, which suggests that an organism can change during its lifetime in response to its environment, and those changes are passed onto its offspring.

subject: Biology
q: The chromosomes of members of the kingdom Monera are within the
explanation: Members of the kingdom Monera, which includes bacteria, do not have a true nucleus. Instead, their genetic material (chromosomes) is located within the cytoplasm. This is a key characteristic of prokaryotic organisms, which Monera are.

subject: Biology
q: The mangrove swamp in Nigeria is restricted to the
explanation: The mangrove swamp in Nigeria is found in the coastal region, which is part of the Tropical rainforest. Mangroves are trees and shrubs that grow in saline coastal habitats in the tropics and subtropics.

subject: Biology
q: The pancreas secretes enzymes for the digestion of
explanation: The pancreas secretes enzymes that aid in the digestion of fats, proteins, and carbohydrates. These enzymes include lipase (for fats), protease (for proteins), and amylase (for carbohydrates).

subject: Biology
q: The causative agent of bird flu is a
explanation: Bird flu, also known as avian influenza, is caused by viruses. Specifically, it is caused by Influenza type A viruses, which can infect humans, birds, and other animals.

subject: Biology
q: A water medium is necessary for fertilization is
explanation: Ferns are a type of plant that require a water medium for fertilization. This is because their sperm cells are flagellated and need to swim through water to reach the egg cells for fertilization.

subject: Biology
q: An example of a sex-linked trait is the
options: ability to grow long hair in females
explanation: Hemophilia is an example of a sex-linked trait. Sex-linked traits are associated with genes found on sex chromosomes. In humans, the term generally refers to traits that are influenced by genes on the X chromosome. This is because the X chromosome is large and contains many more genes than the smaller Y chromosome. Hemophilia is a sex-linked recessive disorder that is found on the X chromosome.

subject: Biology
q: In which of the following Nigerian states can montane vegetation be found
explanation: Montane vegetation is found in high altitude areas, typically mountains. In Nigeria, the Plateau state is known for its highlands, hence montane vegetation can be found there.

topics.title: LOCAL(NIGERIAN) BIOMES : HIGHLANDS OF MONTANE FORESTS AND GRASSLANDS

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following is true of cloning?
options: It is welcomed as an ethically and morally sound science
It involves the asexual multiplication of the tissues of the original organism
The clone is similar to but not exactly like the original organism
Only one cell of the original organism is needed to initiate the process
explanation: The clone is similar to but not exactly like the original organism

subject: Biology
q: The process of shedding the exoskeleton of an arthropod is known as
explanation: Ecdysis is the process of shedding the old skin (in reptiles) or casting off the old exoskeleton (in insects and other arthropods) to enable growth. The term is derived from the Greek 'ekdysis', meaning 'getting out'.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : STRUCTURAL ADAPTATIONS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following is a major cause of constipation in humans?
explanation: Constipation is often caused by a lack of dietary fiber, also known as roughage. Fiber adds bulk to the stool, which helps it move through the intestines more easily. Without enough fiber, stools can become hard and difficult to pass, leading to constipation. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Lack of roughage'.

subject: Biology
q: In mammals, the organ directly on top of the kidney is the
explanation: In mammals, the adrenal glands are located at the top of each kidney. The adrenal glands are part of the endocrine system and produce hormones that help regulate your metabolism, immune system, blood pressure, response to stress, and other essential functions. Therefore, the correct answer is 'adrenal gland'.

topics.title: INTERNAL STRUCTURE OF A MAMMAL : MAMMALIAN INTERNAL ORGANS

subject: Biology
q: An accurate identification of a rapist can be carried out by conducting a
explanation: DNA analysis is the most accurate method for identifying a rapist. This is because each person's DNA is unique (except for identical twins). Therefore, if DNA from a crime scene (such as semen, blood, or hair) matches the DNA of a suspect, it is very strong evidence that the suspect committed the crime. Other tests, like RNA analysis, blood group test, and behavioural traits test, are not as accurate or conclusive. Therefore, the correct answer is 'DNA analysis'.

topics.title: HEREDITY : APPLICATION OF THE PRINCIPLES OF HEREDITY

subject: Biology
q: An example of a fish that aestivates is
explanation: Aestivation is a state of animal dormancy, similar to hibernation, characterized by inactivity and a lowered metabolic rate, that is entered in response to high temperatures and arid conditions. Lung fish is known to aestivate during dry periods, by burrowing into the mud and secreting a mucous cocoon around itself.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : ADAPTIVE COLOURATION AND ITS FUNCTIONS

subject: Biology
q: The opening and closing of the stoma are regulated by
explanation: The opening and closing of the stoma are regulated by osmosis. The guard cells that surround the stoma can take in or lose water, causing them to swell or shrink. When they take in water and swell, the stoma opens. When they lose water and shrink, the stoma closes. This process is driven by osmosis, the movement of water from an area of high concentration to an area of low concentration.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following is common to the mosquito, housefly and blackfly?
options: Their adults have two pairs of wings
explanation: The mosquito, housefly, and blackfly all undergo complete metamorphosis. This is a type of insect development that includes four distinct stages: egg, larva, pupa, and adult. Not all insects undergo complete metamorphosis, but these three do.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : STRUCTURAL ADAPTATIONS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: The organs that will be most useful to giant African rats in finding their way in underground habitats are the
explanation: Vibrissae, also known as whiskers, are highly sensitive to touch. They can detect changes in their surroundings, making them useful for navigation in the dark. This would be particularly useful for a giant African rat in an underground habitat, where visibility is limited.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : STRUCTURAL ADAPTATIONS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: A crucible of 5gm weighed 10gm after filling with fresh soil. It is then heated in an oven at 1,000 °C for 1 hour. After cooling in a desiccators the weight was 8gm. The percentage of water in the soil is
explanation:
The weight of the crucible = 5gm
Weight of crucible + soil = 10gm
Weight of soil = 10 - 5 = 5gm
Dehydration caused by heating leads to a reduction in the mass of the crucible.
The new weight of soil + crucible = 8gm
Weight of dry (dehydrated) soil = 8gm - 5gm = 3gm
Hence: We have 2gm of water
=2
5		×100
1		=40%
= 0.4

topics.title: SOIL : CHARACTERISTICS OF DIFFERENT TYPES OF SOIL

subject: Biology
q: The waste product of plants used in the conversion of hide to leather is
explanation: Tannin is a type of polyphenolic compound found in various plant tissues. It has astringent properties and is used in the conversion of animal hides into leather, a process known as tanning. Therefore, tannin is the waste product of plants used in the conversion of hide to leather.

subject: Biology
q: The correct sequence of the movement of urea during urine formation is
options: glomerulus → bowman's capsule → convoluted tubule → henle's loop → collecting tubule
convoluted tubule → glomerulus → Henle's loop → Bowman's capsule → collecting tubule
glomerulus → Bowman's capsule → convoluted tubule → collecting tubule→ Henle's loop
convoluted tubule → Bowman's capsule → Henle's loop → glomerulus → collecting tubule
explanation: The movement of urea during urine formation starts from the glomerulus, then to the Bowman's capsule, then to the convoluted tubule, then to the Henle's loop, and finally to the collecting tubule. This is the process of urine formation in the nephron, the functional unit of the kidney.

subject: Biology
q: In lizards, the lowing of the gular fold is used to
explanation: In lizards, the lowering of the gular fold (throat fan) is used to attract mates. It is a form of display behavior used in courtship.

subject: Biology
q: The photosynthetic pigments include
explanation: The photosynthetic pigments include chlorophyll and carotenoids. These pigments absorb light energy used in photosynthesis. Chlorophyll absorbs mainly blue and red light, while carotenoids absorb other wavelengths and help to broaden the spectrum of light that a plant can use for photosynthesis.

subject: Biology
q: The highest level of ecological organization is the
explanation: The highest level of ecological organization is the biosphere. The biosphere is the global sum of all ecosystems. It can also be termed as the zone of life on Earth, a closed system, and largely self-regulating.

subject: Biology
q: A biotic factor which affects the distribution and abundance of organism in a terrestrial habitats is
explanation: A biotic factor which affects the distribution and abundance of organism in a terrestrial habitats is competition. Biotic factors are the living components of an ecosystem. They are sorted into three groups: producers or autotrophs, consumers or heterotrophs, and decomposers or detritivores. Competition among species is a significant biotic factor that affects the distribution and abundance of organisms.

topics.title: ECOLOGY : FACTORS AFFECTING THE DISTRIBUTION OF ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: The eye defect that arises because the cornea is not curved smoothly is
explanation: The eye defect that arises because the cornea is not curved smoothly is astigmatism. Astigmatism is a type of refractive error in which the eye does not focus light evenly on the retina. This results in distorted or blurred vision at all distances. It occurs when the cornea or lens is not smoothly curved.

topics.title: COORDINATION AND CONTROL : THE SENSE ORGANS

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following is an example of parasitism?
options: A squirrel living in an abandoned nest of a bird
Fungi growing on a dead tree branch
Cattle egrets taking ticks from the body of cattle
explanation: Parasitism is a type of symbiotic relationship between species, where one organism, the parasite, lives on or in another organism, the host, causing it some harm, and is adapted structurally to this way of life. The Mistletoe growing on an orange tree is an example of parasitism because the mistletoe (parasite) extracts water and nutrients from the orange tree (host), which can harm or even kill the tree.

topics.title: ECOLOGY : SYMBIOTIC INTERACTIONS OF PLANTS AND ANIMALS

subject: Biology
q: The increasing order of the particle size in the following soil types is
explanation: The particle size of different soil types varies. Clay particles are the smallest, followed by silt, then sand, and finally gravel which has the largest particles. Therefore, the correct order from smallest to largest is clay → silt → sand → gravel.

topics.title: SOIL : CHARACTERISTICS OF DIFFERENT TYPES OF SOIL

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following factors can bring about competition in a population?
explanation: Competition in a population can be brought about by various factors, one of which is limited resources. Drought is a condition that significantly reduces the availability of water, a vital resource, thereby increasing competition among organisms in a population for this scarce resource.

subject: Biology
q: Stunted growth and poor root development are a result of a deficiency in
explanation: Phosphorus is a vital nutrient for plants, playing a key role in various plant processes such as energy transfer and photosynthesis. It is also crucial for root development and plant growth. A deficiency in phosphorus can lead to stunted growth and poor root development in plants.

explanation: Mitochondrion is responsible for respiration in cells, energy is released in the mitochondrion from simple sugar. Mitochondrion is usually referred to as the power house of the cell.
