q:
Use the diagram above to answer this question
The organelle responsible for heredity is
explanation: Chromosome is the part labelled III, it is responsible for heredity as it contains DNA which stores genetic traits.

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer this question

The process illustrated is
explanation:
The diagram is the sexual reproduction in Rhizopus. Sexual reproduction in Rhizopus involves:

1. Gametangia Formation:Hyphae from two compatible mating types (+ and -) form gametangia.
2. Plasmogamy: Cytoplasm of gametangia fuse, forming a zygosporangium.
3. Karyogamy: Nuclei fuse within the zygosporangium, creating diploid nuclei.
4. Zygospore Formation: The zygosporangium becomes a thick-walled zygospore.
5. Germination: Under favorable conditions, the zygospore undergoes meiosis and germinates.
6. Spore Release: Haploid spores are released from a new sporangium, growing into new hyphae.

explanation: The structure labeled I is the is the sporangium.

q:

explanation: The diagram depicts paramecium. Paramecium caudatum is a protists, protists reproduce sexually by spore formation and asexual either by spores or binary fission. The micro nucleus (the part labelled III) is responsible for sexual reproduction.

explanation:
The part labelled IV is to posterior contractile vacuole
Contractile vacuole helps to regulate the amount of salt and water in the cell (osmoregulation).

subject: Biology
q:
Use the diagram below to answer this question

The part labelled I is the
explanation: The part labelled I is the pulmonary artery. The pulmonary artery originates from the right ventricle of the heart, carrying deoxygenated blood to the lungs for oxygenation. It branches into the left and right pulmonary arteries, which lead to the respective lungs where gas exchange occurs. This artery is critical for transporting blood to the lungs and facilitating the exchange of carbon dioxide for oxygen during respiration.

Oxygenated blood is pumped to the entire body from the part labelled
explanation:
The part labelled (IV) is the left ventricle
Oxygenated blood usually passes from the left ventricle passes into the aorta to the body.

explanation:
It demonstrates geotropism. It is a turning or growth movement by a plant or fungus in response to gravity. It is a general feature of all higher and many lower plants as well as other organisms.
Geotropism is the directional growth of parts of plant in response to gravity. This experiment demonstrate geotropism showing that the root undergoes positive geotropism (towards gravity) and the stem undergoes negative geotropism (against gravity)

q:

explanation: Amplexus is a type of mating behavior exhibited by some externally fertilizing species in which a male grasps a female with his front legs as part of the mating process, and at the same time or with some time delay, he fertilizes the eggs as they are released from the female's body.

Use the diagram above to answer this question

explanation: The organisms are oviparous as eggs are laid externally and the male organism fertilizing it.

subject: Biology
q: Insulin is produced by the endocrine organ labelled
explanation: Insulin is produced by the endocrine organ labelled III, Insulin is produced by the pancreas

subject: Biology
q: In the mammalian male reproductive system, the part that serves as a passage for both urine and semen is the
explanation: In the male reproductive system, the urethra serves as a common passage for both urine and semen. The urethra extends from the bladder to the tip of the penis, allowing the discharge of urine from the body. During ejaculation, it also serves as a passage for semen.

subject: Biology
q:

options: All its offspring will be born without tails
3/4 of its offsprings will be born without tails
None of the offspring will be born without a tail
1/4 of its offspring will be born without tails.
explanation: When dog II mates which dog III, none of the offspring will be born without a tail.

explanation: Dog IV has a high probability of having the genotype gg. This is due to its skin colour.

Countershading is a protective adaptation whereby an animal possess a darker colour in its dorsal side and a lighter colour in its ventral side. This helps the animal to camouflage and escape predation. This is exhibited by the animal in the above diagram.

q:

The structure that conducts water round the plant body is labelled

topics.title: INTERNAL STRUCTURE OF A FLOWERING PLANT : ROOT

subject: Biology
q: The relationship between a herbivore and the bacteria that lives in its caecum is
explanation: The relationship between a herbivore and the bacteria that live in its caecum is mutualism. Mutualism is a type of symbiotic relationship where both organisms benefit. In this case, the bacteria help the herbivore digest plant material, and in return, the bacteria receive a warm, nutrient-rich environment to live in.

topics.title: ECOLOGY : SYMBIOTIC INTERACTIONS OF PLANTS AND ANIMALS

subject: Biology
q: An example of an animal that exhibits counter shading is
explanation: Countershading is a method of camouflage in which an animal's coloration is darker on the upper side and lighter on the underside of the body. This reduces the animal's visibility from different angles, both from above and below. Tilapia fish is an example of an animal that exhibits countershading. The other options do not typically exhibit countershading.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : ADAPTIVE COLOURATION AND ITS FUNCTIONS

subject: Biology
q: The presence of Chaetae on Earthworm is for
explanation: Chaetae in earthworms are tiny, bristle-like structures that the earthworm uses for locomotion. They help the earthworm to grip the soil and move forward. They are not involved in respiration, reproduction, or excretion.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : STRUCTURAL ADAPTATIONS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: The presence of four chambered heart is a characteristic feature of
explanation: A four-chambered heart is a characteristic feature of mammals. This type of heart allows for complete separation of oxygenated and deoxygenated blood, which makes the circulatory system of mammals more efficient. Pisces (fish), reptilia (reptiles), and amphibians do not have a four-chambered heart.

topics.title: INTERNAL STRUCTURE OF A MAMMAL : MAMMALIAN INTERNAL ORGANS

subject: Biology
q: The sum total of adaptive changes that have taken place over a very long period of time is referred to as
explanation: Evolution is the process by which different kinds of living organisms are thought to have developed and diversified from earlier forms during the history of the earth. It is the sum total of adaptive changes that have taken place over a very long period of time.

subject: Biology
q: The effect of prolactin hormone in human is that it
options: stimulates the development of female sex organ
explanation: Prolactin is a hormone produced by the pituitary gland in the brain. It's primary role is to promote breast milk production (lactation) in women after childbirth. Hence, it induces milk production in pregnant women.

subject: Biology
q: An example of organism that reproduce by binary fission is
explanation: Binary fission is a type of asexual reproduction where an organism splits into two offspring. Paramecium, a single-celled protist, reproduces by binary fission.

subject: Biology
q:
I. Gritty when wet.
II. High air content.
III. High capillarity.
IV. Fine particles.
Which of the above are properties of clay soil?
explanation: Clay soil is known for its fine particles and it becomes gritty when wet. Clay soil has a high cation-exchange capacity, meaning it can hold onto nutrients and water much better than sandy soil. The high capillarity refers to the ability of the soil to draw water upwards from a water source. This is not a characteristic of clay soil. Clay soil does not have high air content, it is actually known for being dense and heavy, with low porosity.

topics.title: SOIL : CHARACTERISTICS OF DIFFERENT TYPES OF SOIL

subject: Biology
q: The male reproductive organ of a flower is the
explanation: The male reproductive organ of a flower is the androecium. The androecium is the collective term for all the male organs of a flower, which includes the stamens. Each stamen is made up of a filament (stalk) and an anther, which produces pollen grains. The calyx and corolla are the outer parts of a flower, and the gynoecium is the female reproductive organ.

topics.title: INTERNAL STRUCTURE OF A FLOWERING PLANT : STEM

subject: Biology
q: Which of the organisms in the food chain above have the lowest number?
explanation:
As the trophic level goes higher, the number of organisms decreases .
Grasses → Grasshoppers → Lizards → Hawks
The number of grasses eaten by lizards and finally, the number of lizards eaten by the hawks is greater than the number of hawks.

topics.title: ECOLOGY : FOOD CHAINS FOOD WEBS AND TROPHIC LEVELS

subject: Biology
q: The transfer of pollen grains from the anther to the stigma is referred to as
explanation: The transfer of pollen grains from the anther (the part of the stamen that produces and contains pollen) to the stigma (the part of the pistil where pollen germinates) of a flower is referred to as pollination. Fertilization is the fusion of the male gametes (from the pollen) with the female gametes (in the ovule). Placentation refers to the arrangement of ovules within the ovary, and germination is the process by which a plant grows from a seed or similar structure.

subject: Biology
q: The organ that is responsible for detoxification in the body is the
explanation: The liver is the organ that is responsible for detoxification in the body. It does this by metabolizing and removing harmful substances from the bloodstream. The other options, bladder, spleen, and kidney, have different functions. The bladder stores urine, the spleen filters blood, and the kidneys filter blood to produce urine.

topics.title: INTERNAL STRUCTURE OF A MAMMAL : MAMMALIAN INTERNAL ORGANS

subject: Biology
q: In plants, the structure which translocate manufactured food from the site of production to where it is used up is the
explanation: In plants, the phloem is the structure that translocates manufactured food from the site of production (usually the leaves) to where it is used or stored. The phloem is part of the plant's vascular system, which also includes the xylem. The xylem transports water and minerals from the roots to the rest of the plant, not food. Lenticels and food vacuoles have different functions.

subject: Biology
q: The part of the eye that is sensitive to light is the
explanation: The retina is the part of the eye that is sensitive to light. It contains cells called photoreceptors which convert light into electrical signals. These signals are then sent to the brain via the optic nerve where they are interpreted as visual images. The sclerotic layer, optic nerve, and conjunctiva have different functions in the eye.

topics.title: COORDINATION AND CONTROL : THE SENSE ORGANS

subject: Biology
q: The possession of hollow and light bones is an adaptive feature of
explanation: Birds have hollow and light bones. This is an adaptive feature that allows them to fly. The hollow bones are strong and provide the necessary support for flight, while their lightness reduces the bird's overall weight, making it easier for them to stay aloft.

subject: Biology
q: The condition in which the skin is non-pigmented because of lack of melanin is known as
explanation: Albinism is a condition that causes the skin, hair, or eyes to have little or no color. Albinism is caused by mutations (changes) in several genes. The mutations may be passed down through families. Melanin is the pigment that gives color to the skin, hair, and eyes. In albinism, the cells that produce melanin do not work properly because of the mutations.

topics.title: HEREDITY : INHERITANCE OF CHARACTERS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: Organisms at the cell level of organization include
explanation: Organisms at the cell level of organization include amoeba, paramecium and chlamydomonas. These are all unicellular organisms, meaning they are made up of a single cell. In contrast, organisms like hydra and volvox are multicellular, meaning they are made up of many cells.

subject: Biology
q: The net result of the influence of natality and mortality rate of organisms in a given habitat is referred to as
explanation: The net result of the influence of natality (birth rate) and mortality (death rate) of organisms in a given habitat is referred to as population growth rate. This is because the difference between the birth rate and the death rate determines whether the population is growing, shrinking, or remaining stable.

topics.title: THE ECOLOGY OF POPULATIONS : FACTORS AFFECTING POPULATION SIZES

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following is NOT true of plants?
explanation: Plants do not have a developed excretory system like animals. Instead, they get rid of waste products through different methods such as storing them in leaves that fall off, or releasing them into the soil through their roots.

subject: Biology
q: Trypanosome gambinese is a parasite transmitted to human by infected.
explanation: Trypanosoma gambiense , the parasite that causes African sleeping sickness, is transmitted to humans by the tsetse fly. When an infected tsetse fly bites a human, the parasite is transferred into the human's bloodstream.

topics.title: ECOLOGY : SYMBIOTIC INTERACTIONS OF PLANTS AND ANIMALS

subject: Biology
q: Severe diarrhoea, dehydration and weakness of the body are symptoms of
explanation: Cholera is an infectious disease that causes severe watery diarrhea, which can lead to dehydration and even death if untreated. It is caused by eating food or drinking water contaminated with a bacterium called Vibrio cholerae .

subject: Biology
q: The structure which allows selective movement of materials in and out of the cell is the
explanation: The cell membrane, also known as the plasma membrane, is a biological membrane that separates the interior of all cells from the outside environment. It is selectively permeable to ions and organic molecules and controls the movement of substances in and out of cells.

topics.title: LIVING ORGANISMS : CELL STRUCTURE AND FUNCTIONS OF CELL COMPONENTS

subject: Biology
q: In multicellular organisms, a group of numerous, similar cells arranged together to perform specific function is referred to as
explanation: In multicellular organisms, a tissue is an ensemble of similar cells and their extracellular matrix from the same origin that together carry out a specific function. Organs are then formed by the functional grouping together of multiple tissues.

subject: Biology
q: The most suitable medium for the growth of saprophytic organisms is
explanation: Saprophytic organisms are organisms that feed on dead and decaying organic matter. Therefore, the most suitable medium for their growth is on decomposing materials.

subject: Biology
q: The raw materials of photosynthesis are
explanation: Photosynthesis is the process by which green plants and some other organisms use sunlight to synthesize foods with the aid of chlorophyll pigments. The raw materials for photosynthesis are carbon dioxide (carbon(IV)oxide) and water.

subject: Biology
q: Voluntary actions are controlled by the
explanation: Voluntary actions are those actions which are under the conscious control of the brain. They are controlled by the cerebrum, the largest part of the brain, which is responsible for all voluntary actions in the body.

subject: Biology
q: A condition in which a harmless organism resembles a distasteful or harmful one to scare its enemies is
explanation: Batesian mimicry is a form of mimicry where a harmless species has evolved to imitate the warning signals of a harmful species directed at a predator of them both. It is named after the English naturalist Henry Walter Bates, after his work in the rainforests of Brazil.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : ADAPTIVE COLOURATION AND ITS FUNCTIONS

subject: Biology
q: Celebro spinal meningitis is caused by
explanation: Cerebrospinal meningitis, also known as meningococcal meningitis, is a bacterial form of meningitis, a serious infection of the meninges that affects the brain membrane. It can cause severe brain damage and is fatal in 50% of cases if untreated. It is caused by the bacterium Neisseria meningitis , also known as meningococcus.

subject: Biology
q: A typical example of a thallophyta is
explanation: Thallophyta is a division of the plant kingdom that includes all the lower plants i.e., the algae, fungi, lichens, and bacteria. A typical example of a thallophyta is spirogyra. Spirogyra is a genus of filamentous green algae of the order Zygnematales, named for the helical or spiral arrangement of the chloroplasts that is characteristic of the species.

subject: Biology
q: The character that determines physical appearance and helps to identify the individual of a population is
explanation: Morphological variations refer to the differences in physical characteristics among individuals of the same species. These variations can include differences in size, shape, color, and other observable traits.

subject: Biology
q: Movement of minerals and chemical compounds within a plant occurs during
explanation: Translocation in plants refers to the movement of organic compounds, such as sugars, amino acids, and minerals, from the leaves (where they are produced through photosynthesis) to other parts of the plant.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following is NOT an example of transmittable character in human things?
explanation: Life span or pattern of growth is not a transmittable character in human beings. Transmittable characters are traits that can be passed from parents to offspring through genes. While certain aspects of growth and development can be influenced by genetics, the overall life span and pattern of growth are largely determined by environmental factors and individual lifestyle choices, not genetics.

topics.title: HEREDITY : INHERITANCE OF CHARACTERS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: The organ that is responsible for excretion in insects is the
explanation: In insects, the organ responsible for excretion is the Malpighian tubules. These tubules float in the haemolymph and collect waste products, which are then excreted out of the body. Flame cells are excretory structures in flatworms, nephridia are found in earthworms, and contractile vacuoles are found in some single-celled organisms.

subject: Biology
q: The phloem tissues is made up of
explanation: Phloem tissue is made up of sieve tubes and companion cells. Sieve tubes are responsible for the transport of sugars and other nutrients throughout the plant, while companion cells help to maintain the sieve tubes. Wood parenchyma, tracheids and wood fibres, and xylem vessels are all components of xylem tissue, not phloem.

subject: Biology
q: The dental formula i-3/3, c-1/1, pm-4/4, m-2/3 is found in
explanation: The dental formula i-3/3, c-1/1, pm-4/4, m-2/3 is found in dogs. This formula represents the number of incisors (i), canines (c), premolars (pm), and molars (m) in one half of the mouth. Humans have a dental formula of i-2/2, c-1/1, pm-2/2, m-3/3, while cows have a dental formula of i-0/4, c-0/1, pm-3/3, m-3/3, and rabbits have a dental formula of i-2/1, c-0/0, pm-3/2, m-3/3.

topics.title: INTERNAL STRUCTURE OF A MAMMAL : MAMMALIAN INTERNAL ORGANS

subject: Biology
q: The ecological factor that is common to arboreal habitat
explanation: The ecological factor common to arboreal habitat is altitude. Arboreal habitats are habitats that are located in the trees. Altitude is a factor because the types of trees that can grow in an area, and therefore the type of arboreal habitat, can change with altitude.

topics.title: NATURAL HABITAT : AQUATIC TERRESTRIAL AND ARBOREAL

subject: Biology
q: The local biome which is characterised by little or no rainfall, high temperature and few grasses is the
explanation: The local biome which is characterised by little or no rainfall, high temperature and few grasses is the desert. Deserts are known for their harsh conditions, including extreme temperatures and lack of water.

subject: Biology
q: The pairs of genes on the position of a chromosome that control contrasting characters is known as
explanation: The pairs of genes on the position of a chromosome that control contrasting characters are known as allelomorphs. In genetics, an allelomorph is an alternative form of a gene (one member of a pair) that is located at a specific position on a specific chromosome. These DNA codings determine distinct traits that can be passed on from parents to offspring through sexual reproduction. The concept of allelomorphs is fundamental to the study of genetics.

topics.title: HEREDITY : INHERITANCE OF CHARACTERS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: A man who has the ability to taste phenylthiocarbamide marries a woman who can not taste phenylthiocarbamide. The probability of their children to taste phenylthiocarbamide
explanation: The ability to taste phenylthiocarbamide (PTC) is a dominant trait. Therefore, if one parent has the ability to taste PTC (Tt) and the other parent does not (tt), the possible genotypes of their children would be Tt and tt. This results in a 50:50 probability of their children being able to taste PTC.

topics.title: HEREDITY : PROBABILITY IN GENETICS AND SEX DETERMINATION

subject: Biology
q: The association between a remora fish and a shark is
explanation: The relationship between a remora fish and a shark is an example of commensalism. Commensalism is a type of symbiotic relationship where one organism benefits and the other is neither harmed nor benefited. In this case, the remora fish benefits by getting a free ride and access to leftover food, while the shark is not affected.

topics.title: ECOLOGY : SYMBIOTIC INTERACTIONS OF PLANTS AND ANIMALS

subject: Biology
q: The part of the human tooth that is sensitive to heat and cold is the
explanation:
The pulp cavity of the tooth contains the nerve endings and blood vessels, which makes it sensitive to heat and cold. The enamel and dentine are hard tissues that protect the pulp cavity, while the gum is the soft tissue that surrounds and supports the teeth. The pulp cavity is sensitive to heat and cold due to the presence of:

- Nerve endings that transmit sensory information
- Blood vessels that respond to temperature changes
- Soft tissue that expands and contracts with temperature changes

This sensitivity helps protect the tooth and surrounding tissues, but can also cause discomfort or pain if the tooth is damaged or infected.

topics.title: INTERNAL STRUCTURE OF A MAMMAL : MAMMALIAN INTERNAL ORGANS

subject: Biology
q: In centipedes, the exchange of gases is carried out through the
explanation: In centipedes, the exchange of gases is carried out through the trachea. The trachea is a system of tubes that allows for the direct exchange of gases into the tissues. This is different from humans and other vertebrates, where gas exchange occurs in the lungs.

subject: Biology
q: The presence of tall trees, broad leaves and buttress roots are characteristic features of the
explanation: The presence of tall trees, broad leaves and buttress roots are characteristic features of the tropical rainforest. These features allow the plants to maximize sunlight absorption, which is abundant in the tropical rainforest. The buttress roots provide additional support to the tall trees.

subject: Biology
q: A viral disease of man is
explanation: Small pox is a viral disease of man. It is caused by the variola virus. The other diseases listed, syphilis, tuberculosis, and cholera, are caused by bacteria, not viruses.

subject: Biology
q: The organelle that controls all life activities of the cell is the
explanation: The nucleus is the organelle that controls all life activities of the cell. It contains the cell's DNA and regulates the cell's activities such as metabolism, growth, and reproduction by controlling protein synthesis.

topics.title: LIVING ORGANISMS : CELL STRUCTURE AND FUNCTIONS OF CELL COMPONENTS

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following organisms does not exhibit adaptive colouration?
explanation: Adaptive coloration is a type of camouflage where an organism's coloration or pattern allows it to blend in with its environment. Among the options, the Giraffe does not exhibit adaptive coloration. While its pattern may provide some camouflage in certain environments, it is not a primary function as it is with the other organisms listed.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : ADAPTIVE COLOURATION AND ITS FUNCTIONS

subject: Biology
q: Northern Guinea savanna can be found in
explanation: Northern Guinea savanna can be found in Borno state. The Guinea Savanna is a tropical grassland ecoregion of West Africa. In Nigeria, it is found in the northern part of the country, including Borno state.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following organisms is not found in the marine habitat?
explanation: Tilapia is a freshwater fish and is not found in marine habitats. Marine habitats are saltwater environments like oceans and seas. Tilapia, on the other hand, are found in freshwater environments like rivers, lakes, and ponds.

topics.title: NATURAL HABITAT : AQUATIC TERRESTRIAL AND ARBOREAL

subject: Biology
q: The concave shape of a bird's wing during flight, ensures that the greater air pressure under the wings will ......
options: lift the bird so that it can remain airborne.
enable the pectoral muscles to contract easily
explanation: The concave shape of a bird's wing during flight creates a pressure difference between the top and bottom of the wing. This pressure difference generates lift, which helps the bird to remain airborne. This is based on Bernoulli's principle, which states that an increase in the speed of a fluid occurs simultaneously with a decrease in pressure.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following terms ensures the survival of an organism in its environment?
explanation: Adaptation ensures the survival of an organism in its environment. Adaptation is the process by which an organism becomes better suited to its habitat. This process takes place over many generations and is a key factor in the process of evolution. It involves changes in the organism's structure, function, or behavior that allow it to survive and reproduce in its environment.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : ADAPTIVE COLOURATION AND ITS FUNCTIONS

subject: Biology
q: The number of individuals that the environment can contain at a time is its
explanation: The number of individuals that the environment can contain at a time is its carrying capacity. Carrying capacity refers to the maximum number of individuals of a particular species that an environment can support over a given period of time. This is determined by the available resources such as food, water, and space.

topics.title: THE ECOLOGY OF POPULATIONS : FACTORS AFFECTING POPULATION SIZES

subject: Biology
q: A change in the environment of an organism is termed
explanation: A change in the environment of an organism is termed stimulus. Stimulus is any change in an organism's environment that causes the organism to react. It is a signal to which an organism responds.

subject: Biology
q: Which among these organism in the options given will have the highest biomass?
explanation: Among the given options, Phytoplankton will have the highest biomass. Biomass refers to the total mass of organisms in a given area or volume. Phytoplankton are the primary producers in aquatic ecosystems and form the base of the food chain, thus they have the highest biomass.

topics.title: ECOLOGY : ENERGY FLOW IN THE ECOSYSTEM

subject: Biology
q: Forest conservation includes the following except
options: replacing of harvested trees by planting seedlings
explanation: Forest conservation is all about preserving and taking care of forests. It includes prevention of bush fires, replacing of harvested trees by planting seedlings, and selective exploitation of forest trees. However, extensive felling of forest trees is not a part of forest conservation. In fact, it is the opposite of conservation as it involves cutting down trees in large numbers, which leads to deforestation.

topics.title: HUMANS AND ENVIRONMENT : CONSERVATION OF NATURAL RESOURCES

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following structures controls the activities of the living cell?
explanation: The nucleus is the control center of the cell. It contains the cell's DNA, or the genetic information, from which proteins are synthesized. These proteins are necessary for the cell to grow, reproduce, and perform other cellular activities. Therefore, the nucleus controls the activities of the living cell.

topics.title: LIVING ORGANISMS : CELL STRUCTURE AND FUNCTIONS OF CELL COMPONENTS

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following is used to measure the rate of water uptake in plants?
explanation: A potometer is a device that is used to measure the rate of water uptake of a plant due to photosynthesis and transpiration. It measures how factors such as light, temperature, humidity, wind speed and quality of water affect the rate of transpiration. The other options, hydrometer, hygrometer and aspirator, are not used to measure the rate of water uptake in plants.

subject: Biology
q: Which cell organelle acts as the control center regulating cell functions?
explanation: The nucleus contains the genetic material (DNA) that controls and regulates cell activities, including growth and reproduction. Mitochondria generate energy, ribosomes synthesize proteins, and lysosomes digest waste materials.

topics.title: LIVING ORGANISMS : CELL STRUCTURE AND FUNCTIONS OF CELL COMPONENTS

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following practices is not used in conserving water?
explanation: Deforestation is not a practice used in conserving water. In fact, it is the opposite. Trees play a crucial role in the water cycle, grounding the water in their roots and releasing it into the atmosphere. In the absence of trees, the landscape often becomes barren and dry, which leads to less rainfall and less water for drinking and irrigation.

topics.title: HUMANS AND ENVIRONMENT : CONSERVATION OF NATURAL RESOURCES

subject: Biology
q: Which pigment in the malpighian layer gives skin colouration?
explanation: Melanin is the pigment that gives skin, hair, and eyes their color. Dark-skinned people have more melanin in their skin than light-skinned people. Melanin is produced by cells called melanocytes. It provides some protection against skin damage from the sun, and the melanocytes increase their production of melanin in response to sun exposure.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : ADAPTIVE COLOURATION AND ITS FUNCTIONS

subject: Biology
q: The blood group in humans referred to as universal recipient is
explanation: The blood group AB is referred to as the universal recipient in humans. This is because it has both A and B antigens on the surface of the red blood cells and neither anti-A nor anti-B antibodies in the plasma. This means that individuals with AB blood group can receive blood from donors of any blood group: A, B, AB, or O.

topics.title: HEREDITY : INHERITANCE OF CHARACTERS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: Which of these is NOT a type of finger print?
explanation: Simple is not a type of finger print. Fingerprint patterns are unique to each individual, and they remain unchanged throughout a person's life. This is why fingerprints are used as a form of identification.

subject: Biology
q: Which of these organisms is a producer?
explanation: Spirogyra is a producer. Producers, also known as autotrophs, are organisms that can produce their own food from the substances available in their surroundings using light (photosynthesis) or chemical energy (chemosynthesis). Spirogyra is a type of green algae that performs photosynthesis, thus it is a producer. Mucor, Rhizopus, and Fungus are all types of fungi, which are decomposers, not producers.

topics.title: ECOLOGY : ENERGY FLOW IN THE ECOSYSTEM

subject: Biology
q: In humans, which of the following hormones correspond to male and female respectively.
explanation: Testosterone is the primary male sex hormone and an anabolic steroid. In male humans, testosterone plays a key role in the development of male reproductive tissues such as testes and prostate. Oestrogen, or estrogen, is the primary female sex hormone. It is responsible for the development and regulation of the female reproductive system and secondary sex characteristics. Therefore, the correct answer is Testosterone and Oestrogen.

subject: Biology
q: Viviparity is a type of fertilization that occurs mainly in ......
explanation: Viviparity is a mode of reproduction in animals in which embryos develop inside the body of the mother, as opposed to laying eggs in oviparous species. This is the characteristic of mammals. Therefore, the correct answer is Mammals.

subject: Biology
q: All but one are part of the female reproductive system .
explanation: The female reproductive system includes structures such as the ovaries, fallopian tubes, and uterus. The seminal vesicle is part of the male reproductive system, which produces a fluid that mixes with sperm to create semen. Therefore, the correct answer is seminal vesicle.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following processes does not contribute to the maintenance of the nitrogen content of the soil?
explanation: Denitrification is a process by which nitrate (NO3- in the soil is reduced by bacteria to produce nitrogen (N2) or nitrous oxide gas (N2O), both of which are lost to the atmosphere. This process does not contribute to the maintenance of the nitrogen content of the soil, but rather reduces it.

subject: Biology
q: Soil permeability refers to
options: how easily water passes through the soil
the production of air in the soil
the proportion of water the soil holds
how well water rises up the soil
explanation: Soil permeability refers to the ability of the soil to allow water to pass through it. This is determined by the size and arrangement of the particles in the soil. Larger particles, such as sand, have larger spaces between them, which allows water to pass through more easily than smaller particles, such as clay.

topics.title: SOIL : CHARACTERISTICS OF DIFFERENT TYPES OF SOIL

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following glands secretes a substance into the blood stream of a frightened person?
explanation: The adrenal glands secrete adrenaline into the bloodstream when a person is frightened. Adrenaline is a hormone that prepares the body for a 'fight or flight' response by increasing heart rate, blood pressure, and blood glucose levels, among other things.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following is NOT an example of a reflex action?
options: Contraction of the pupil of the eye
explanation: A reflex action is an involuntary and nearly instantaneous movement in response to a stimulus. Thinking is not a reflex action because it is a voluntary action that involves conscious thought and decision making.

subject: Biology
q: The food vacuole of a plant cell is
options: smaller than that of an animal cell.
explanation: The food vacuole of a plant cell is surrounded by a membrane. A vacuole is a membrane-bound organelle which is present in all plant and fungal cells and some protist, animal and bacterial cells. Vacuoles are essentially enclosed compartments which are filled with water containing inorganic and organic molecules including enzymes in solution, though in certain cases they may contain solids which have been engulfed.

topics.title: LIVING ORGANISMS : CELL STRUCTURE AND FUNCTIONS OF CELL COMPONENTS

subject: Biology
q: Water flies, wood lice and barnacles are arthropods that belong to the class .......
explanation: Water fleas, wood lice and barnacles are arthropods that belong to the class Crustacea. Crustaceans are a large, diverse group of arthropods that includes familiar animals like crabs, lobsters, crayfish, shrimp, krill, woodlice, and barnacles. The water flea is a small crustacean, and woodlice are terrestrial crustaceans.

subject: Biology
q: At the end of mitosis the number of cells produced from a parent cell is
explanation: Mitosis is a process of cell division that results in two genetically identical daughter cells developing from a single parent cell. Thus, at the end of mitosis, the number of cells produced from a parent cell is two.

subject: Biology
q: The organelle responsible for respiration in monerans is ......
explanation: Monerans, which include bacteria, lack membrane-bound organelles such as mitochondria. Instead, they carry out respiration using an organelle called the mesosome. The mesosome is a folded region of the plasma membrane in prokaryotic cells that plays a role in various cellular processes, including respiration.

topics.title: LIVING ORGANISMS : CELL STRUCTURE AND FUNCTIONS OF CELL COMPONENTS

subject: Biology
q: A plant with a relatively short height can be stimulated to grow to normal height by the application of ......
explanation: Gibberellins are a group of plant hormones that stimulate stem elongation, germination, and flowering. They can be applied to a plant with a relatively short height to stimulate it to grow to normal height. Cytokinins, on the other hand, promote cell division in plant roots and shoots. Thyroxine is a hormone produced by the thyroid gland in animals, not plants. Abscisic acid is a plant hormone that inhibits growth.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following gives the functions of ribosomes in cells?
explanation: Ribosomes are a cell structure that makes protein. Protein is needed for many cell functions such as repairing damage or directing chemical processes. Ribosomes can be found floating within the cytoplasm or attached to the endoplasmic reticulum.

topics.title: LIVING ORGANISMS : CELL STRUCTURE AND FUNCTIONS OF CELL COMPONENTS

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following traits in human is not a morphological variation?
explanation: Morphological variations refer to the physical characteristics that vary among individuals of a species. These include traits like height, shape of head, and color of skin. The ability to roll your tongue is not a morphological variation, but rather a genetic trait.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following is not a morphological variation in human being?
explanation: Morphological variations refer to the physical characteristics that vary among individuals of a species. These include traits like size, color, and finger length. Blood groups, however, are not a morphological variation but rather a genetic trait.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following will ease the friction between the ends of bones in a movable joint?
explanation: Synovial fluid is a viscous, non-Newtonian fluid found in the cavities of synovial joints. It reduces friction between the articular cartilage of synovial joints during movement. Therefore, it eases the friction between the ends of bones in a movable joint.

topics.title: SUPPORT AND MOVEMENT : TYPES AND FUNCTIONS OF THE SKELETON

subject: Biology
q: All but one are the functions of the mammalian skeleton.
explanation: The mammalian skeleton serves several functions including support, protection, movement, and production of blood cells. However, it does not play a direct role in transportation. The circulatory system, not the skeletal system, is responsible for transportation in the body.

topics.title: SUPPORT AND MOVEMENT : TYPES AND FUNCTIONS OF THE SKELETON

subject: Biology
q: In flowering plants, fertilization would occur when ......
options: the pollen grows downwards penetrating the style.
a pollen grain is transferred to the stigma
one of the nuclei inside the pollen grain tube fuses with the ovum.
the nucleus inside the pollen tube divides
explanation: In flowering plants, fertilization occurs when one of the nuclei inside the pollen grain tube fuses with the ovum. This process is known as syngamy and results in the formation of a zygote, which eventually develops into a seed.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following activities least affects the carbon(IV)oxide content in the atmosphere (air belt)
explanation: Plant and animal respiration least affects the carbon(IV)oxide content in the atmosphere. This is because respiration is a part of the natural carbon cycle. The carbon dioxide produced by respiration is used by plants in photosynthesis, which in turn releases oxygen that animals use for respiration. This cycle keeps the carbon dioxide levels in the atmosphere relatively stable. The other options involve activities that add extra carbon dioxide to the atmosphere, thereby increasing its content.

topics.title: ECOLOGY : ENERGY FLOW IN THE ECOSYSTEM

subject: Biology
q: Which among these is a direct photosynthesis product?
explanation: Glucose is a direct product of photosynthesis. Photosynthesis is a process used by plants and other organisms to convert light energy, usually from the Sun, into chemical energy that can be later released to fuel the organisms' activities. This chemical energy is stored in carbohydrate molecules, such as sugars, which are synthesized from carbon dioxide and water. The other options, starch, protein, and fats, are not direct products of photosynthesis. They are synthesized in the plant using glucose as a starting material.

subject: Biology
q: The instrument used in measuring relative density is ..........
explanation: A hydrometer is used to measure the relative density (also known as specific gravity) of liquids. It works by floating in the liquid and the level at which it floats allows the density to be determined. A hygrometer is used to measure humidity, a wind vane is used to measure wind direction, and a barometer is used to measure atmospheric pressure.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : ADAPTIVE COLOURATION AND ITS FUNCTIONS

subject: Biology
q: Which of these factor are common to both aquatic and terrestrial habitat?
explanation: Both aquatic and terrestrial habitats share common factors such as light and temperature. Light is essential for photosynthesis in plants, and temperature affects the metabolic rates of organisms. Rainfall is not common to both as it is specific to terrestrial habitats. Turbidity and tide are specific to aquatic habitats. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Light, rainfall and temperature'.

topics.title: NATURAL HABITAT : AQUATIC TERRESTRIAL AND ARBOREAL

subject: Biology
q: The malpighian body of the kidney is made up of
explanation: The Malpighian body, also known as the renal corpuscle, is part of the nephron in the kidney. It is composed of the Bowman's capsule and the glomerulus. The Bowman's capsule is a cup-like sac at the beginning of the tubular component of a nephron that performs the first step in the filtration of blood to form urine. The glomerulus is a network of capillaries that performs the first step in filtering blood to form urine.

topics.title: INTERNAL STRUCTURE OF A MAMMAL : MAMMALIAN INTERNAL ORGANS

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following describes the function of thyroid gland?
explanation: The thyroid gland is an endocrine gland in the neck that produces thyroid hormones. These hormones primarily influence the metabolic rate and protein synthesis. The gland also produces the hormone calcitonin, which plays a role in calcium homeostasis. Hence, the correct answer is 'regulate the rate of metabolism'.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following is not a behavioural adaptation used by social insects?
explanation: Behavioural adaptations are changes in the behaviour of an organism that help it to survive and reproduce. The options given are types of adaptations, but 'Season' is not a behavioural adaptation used by social insects. Cryptic, Mimicry, and Flash are all behavioural adaptations used by insects to avoid predators. Cryptic is when insects blend in with their environment, Mimicry is when they mimic the appearance of other organisms, and Flash is when they use light signals to communicate.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : BEHAVIOURAL ADAPTATIONS IN SOCIAL ANIMALS

subject: Biology
q: Which among these processes removes water from the water cycle?
explanation: The water cycle is a process where water circulates between the earth's oceans, atmosphere, and land, involving precipitation as rain and snow, drainage in streams and rivers, and return to the atmosphere by evaporation and transpiration. Photosynthesis is the process that removes water from the water cycle because it uses water to produce glucose and oxygen. The water used in photosynthesis is not returned to the atmosphere or the soil, it is stored in the plant.

subject: Biology
q: The maximum population size an environment can support without degradation is known as its
explanation: Carrying capacity refers to the largest number of individuals of a species that an environment can sustainably support, considering available resources such as food, water, and shelter. If the population exceeds this limit, resources become depleted and environmental degradation can occur. Growth rate refers to how fast a population increases, while total capacity and population size are not precise ecological terms for this concept.

topics.title: THE ECOLOGY OF POPULATIONS : FACTORS AFFECTING POPULATION SIZES

subject: Biology
q: Any factor in an organism's surroundings that can trigger a reaction is called
explanation: A stimulus is any change or factor in the internal or external environment that can cause an organism to respond. Sensitivity is the ability to detect the stimulus, impulse is the electrical signal sent through nerves, and response is the action taken after detecting the stimulus.

subject: Biology
q: A farmer notices that his crop plants are stunted and have unusually short stems. Which plant hormone should he apply to encourage stem elongation?
explanation: Gibberellins are plant hormones that promote stem elongation, seed germination, and flowering. Applying gibberellins to stunted plants can stimulate them to grow taller. Cytokinins mainly promote cell division, auxins stimulate cell elongation mainly in shoots and roots, and abscisic acid slows down growth by promoting dormancy.

subject: Biology
q: What is the mammalian organ through which nourishment and oxygen diffuse into a developing embryo called ?
explanation: The mammalian organ through which nourishment and oxygen diffuse into a developing embryo is called the 'placenta'. The placenta is an organ that develops in the uterus during pregnancy. It provides oxygen and nutrients to the growing baby and removes waste products from the baby's blood. The amnion is a membrane that closely covers the embryo when first formed. The chorion is the outermost fetal membrane around the embryo, and it takes part in the formation of the placenta. The umbilical cord is the life-support system that carries oxygen and nutrients from your placenta into your baby's bloodstream.

subject: Biology
q: In mammals, the outermost embryonic membrane is the .......
explanation: In mammals, the outermost embryonic membrane is the Chorion. The chorion is one of the membranes that exist during pregnancy between the developing fetus and mother. It involves in the formation of the placenta.

subject: Biology
q: Which hormone is responsible for regulating blood sugar levels in the human body?
explanation: Insulin is the hormone responsible for regulating blood sugar levels. It is produced by the pancreas and helps cells absorb glucose from the bloodstream to be used for energy or stored for later use. Thyroxine controls metabolism, adrenalin prepares the body for 'fight or flight', and oestrogen regulates female reproductive functions.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following factors is not associated with aquatic habitat?
explanation: Humidity is not associated with aquatic habitat. Humidity primarily relates to atmospheric moisture and has minimal direct impact on the underwater environment.

topics.title: NATURAL HABITAT : AQUATIC TERRESTRIAL AND ARBOREAL

subject: Biology
q: Lakes, ponds, seas, rivers and oceans are grouped together as ......
explanation: Lakes, ponds, seas, rivers, and oceans are all bodies of water, hence they are grouped together as an 'aquatic habitat'. An aquatic habitat is a habitat with water. It includes areas that are permanently covered by water and surrounding areas that are occasionally covered by water.

topics.title: NATURAL HABITAT : AQUATIC TERRESTRIAL AND ARBOREAL

subject: Biology
q: The main similarity between unicellular and multicellular organisms is that they
explanation: The main similarity between unicellular and multicellular organisms is that they 'perform all life activities'. Both unicellular and multicellular organisms carry out all the necessary life processes such as respiration, digestion, excretion, reproduction, etc. for their survival.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following group of arthropods has no antennae?
explanation: Arachnida is the correct answer. Arachnids, a group of arthropods, which includes spiders, scorpions, ticks, and mites, do not have antennae. In contrast, other groups like crustacea, chilopoda, and diplopoda do have antennae.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : STRUCTURAL ADAPTATIONS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following statements defines plasmolysis?
options: Shrinking of a plant cell in a solution
Shrinking away of cytoplasm from an animal cell membrane
Shrinking away of the cytoplasm away from the plant cell wall
Shrinking of the vacuole and leaving the cytoplasm attached to the cell wall.
explanation: Plasmolysis is the process in which cells lose water in a hypertonic solution. This leads to the shrinking away of the cytoplasm from the plant cell wall. This is a result of water moving out of the cell due to osmosis. In extreme cases, the cell becomes plasmolysed. The cell membrane peels away from the cell wall and the cell takes on a different shape.

topics.title: LIVING ORGANISMS : CELL STRUCTURE AND FUNCTIONS OF CELL COMPONENTS

subject: Biology
q: The neurones that receive stimuli from the body or internal organs are known as
explanation: Sensory neurons are nerve cells within the nervous system responsible for converting external stimuli from the organism's environment into internal electrical impulses. For example, some sensory neurons respond to tactile stimuli and can activate motor neurons in order to achieve muscle contraction. Efferent neurons, relay neurons, and motor neurons have different functions in the nervous system.

subject: Biology
q: The following stimuli are perceived through the skin of mammals except
explanation: The skin of mammals is sensitive to a variety of stimuli such as heat, pain, and pressure. However, it does not perceive light. Light is perceived by the eyes, which have specialized cells called photoreceptors that convert light into electrical signals that can be processed by the brain.

topics.title: COORDINATION AND CONTROL : THE SENSE ORGANS

subject: Biology
q: Reading the verdict of a case by a judge or magistrate is an example of
explanation: Reading the verdict of a case by a judge or magistrate is an example of a voluntary action. Voluntary actions are those that we consciously control. They involve the use of our brain and nervous system to actively command a part of our body to perform a certain action. The other options do not fit because a reflex action is an automatic response to a stimulus, an instinct is an innate behavior, and a conditioned reflex is a learned response to a stimulus.

subject: Biology
q: The fluid that bathes all cells of the body is called ......
explanation: The fluid that bathes all cells of the body is called Lymph. Lymph is a clear fluid that circulates throughout the lymphatic system. It provides cells with nutrients and oxygen while also removing waste products. Fibrinogen is a protein involved in blood clotting, Plasma is the liquid component of blood, and Blood itself is a body fluid that delivers necessary substances to the cells and transports waste products away from those same cells.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following pairs of organisms are photosynthetic?
explanation: The pair of organisms that are photosynthetic are Chlamydomonas and Euglena. Photosynthesis is the process by which green plants and some other organisms use sunlight to synthesize foods with the help of chlorophyll pigments. In this process, light energy is converted into chemical energy. Chlamydomonas and Euglena are both unicellular green algae that perform photosynthesis. The other pairs listed either do not perform photosynthesis or only one organism in the pair does.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following divisions/subdivisions are the most advanced?
explanation: Gymnosperms are the most advanced among the given options. The order of advancement in plant kingdom is Thallophytes < Bryophytes < Pteridophytes < Gymnosperms. Gymnosperms are vascular plants that produce seeds without a protective fruit, which distinguishes them from angiosperms, the most advanced plants.

subject: Biology
q: All the following processes are involved in the carbon cycle except?
explanation: The carbon cycle involves the processes of photosynthesis, respiration, decay, and combustion (burning). However, transportation is not involved in the carbon cycle. Transportation here could refer to the movement of water in plants or the movement of substances in animals, neither of which directly involve carbon.

subject: Biology
q: In the process of respiration, energy is transferred from glucose molecules to the molecules of
explanation: In the process of respiration, energy is transferred from glucose molecules to the molecules of ATP (Adenosine Triphosphate). ATP is the main energy currency of cells. It is produced in the mitochondria during the process of cellular respiration and is used to power many of the cell's metabolic processes.

subject: Biology
q: In an adult toad, gaseous exchange takes place through the ......
explanation: In an adult toad, gaseous exchange takes place through the buccal cavity, skin, and lungs. The buccal cavity is used for breathing when the toad is out of water, the skin is used for gas exchange when the toad is submerged in water, and the lungs are used for breathing when the toad is on land.

subject: Biology
q: What type of relationship is between protozoa and termites?
explanation: The relationship between protozoa and termites is symbiosis. Symbiosis is a close and long-term biological interaction between two different biological organisms. In this case, protozoa in the termite's gut help in breaking down cellulose from the wood the termite eats, which the termite cannot do on its own. In return, the protozoa get a safe environment and nutrients from the termite.

topics.title: ECOLOGY : SYMBIOTIC INTERACTIONS OF PLANTS AND ANIMALS

subject: Biology
q: A similar feature of the caste system of bees and termites is that .........
options: The kings are bigger than the queens
Nuptial flight is performed by all members.
explanation: A similar feature of the caste system of bees and termites is that the workers are sterile. In both species, the worker caste is typically unable to reproduce. This is a form of division of labor, where different members of the colony perform different tasks. In this case, the workers are responsible for gathering food, maintaining the nest, and caring for the young, while the queen and king are responsible for reproduction.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : BEHAVIOURAL ADAPTATIONS IN SOCIAL ANIMALS

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following infections is unlikely to respond to antibiotic treatment?
explanation: Measles is a viral infection and antibiotics are not effective against viruses. Antibiotics are used to treat bacterial infections, not viral infections. They work by killing bacteria or preventing them from reproducing and spreading.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following best describes the type of reproduction involved in cloning?
explanation: Cloning is a form of asexual reproduction because it does not involve the fusion of male and female gametes. Instead, it uses the genetic material from a single organism to produce an identical copy. This differs from sexual reproduction, which combines genetic material from two parents, leading to genetic variation.

subject: Biology
q: Which activity negatively impacts water conservation efforts?
explanation: Cutting down large areas of forest (deforestation) reduces water retention and disrupts the water cycle, harming water conservation. Afforestation, dam construction, and reforestation all help conserve water resources.

topics.title: HUMANS AND ENVIRONMENT : CONSERVATION OF NATURAL RESOURCES

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following soils have the highest water-retaining capacity?
explanation: Clay soil has the highest water-retaining capacity because its particles are very small and compact, which allows them to hold water better than other types of soil.

topics.title: SOIL : CHARACTERISTICS OF DIFFERENT TYPES OF SOIL

subject: Biology
q: Very low annual rainfall, sparse vegetation, high day temperatures and cold nights are the characteristic of the biome known as
explanation: The characteristics described in the question are typical of a desert biome. Deserts are known for their very low annual rainfall, sparse vegetation due to the lack of water, and extreme temperature fluctuations between day and night. During the day, the temperatures can be extremely high due to the direct exposure to the sun's rays, while at night, the temperatures can drop significantly due to the lack of humidity and cloud cover to trap the heat.

topics.title: SOIL : CHARACTERISTICS OF DIFFERENT TYPES OF SOIL

subject: Biology
q:
Which of the following processes are associated with photosynthesis?
I. Energy from sunlight is absorbed
II. Carbon dioxide is evolved
III. Oxygen is given off
IV glucose is synthesized
explanation: The processes associated with photosynthesis include the absorption of energy from sunlight, the release of oxygen, and the synthesis of glucose. Carbon dioxide is not evolved but rather consumed in the process of photosynthesis.

subject: Biology
q: The conservation of natural resources can be achieved by the following except
explanation: Conservation of natural resources involves protecting, preserving, managing, and restoring natural environments and wildlife. This can be achieved through the creation of national parks, formation of conservation societies and agencies, and establishment of game reserves. However, deregulated harvesting does not contribute to conservation. Deregulated harvesting means there are no rules or restrictions on how much can be harvested, which can lead to overexploitation and depletion of resources.

topics.title: HUMANS AND ENVIRONMENT : CONSERVATION OF NATURAL RESOURCES

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following practices is not considered a method of forest conservation?
explanation: Forest conservation aims to protect forests and sustain their resources. Controlled burning, replanting, and selective harvesting help maintain forest health. However, clear-cutting large areas causes deforestation and is not a conservation method.

topics.title: HUMANS AND ENVIRONMENT : CONSERVATION OF NATURAL RESOURCES

subject: Biology
q: Malpighian tubules is the excretory organ for which of the following organism
explanation: Malpighian tubules are the excretory organs found in insects. They help in the removal of waste products from the insect's body. They are not found in earthworms, fish, or hydra. Earthworms use nephridia for excretion, fish use gills and kidneys, and hydra use their body surface for excretion.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following best describes excretion?
explanation: Excretion is the process by which waste products of metabolism and other non-useful materials are eliminated from an organism. This is primarily carried out by excretory organs such as the kidneys, skin, and lungs. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Removal of the waste products.'

subject: Biology
q: The genes affecting the same trait and are located at the same position on the homologous chromosomes are called ......
explanation: The genes affecting the same trait and are located at the same position on the homologous chromosomes are called alleles. Alleles are different forms of the same gene. They are responsible for variations in genetic traits. Therefore, the correct answer is 'alleles.'

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following human traits is determined by genetic inheritance?
explanation: Hair color is primarily determined by genetics, inherited from parents. Abilities like speaking languages, playing instruments, and height (which is influenced by both genetics and environment) involve learned skills or multifactorial traits.

subject: Biology
q: The physical appearance of an organism is described as its
explanation: The genes affecting the same trait and are located at the same position on the homologous chromosomes are called alleles. Alleles are different forms of the same gene. They are located at the same position (locus) on homologous chromosomes. Therefore, the correct answer is 'alleles'.

subject: Biology
q: When a red-flowered four o'clock plant is crossed with a white-flowered one, what color are the flowers in the F1 generation due to incomplete dominance?
explanation: Incomplete dominance occurs when neither allele is completely dominant, resulting in a blending of traits. In this case, crossing red and white flowers produces pink flowers in the F1 generation, which is an intermediate phenotype.

topics.title: HEREDITY : INHERITANCE OF CHARACTERS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following adaptation is not found in fruits and seeds dispersed by the wind?
explanation: The adaptation not found in fruits and seeds dispersed by the wind is 'Spongy coats'. Wind-dispersed fruits and seeds are typically small and light, often with wings or other structures to catch the wind, or with turfs of hair that can float on air currents. Spongy coats are not a typical adaptation for wind dispersal. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Spongy coats'.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : ADAPTIVE COLOURATION AND ITS FUNCTIONS

subject: Biology
q: Which among the options given is the recently evolved structure in animals?
explanation: Feathers are the most recently evolved structures among the options given. They are unique to birds and are a key characteristic that distinguishes birds from other animal groups. Hair, cilium, and scales are found in a wider range of animal groups, indicating they evolved earlier.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following is true about the movement of the fore arm?
options: Bending the arm , the biceps relax and triceps contract
Bending the arm, the biceps contract and triceps relax
Straightening the arm, the biceps contract and triceps relax
straightening the arm, both the biceps and triceps contract"
explanation: When you bend your arm, your biceps muscle contracts and your triceps muscle relaxes. This is because the biceps and triceps muscles work in opposition to each other. When one contracts, the other relaxes. This is known as antagonistic muscle action.

topics.title: SUPPORT AND MOVEMENT : TYPES AND FUNCTIONS OF THE SKELETON

subject: Biology
q: The part of the brain the is responsible for the control of voluntary movement and interpretations of sensations is known as
explanation: The cerebrum is the largest part of the brain and is responsible for voluntary movements and the interpretation of sensations. The cerebellum is involved in the coordination of movements and balance, the optic lobe is involved in vision, and the olfactory lobe is involved in the sense of smell.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following roles is not performed by the mammalian skeletal system?
explanation: The mammalian skeleton provides support, protects organs, facilitates movement through attachment to muscles, and produces blood cells in the bone marrow. However, it does not conduct gas exchange, which is a function of the respiratory system.

topics.title: SUPPORT AND MOVEMENT : TYPES AND FUNCTIONS OF THE SKELETON

subject: Biology
q: Which part of the brain enables humans to consciously decide to move their arms and also helps them interpret touch, taste, and sound?
explanation: The cerebrum is responsible for voluntary movements and for interpreting sensory inputs such as touch, taste, and sound. The cerebellum mainly coordinates movement and balance, the medulla oblongata controls involuntary actions like heartbeat and breathing, and the optic lobe processes visual information.

subject: Biology
q: Deamination of amino acids in the liver produces
explanation: Deamination is the process by which amino acids are broken down if not needed for protein synthesis. The liver carries out this process, producing ammonia, which is then converted into a less toxic substance called urea. This urea is then excreted from the body via the kidneys.

subject: Biology
q: The persistent presence of glucose in a person's urine indicates the malfunctioning of the
explanation: The persistent presence of glucose in a person's urine is a condition known as 'glycosuria'. This condition is often associated with diabetes, a disease that is caused by the malfunctioning of the pancreas. The pancreas is responsible for producing insulin, a hormone that regulates the amount of glucose in the blood. When the pancreas is not functioning properly, it may not produce enough insulin, leading to an excess amount of glucose in the blood. Some of this excess glucose is then excreted in the urine.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following is not an evidence of evolution?
explanation: Polymorphism is not an evidence of evolution. Polymorphism is a term used in genetics to describe the occurrence of two or more different forms or 'phenotypes' in the population of a species. While polymorphism can be a result of genetic variation, which is a driving force of evolution, it is not in itself an evidence of evolution. On the other hand, fossil records, comparative embryology, and comparative anatomy are all evidences of evolution as they show changes in species over time or similarities between different species that suggest a common ancestor.

subject: Biology
q: Lamarck's theory of evolution is based on the fact that
options: organisms pass on acquired characteristics to their offspring
characteristics of organisms are determined by their creator
characteristics of organisms are the sum of their parents' characteristics
explanation: Lamarck's theory of evolution, also known as Lamarckism, is based on the idea that an organism can change during its lifetime in response to its environment, and those changes are then passed on to its offspring. This is known as the principle of 'inheritance of acquired characteristics'. However, this theory has been largely discredited by modern genetics, which shows that characteristics acquired or lost during an organism's lifetime do not change its genes and therefore cannot be passed on to the next generation.

subject: Biology
q: Wild life conservation is enhanced by
options: suppressing the activities of wildlife conservation agencies
explanation: Wildlife conservation is enhanced by discouraging the elimination of endangered species. This is because the main goal of wildlife conservation is to ensure that nature will be around for future generations to enjoy and to recognize the importance of wildlife and wilderness lands to humans. Therefore, discouraging the elimination of endangered species is a direct way to enhance wildlife conservation.

topics.title: HUMANS AND ENVIRONMENT : CONSERVATION OF NATURAL RESOURCES

subject: Biology
q: Where are epiphytic plants most commonly found growing?
explanation: Epiphytic plants grow on other plants, especially tree branches, where they can access better light without harming the host. They are not aquatic or ground-dwelling plants, so freshwater, marsh, or forest floor habitats do not describe their typical environment.

topics.title: NATURAL HABITAT : AQUATIC TERRESTRIAL AND ARBOREAL

subject: Biology
q: Breathing movement in mammals is acompanied by regular movements of the diaphragm and the
explanation: Breathing movements in mammals are accompanied by regular movements of the diaphragm and the intercostal muscles. The diaphragm is a sheet of muscle that separates the chest from the abdomen. When it contracts, it flattens out, increasing the volume of the chest and drawing air into the lungs. The intercostal muscles are located between the ribs and assist in the expansion and contraction of the chest cavity to facilitate breathing.

topics.title: RESPIRATION : THE MECHANISM OF GASEOUS EXCHANGE IN PLANTS AND MAMMALS

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following statement is not correct of respiration?
options: Oxygen combines with haemoglobin at the respiratory surface.
Carbon dioxide produced in the tissues is removed by the process of osmosis
There are no special organs for respiration in plants
explanation: The statement 'Carbon dioxide produced in the tissues is removed by the process of osmosis' is not correct. Osmosis is the process of movement of water molecules from a region of lower concentration to a region of higher concentration through a semi-permeable membrane. It does not involve the removal of carbon dioxide. Carbon dioxide is removed from the body through the process of exhalation, which is a part of respiration.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following methods of reproduction is common to paramecium and amoeba?
explanation: Both paramecium and amoeba reproduce by binary fission. Binary fission is a type of asexual reproduction where a parent cell splits into two identical daughter cells. This method of reproduction is common in single-celled organisms like paramecium and amoeba.

subject: Biology
q: Amoeba moves by the means of
explanation: Amoeba moves by the means of pseudopodia. Pseudopodia, also known as 'false feet', are temporary projections of the cell. The amoeba moves by extending its pseudopodia and flowing its cytoplasm into the pseudopodia.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following is the most abundant substance found in the living part of a cell, the protoplasm.
explanation: The most abundant substance found in the living part of a cell, the protoplasm, is water. Protoplasm is the living part of the cell, which includes the cytoplasm and the nucleus. It is composed of water, proteins, lipids, carbohydrates, and inorganic salts. Among these, water is the most abundant, making up about 70-90% of the cell.

topics.title: LIVING ORGANISMS : CELL STRUCTURE AND FUNCTIONS OF CELL COMPONENTS

subject: Biology
q: The hierarchy of organic evolution in plant progressiveness is
explanation: The hierarchy of organic evolution in plant progressiveness is schizophyta, thallophyta, bryophyta, pteridophyta, and spermatophyta. This sequence represents the evolution of plants from simple to complex forms. Schizophyta are the simplest, unicellular organisms. Thallophyta are a little more complex, with a thallus-like structure. Bryophyta are non-vascular plants, while pteridophyta are the first true vascular plants. Spermatophyta are the most advanced, with seeds for reproduction.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following organisms cannot exist freely on its own?
explanation: Plasmodium cannot exist freely on its own. Plasmodium is a genus of parasitic protozoa that cause malaria in their hosts. They require a host to complete their life cycle and cannot survive independently. On the other hand, Chlamydomonas, Amoeba, and Paramecium are all free-living organisms.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following substances passes through the root by osmosis?
explanation: Osmosis is the process by which water molecules move from an area of high concentration to an area of low concentration through a semi-permeable membrane. In plants, water is absorbed from the soil into the root cells by osmosis.

subject: Biology
q: Members of which arthropod class are characterized by having no antennae?
explanation: Arachnids (e.g., spiders, scorpions, ticks, and mites) lack antennae, which distinguishes them from other arthropod groups. Instead, they possess specialized appendages called chelicerae and pedipalps for feeding and sensing. In contrast, crustaceans, centipedes, and millipedes have antennae that help them detect their surroundings.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : STRUCTURAL ADAPTATIONS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following organism has a hydrostatic skeleton?
explanation: A hydrostatic skeleton is a structure found in many soft-bodied animals, including earthworms, that allows them to maintain their shape and move. It is formed by a fluid-filled compartment within the body, the coelom, which is surrounded by muscles. When the muscles contract, they exert pressure on the fluid, which gives the animal its shape and allows it to move.

topics.title: SUPPORT AND MOVEMENT : SUPPORTING TISSUES IN ANIMALS

subject: Biology
q: Organisms that can be found in a fresh water pond are
options: water beetles, shrimps, water snails and crab.
water lily, fish, scorpion and dragon fly larva.
pond skater, waterlily, shark and mosquito larva.
explanation: Tadpoles, water boatmen, and leeches are all organisms that can be found in a freshwater pond. Crabs are typically found in saltwater environments, but there are also freshwater crabs. Therefore, the first option is the most accurate.

topics.title: ECOLOGY : FACTORS AFFECTING THE DISTRIBUTION OF ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following traits is not a morphological variation in humans?
explanation: PTC tasting is a genetic trait, not a morphological one. Morphological traits refer to physical characteristics, such as color, size, and fingerprint patterns. PTC (phenylthiocarbamide) tasting is a genetic trait that varies among individuals. Some people can taste PTC as bitter, while others cannot taste it at all. This trait is controlled by a single gene with two alleles.

subject: Biology
q: When a person moves from a dark room into bright light, the pupils becomes.
explanation: When a person moves from a dark room into bright light, the pupils become smaller. This is a reflex action to protect the retina from excessive light. The muscles in the iris contract, reducing the size of the pupil and limiting the amount of light that enters the eye.

topics.title: COORDINATION AND CONTROL : THE SENSE ORGANS

subject: Biology
q: The structure of the ear that is responsible for balancing is the
explanation: The semi-circular canals in the inner ear are responsible for maintaining balance in the body. They are filled with a fluid that moves when your head changes position. This movement of fluid stimulates hair cells inside the canals, sending signals to the brain about the body's motion and position.

topics.title: COORDINATION AND CONTROL : THE SENSE ORGANS

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following stimuli are not perceived through the skin of mammals?
explanation: The skin of mammals is not capable of perceiving light. The skin contains receptors for touch, pressure, pain, and temperature, but not for light. Light is perceived by the photoreceptor cells in the retina of the eye.

topics.title: COORDINATION AND CONTROL : THE SENSE ORGANS

subject: Biology
q: The factors that produces character in living organisms are ......
explanation: Genes are the factors that produce character in living organisms. They are segments of DNA located on chromosomes and contain the instructions for the development and functioning of all the cells in the body. Each gene carries a specific trait that can be passed from parents to offspring.

subject: Biology
q: In humans, sex is determined by
options: a dominant gene for maleness present in male
two similar sex chromosomes in the male and dissimilar in the female
the difference in the nature of the X and Y chromosomes in the male
explanation: In humans, sex is determined by the difference in the nature of the X and Y chromosomes in the male. Females have two of the same kind of sex chromosome (XX), while males have two distinct sex chromosomes (XY). The presence of the Y chromosome determines maleness.

topics.title: HEREDITY : PROBABILITY IN GENETICS AND SEX DETERMINATION

subject: Biology
q: If a heterozygous red flowered plant,(Rr) was self fertilized, the offspring would be expected to be
explanation: If a heterozygous red flowered plant (Rr) was self fertilized, the offspring would be expected to be both red flowered and white flowered plants. This is because the heterozygous plant (Rr) can produce two types of gametes (R and r). When self-fertilized, it can produce RR (red), Rr (red), rR (red), and rr (white) offspring. This is based on Mendel's law of segregation.

topics.title: HEREDITY : INHERITANCE OF CHARACTERS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following processes are involved in a water cycle?
explanation: The water cycle involves the processes of evaporation, condensation, and precipitation. Evaporation is the process by which water changes from a liquid to a gas or vapor. Condensation is the process where water vapor becomes liquid. Precipitation is water released from clouds in the form of rain, freezing rain, sleet, snow, or hail.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following statements about feeding relationships is correct?
options: In a food web on land, grass hopper feeds on praying mantis and on predator bugs
In a food chain on land, green plant is the producer
In an aquatic food web, various copepods feed on mollusc larvae and on sea eel
In an aquatic food chain, mollusc larvae are the producer
explanation: In a typical food chain on land, green plants are the producers. Producers are organisms that produce their own food through the process of photosynthesis. They form the base of the food chain, providing energy for all other organisms in the ecosystem.

topics.title: ECOLOGY : FOOD CHAINS FOOD WEBS AND TROPHIC LEVELS

subject: Biology
q:
Which of the following correctly lists events that occur during photosynthesis?

I. Water molecules are split to release oxygen
II. Carbon dioxide is used to form glucose
III. Light energy is captured by chlorophyll
IV. Heat energy is released as the main product
explanation: During photosynthesis, chlorophyll captures light energy (III), which is used to split water molecules, releasing oxygen (I). Carbon dioxide is then fixed into glucose molecules (II). Heat energy is not the main product of photosynthesis; instead, chemical energy in the form of glucose is produced.

subject: Biology
q: Which of these methods is best for accurately comparing the growth of two plants?
explanation: Measuring dry weight is the most accurate way to compare growth because it excludes water content, which can fluctuate depending on environmental conditions. Fresh weight, height, and leaf number can be misleading since they are affected by factors other than actual biomass.

subject: Biology
q: Soil permeability is best described as
options: The ease with which liquids can flow through soil
The rate at which soil produces organic matter
The amount of nutrients stored in soil particles
The process of water moving upward in soil pores
