explanation: Soil permeability describes how easily water or other liquids can pass through soil. It depends on the size, arrangement, and connectivity of the pores between soil particles. Sandy soils, with large pore spaces, have high permeability, while clay soils, with tiny pores, have low permeability.

topics.title: SOIL : CHARACTERISTICS OF DIFFERENT TYPES OF SOIL

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following farming activities can cause loss of soil fertility?
explanation: Intensive cropping can cause loss of soil fertility. This is because it involves growing the same crop on the same land year after year, which can deplete the soil of certain nutrients. The other options like mulching, compost application, and terracing are all practices that can actually improve soil fertility.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following organisms is a primary consumer in a grassland ecosystem?
explanation: Primary consumers are herbivores that feed directly on producers (plants). In a grassland ecosystem, a grasshopper feeds on grass, making it a primary consumer. Lions and eagles are carnivores (secondary or tertiary consumers), while grass is a producer.

topics.title: ECOLOGY : ENERGY FLOW IN THE ECOSYSTEM

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following world biomes is found in Nigeria?
explanation: Nigeria is located in West Africa and it is characterized by different types of vegetation. One of the prominent vegetation types in Nigeria is the Sahel savanna. The Sahel savanna is a type of grassland biome that is found in tropical and subtropical regions, characterized by tall grasses and a variety of wildlife. It is not a desert, tundra, or afro-alphine biome.

subject: Biology
q: Which membrane in mammals surrounds all other embryonic membranes and helps form the placenta?
explanation: The chorion is the outermost embryonic membrane in mammals. It encloses the embryo and the other membranes, playing a key role in placenta formation and nutrient–waste exchange with the mother. The amnion protects the embryo with fluid, the blastocyst lining is part of early cell development, and the umbilical covering shields the cord but is not the outermost membrane.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following correctly explains gestation in mammals. Gestation is the period ......
explanation: Gestation in mammals is the period of development from the time of fertilization of the egg (zygote) to birth. It involves the growth and development of the fetus in the uterus. The other options do not correctly define gestation.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following organisms is viviparous?
explanation: Viviparous organisms are those that give birth to live young ones instead of laying eggs. Among the options provided, only sheep fall under this category. Tapeworms, snails, and grasshoppers are all oviparous, meaning they lay eggs.

subject: Biology
q: Which type of nerve cell carries information from sense organs to the brain or spinal cord?
explanation: Sensory neurones transmit impulses from receptors in sense organs (like the skin, eyes, or ears) to the central nervous system. This allows the body to detect and respond to changes in the environment. Relay and motor neurones handle other parts of the pathway but do not collect stimuli directly.

subject: Biology
q: The endocrine gland which co-ordinate the action of other glands and is also associated with the nervous system is the
explanation: The pituitary gland is often referred to as the 'master gland' because it controls the function of most other endocrine glands. It is also closely associated with the nervous system as it is connected to the hypothalamus region of the brain, which sends signals to the pituitary gland to either stimulate or inhibit hormone production.

subject: Biology
q: Siphons and gills are structure in are structures in aquatic organisms used for
explanation: Siphons and gills are structures found in aquatic organisms that are used for gaseous exchange. Gills are specialized organs containing filaments, which further divide into lamellae. These structures have a rich blood supply and are used for the exchange of gases (oxygen and carbon dioxide) between the organism and the surrounding water. Siphons, on the other hand, are tube-like structures in some aquatic organisms that facilitate the intake of water into the body where gaseous exchange can occur.

subject: Biology
q: At which of the following stages of mitosis do the two daughter chromosomes separate completely?
explanation: The correct stage of mitosis where the two daughter chromosomes separate completely is Anaphase. Mitosis is a process of cell division that results in two genetically identical daughter cells developing from a single parent cell. It is divided into several stages: Prophase, Metaphase, Anaphase, and Telophase. During Anaphase, the sister chromatids separate into individual chromosomes and are moved apart.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following pairs of scientist discovered the Rhesus factor in human blood?
explanation: The Rhesus factor in human blood was discovered by Karl Landsteiner and Alexander S. Wiener. Landsteiner and Levine are therefore the correct pair of scientists. The Rhesus factor is an antigen that exists on the surface of red blood cells in most people (about 85% of humans). It is important in blood transfusion and pregnancy, as it can lead to complications if the mother is Rh- and the baby is Rh+.

topics.title: HEREDITY : APPLICATION OF THE PRINCIPLES OF HEREDITY

subject: Biology
q: Which of the statement is true about fingerprint?
options: may be exactly the same for two individuals
explanation: Fingerprints are unique to each individual and remain consistent throughout a person's lifetime. They do not change with age, so the patterns you have as a child will remain the same in adulthood. This makes fingerprints a reliable way to identify individuals. The other options are incorrect because fingerprints do not correlate with blood group, they are not only prominent at puberty, and they are not exactly the same for two individuals.

topics.title: HEREDITY : INHERITANCE OF CHARACTERS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: When a plant with genotype Rr (red flowers) self-pollinates, what flower color ratio would be expected in the offspring?
explanation: A heterozygous plant (Rr) has one dominant allele (R) for red flowers and one recessive allele (r) for white flowers. When it self-pollinates, the offspring genotypes typically follow a 1:2:1 ratio — 25% RR (red), 50% Rr (red), and 25% rr (white). Therefore, both red and white flowered plants appear in the offspring.

topics.title: HEREDITY : PROBABILITY IN GENETICS AND SEX DETERMINATION

subject: Biology
q: How many haploid number of chromosomes can be found in a man?
explanation: In humans, the haploid number of chromosomes is 23. This is the number of chromosomes in a sperm or egg cell, and it is half the diploid number, which is the total number of chromosomes in other human cells (46). The other options are incorrect because they do not match the correct haploid number of chromosomes in humans.

subject: Biology
q: In human joints such as the knee or elbow, what fluid helps smooth movement by reducing friction between bones?
explanation: Synovial fluid is a thick, slippery liquid found inside the cavities of synovial joints. It lubricates the ends of bones, reduces friction during movement, and nourishes the cartilage. Without it, joint motion would be stiff and painful.

topics.title: SUPPORT AND MOVEMENT : TYPES AND FUNCTIONS OF THE SKELETON

subject: Biology
q: During the action of lifting a cup to your mouth, which muscles are active and which are relaxed?
explanation: When you lift (flex) your forearm, the biceps contract to pull the forearm upward while the triceps relax. These muscles work as antagonistic pairs—when one shortens, the other lengthens to allow smooth movement.

topics.title: SUPPORT AND MOVEMENT : SUPPORTING TISSUES IN ANIMALS

subject: Biology
q: At what level of organisation is Amoeba?
explanation: Amoebas are unicellular, shape-changing organisms. There are thousands of different types of amoeba, and they are found mostly in aqueous environments. Amoebas are usually microscopic, but some types found deep in the ocean can be as large as 20 centimeters in diameter.

topics.title: LIVING ORGANISMS : CELL STRUCTURE AND FUNCTIONS OF CELL COMPONENTS

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following cell organelle is the site for the production ATP?
explanation: The mitochondrion is the site for the production of ATP (Adenosine Triphosphate). ATP is the main source of energy for most cellular processes. The process of ATP production in the mitochondrion is known as cellular respiration, where glucose and oxygen are used to produce ATP, carbon dioxide, and water.

topics.title: LIVING ORGANISMS : CELL STRUCTURE AND FUNCTIONS OF CELL COMPONENTS

subject: Biology
q: Which device is used to measure the rate of transpiration in a potted plant?
explanation: A potometer measures the rate of transpiration by estimating the amount of water a plant absorbs, which is closely related to the water lost through leaves. Barometers measure air pressure, thermometers measure temperature, and anemometers measure wind speed.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following processes removes carbon from the atmosphere?
explanation: Photosynthesis is the process that removes carbon from the atmosphere. During photosynthesis, plants take in carbon dioxide from the atmosphere and use it to produce glucose and oxygen. The glucose is used by the plant for energy, and the oxygen is released back into the atmosphere.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following explains why it is so difficult to see with dim light?
options: Initially the eyes cannot operate in dim light
The choroid reflects on the light that enters the eye
Little light reaches the retina and so the cones may not be stimulated at all.
Dim light causes the pupil to close up so that not much light enters the eye.
explanation: In dim light conditions, the amount of light that reaches the retina is reduced. The cones, which are responsible for color vision and function best in relatively bright light, may not be stimulated at all. This is why it can be difficult to see in dim light. The rods, which are more sensitive to light and do not mediate color vision, take over. However, they are not as sharp as the cones.

topics.title: COORDINATION AND CONTROL : THE SENSE ORGANS

subject: Biology
q: Courtship behaviour in animals may include all the following except
options: production of odour by female to attract the male
basking in the sun by a lizard
explanation: Courtship behavior in animals is a set of display behaviors in which an animal, usually a male, attempts to attract a mate and signal his suitability as a partner. These behaviors often include vocalizations, displays of beauty or strength, and specific behaviors such as the howling of a dog and croaking of a toad. Basking in the sun by a lizard is not a courtship behavior, but a way for the lizard to regulate its body temperature.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : BEHAVIOURAL ADAPTATIONS IN SOCIAL ANIMALS

subject: Biology
q: The oxygen that is given during photosynthesis process is gathered from .........
explanation: During photosynthesis, plants take in carbon dioxide (CO2) and water (H2O). Through a series of reactions, they transform these inputs into glucose (a sugar) and oxygen (O2). The oxygen that is released during photosynthesis comes from the water that the plant absorbs, not from carbon dioxide, sunlight, or chlorophyll.

subject: Biology
q: What happens during inspiration?
options: The diaphragm flattens out, and the ribs are raised
The diaphragm is raised, and the ribs are lowered
The diaphragm flattens out, and the ribs are lowered
The diaphragm raised, and the ribs are raised
explanation: During inspiration, the diaphragm contracts and moves downward (flattens out) and the intercostal muscles contract to expand the rib cage, which increases the volume of the thoracic cavity. This decrease in pressure allows air to flow into the lungs.

topics.title: RESPIRATION : THE MECHANISM OF GASEOUS EXCHANGE IN PLANTS AND MAMMALS

subject: Biology
q: Which instrument is used to measure speed of wind?
explanation: An anemometer is a device used for measuring the speed of wind, and is also a common weather station instrument. The term is derived from the Greek word anemos, which means wind, and is used to describe any wind speed instrument used in meteorology.

subject: Biology
q: The following factors can contribute towards the conservation of natural resources except
explanation: Deforestation is the removal or clearing of forests, often resulting in damage to the quality of the land. It does not contribute to the conservation of natural resources, rather it leads to loss of biodiversity and increases greenhouse gas emissions. The other options like afforestation, contour ploughing and establishment of game reserves are all strategies used to conserve natural resources.

topics.title: HUMANS AND ENVIRONMENT : CONSERVATION OF NATURAL RESOURCES

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following cell organelle eliminates water from the body of the protozoa ?
explanation: The contractile vacuole is a cell organelle in protozoa that eliminates water. It functions in osmoregulation by collecting and pumping water out of the cell to prevent the cell from bursting. This process is known as contractile vacuole osmoregulation.

topics.title: LIVING ORGANISMS : CELL STRUCTURE AND FUNCTIONS OF CELL COMPONENTS

subject: Biology
q: The examples of autotrophic organism include the following except.
explanation: Paramecium is a heterotrophic organism, not autotrophic. Autotrophic organisms are capable of synthesizing their own food from inorganic substances using light or chemical energy. Euglena, Chlamydomonas, and Spirogyra are examples of autotrophic organisms because they contain chlorophyll and can perform photosynthesis.

subject: Biology
q: The part of a leguminous plant where bacteria like Azobacter can be found is
explanation: Azotobacter is a type of bacteria that can be found in the root nodules of leguminous plants. These bacteria are known for their ability to fix atmospheric nitrogen into a form that plants can use. This symbiotic relationship benefits the plant by providing it with a source of nitrogen, which is essential for the synthesis of proteins and nucleic acids.

subject: Biology
q: If a laboratory test shows sugar consistently present in urine, which organ is most likely not working properly?
explanation: Consistent sugar in urine (glycosuria) is usually a sign of diabetes mellitus, which occurs when the pancreas does not produce enough insulin or the body cannot use it effectively. Insulin regulates blood sugar levels, and when it's lacking, excess glucose is filtered into the urine.

subject: Biology
q: The smallest branches on the cell body of a neurone are called
explanation: In a neuron, the smallest branches on the cell body are called dendrites. Dendrites are responsible for receiving signals from other neurons and transmitting these signals to the cell body. The dendrites increase the surface area of the neuron, allowing it to receive signals from multiple other neurons at the same time.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following processes is a form of sexual reproduction?
explanation: Conjugation in spirogyra is a form of sexual reproduction. In this process, two filaments of spirogyra come into contact and form conjugation tubes. The protoplast of one cell then passes through the tube and fuses with the protoplast of the other cell to form a zygote. This zygote eventually develops into a new organism. The other options listed are forms of asexual reproduction.

subject: Biology
q: In mammals, which temporary structure enables the transfer of nutrients and oxygen from the mother to the foetus?
explanation: The placenta is a temporary yet vital organ in mammals that facilitates the exchange of oxygen, nutrients, and waste products between the mother and the developing fetus. It connects to the fetus through the umbilical cord and plays a central role in sustaining pregnancy.

subject: Biology
q: The fusion of genetic materials between two Spirogyra filaments occurs during
explanation: Conjugation in Spirogyra is a sexual reproduction process where filaments exchange and combine genetic material before forming zygospores. Budding in yeast, binary fission in Amoeba, and rhizome production in ginger are all examples of asexual reproduction.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following organisms exhibit extracellular digestion?
explanation: Moulds, a type of fungi, exhibit extracellular digestion. They secrete enzymes into their environment, breaking down large food molecules into smaller ones, which they then absorb. The other options listed do not exhibit extracellular digestion.

subject: Biology
q: A major difference between plants and animals is that
options: there is free movement in animals while it is limited in plants
there is indefinite growth in animals only
animals consume inorganic materials as food while plants do not
animals give up carbon(iv)oxide as a waste product while plants do not
explanation: The major difference between plants and animals is that animals have the ability to move freely while plants are stationary. This is due to the fact that animals have muscles and a nervous system that allow them to move in response to stimuli, while plants are rooted in one place and can only grow towards or away from stimuli.

subject: Biology
q: Raw materials required by green plants to manufacture their food are
explanation: Green plants utilize simple inorganic materials to prepare their own food(glucose) for energy. The raw materials used are carbon dioxide and water.

subject: Biology
q: The long necks and legs of giraffes were some of the basis of
explanation: The long necks and legs of giraffes were some of the basis of Lamarck's Theory of Evolution. According to this theory, an organism can change during its lifetime in response to its environment, and those changes are then passed onto its offspring. In the case of giraffes, Lamarck believed that they acquired their long necks and legs by stretching to reach leaves high in the trees, and then passed these traits onto their offspring.

subject: Biology
q: Growth include the following processes except
explanation: Growth in biology refers to the process where an organism increases in size and number of cells. This involves processes such as differentiation, where cells become specialized in their function, enlargement, where cells increase in size, and mitosis, where cells divide to increase in number. Meiosis, on the other hand, is a type of cell division that results in four daughter cells each with half the number of chromosomes of the parent cell. It is involved in sexual reproduction and does not contribute to growth.

subject: Biology
q: Which of these vegetation zones exists in Nigeria?
explanation: The Sudan savanna is a tropical grassland biome found in parts of northern Nigeria. It features tall grasses and scattered trees adapted to seasonal rainfall. This zone lies between the Sahel savanna to the north and the Guinea savanna to the south. The other options — temperate grassland, coniferous forest, and Mediterranean scrub — are not part of Nigeria's natural vegetation.

subject: Biology
q: All of the following are measures that promote the protection of natural resources except
options: Allowing unlimited fishing in rivers and lakes
explanation: Protection of natural resources involves actions that prevent their overuse or destruction, such as enforcing hunting laws, establishing protected areas, and carrying out reforestation. Allowing unlimited fishing in rivers and lakes is an example of unregulated exploitation, which can lead to resource depletion and is not a method of conservation.

topics.title: HUMANS AND ENVIRONMENT : CONSERVATION OF NATURAL RESOURCES

subject: Biology
q: When viewing an object that is close to the human eye, the
explanation: When viewing an object that is close to the human eye, the eye lens becomes fat. This is because the ciliary muscles contract, which reduces the tension on the suspensory ligaments holding the lens in place. This allows the lens to become more convex (or 'fatter'), increasing its refractive power to focus on the close object.

topics.title: COORDINATION AND CONTROL : THE SENSE ORGANS

subject: Biology
q: A basic similarity between nervous and hormonal systems is that both
explanation: Both the nervous system and the hormonal system involve chemical transmission. The nervous system uses neurotransmitters to transmit signals between neurons, while the hormonal system uses hormones to transmit signals throughout the body. The other options are not universally true for both systems.

topics.title: COORDINATION AND CONTROL : HORMONAL CONTROL~~COORDINATION AND CONTROL : NERVOUS COORDINATION

subject: Biology
q: A neurone that transmits impulse directly from sensory cells is called
explanation: A neurone that transmits impulse directly from sensory cells is called an afferent neurone. 'Afferent' means 'inwards' or 'towards'. So, afferent neurones carry nerve impulses from sensory receptors towards the central nervous system. This is in contrast to efferent neurones, which carry nerve impulses away from the central nervous system towards effectors such as muscles or glands.

subject: Biology
q: At the early stage of rise of water in sandy, clayey and loamy soils, it is observed that the rise of water is faster in
explanation: Sandy soil has larger particles and larger spaces between the particles, allowing water to move through it more quickly than through other types of soil.

topics.title: SOIL : CHARACTERISTICS OF DIFFERENT TYPES OF SOIL

subject: Biology
q: The farming practice by which an exhausted farm land is left for a number of years before cultivation is known as
explanation: Bush fallowing is a farming method, where a piece of land is cultivated for several years and left to fallow or rest of some years to allow it regain its fertility.

topics.title: SOIL : CHARACTERISTICS OF DIFFERENT TYPES OF SOIL

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following takes place when the temperature of a person's body rises above 370C?
explanation: Sweating is a cooling mechanism that helps to lower body temperature. Vasodilation is the widening of blood vessels, which allows more blood to flow near the surface of the skin, where it can be cooled by the air.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following does not occur during inhalation in mammals?
explanation: During inhalation in mammals, the diaphragm contracts and flattens, not become dome-shaped. This contraction increases the volume of the thoracic cavity, which decreases the pressure inside the lungs, allowing air to flow in.

topics.title: RESPIRATION : THE MECHANISM OF GASEOUS EXCHANGE IN PLANTS AND MAMMALS

subject: Biology
q: Which animal relies on a fluid-filled cavity and muscle contractions for its movement?
explanation: The earthworm uses a hydrostatic skeleton, which consists of a fluid-filled cavity surrounded by muscles. By contracting these muscles against the fluid, the earthworm changes shape and moves. Other animals like tilapia have bony skeletons, while hydra and mosquito larvae have different support structures.

topics.title: SUPPORT AND MOVEMENT : SUPPORTING TISSUES IN ANIMALS

subject: Biology
q: The site for production of ATP in a cells is
explanation: The mitochondrion is the site for production of ATP in a cell. ATP, or Adenosine triphosphate, is the main source of energy for most cellular processes. The process of ATP production in the mitochondrion is called oxidative phosphorylation.

topics.title: LIVING ORGANISMS : CELL STRUCTURE AND FUNCTIONS OF CELL COMPONENTS

subject: Biology
q: Which sensation cannot be detected by the skin in mammals?
explanation: Mammalian skin contains sensory receptors that detect pressure, heat, and pain, but it cannot detect light. Light perception is the function of the eyes, not the skin.

topics.title: COORDINATION AND CONTROL : THE SENSE ORGANS

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following best illustrates a conscious and deliberate human activity?
options: Withdrawing a hand from a hot object
Typing a legal opinion on a computer
explanation: Typing a legal opinion on a computer involves thought, awareness, and intention, which makes it a voluntary action. Unlike reflexes or involuntary actions (such as sneezing or breathing during sleep), voluntary actions are performed under the control of the brain and require conscious effort.

subject: Biology
q: The essential part of a flower that is meant for pollination and fruit formation is .......
explanation: The pistil or gynoecium is the essential part of a flower that is meant for pollination and fruit formation. It is the female reproductive part of the flower and consists of the ovary, style, and stigma. The ovary contains the ovules which, after fertilization, develop into seeds. The corolla and receptacles are not directly involved in pollination or fruit formation.

topics.title: INTERNAL STRUCTURE OF A FLOWERING PLANT : LEAF

subject: Biology
q: A termite with a relatively large head and powerful jaws is likely to be a
explanation: A termite with a relatively large head and powerful jaws is likely to be a soldier. Soldier termites are characterized by their large heads and powerful jaws which they use to defend the colony from invaders. The king and queen are reproductive members of the colony, but they do not have the same physical characteristics as the soldiers.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : BEHAVIOURAL ADAPTATIONS IN SOCIAL ANIMALS

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following is the least adaptive feature for arboreal life?
explanation: The least adaptive feature for arboreal life is 'counter shading of coat colour'. Arboreal animals are those that live in trees. Features like possession of four limbs, possession of claws, and development of long tails are all adaptive features that help these animals to climb, grasp branches, and maintain balance. Counter shading of coat colour, on the other hand, is more related to camouflage and does not directly aid in arboreal locomotion.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : ADAPTIVE COLOURATION AND ITS FUNCTIONS

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following is the best indicator of biomass increase in plants during growth?
explanation: The most reliable estimate of growth is measuring changes in 'Dry weight'. Growth is an increase in physical size and can be measured in various ways. However, measuring changes in dry weight is considered the most reliable because it is not affected by water content, which can vary significantly and affect other measurements like volume, length, and surface area.

subject: Biology
q: The group mollusca is characterised by the presence of ......
explanation: The group Mollusca is characterized by the presence of soft, non-segmented bodies. Molluscs are a diverse group of invertebrates that includes squids, octopuses, clams, snails, and slugs. They are characterized by a soft body that in many species is protected by a hard shell. They do not have joint appendages, star-shaped bodies, or vertebrae.

subject: Biology
q: The structural similarities in Paramecium and Euglena are in the
options: presence of micro and mega nucleiin both
presence of anterior and posterior contractile vacuole
explanation:
Both Paramecium and Euglena share a similar body shape that is characterized by having a blunt anterior end and a pointed posterior end. This similarity in body shape aids in their movement and navigation through their respective environments.

Reasons why other options are incorrect:
Shape of locomotory organelles:
- This is incorrect because Paramecium uses cilia for locomotion, while Euglena uses a flagellum. The cilia and flagellum have different shapes and structures, although they both serve the function of movement.
Presence of micro and mega nuclei both:
- This is incorrect because only Paramecium has both micro and macronuclei. Euglena has a single nucleus and does not have a distinction between micro and macronuclei.
Presence of anterior and posterior contractile vacuole:
- This is incorrect because the presence of contractile vacuoles is a feature of Paramecium but not a distinguishing structural similarity with Euglena. Euglena has a single contractile vacuole, which is typically not located in both the anterior and posterior regions.

Thus, the correct answer is Blunt anterior and pointed posterior, as it accurately describes a structural similarity between Paramecium and Euglena.

subject: Biology
q: How does water primarily enter the root hair cells of plants?
explanation: Water enters root hair cells mainly through osmosis, where it moves from the moist soil (higher water concentration) into the root cells (lower water concentration) across a semi-permeable membrane. Active transport and diffusion involve different substances, while transpiration is the loss of water vapor from leaves.

subject: Biology
q: The haploid number of chromosomes in humans is
explanation: The haploid number of chromosomes in humans is 23. This is the number of chromosomes in a gamete (sperm or egg cell). This is half the diploid number, which is the total number of chromosomes in most other cells of the body. When a sperm and egg combine during fertilization, they create a new cell with the diploid number of 46 chromosomes.

subject: Biology
q: When a single cell completes mitosis, how many identical daughter cells result?
explanation: Mitosis is the process of nuclear division that produces two genetically identical daughter cells from one parent cell. Each daughter cell has the same number and type of chromosomes as the original. This is essential for growth, tissue repair, and asexual reproduction in many organisms.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following correctly describes human fingerprints?
explanation: Human fingerprints are formed before birth during fetal development and remain unchanged for life, except for damage to the skin that reaches the deeper layers. Even identical twins have different fingerprints, making them a reliable tool for personal identification.

subject: Biology
q: Who were the scientists that identified the blood antigen responsible for the Rhesus factor?
explanation: Karl Landsteiner and Alexander Wiener discovered the Rhesus factor in 1940 while conducting experiments involving blood from Rhesus monkeys. This antigen plays a vital role in blood transfusions and pregnancy, especially in Rh incompatibility cases. The other scientists are known for work in genetics, microbiology, and DNA structure, not blood classification.

topics.title: HEREDITY : APPLICATION OF THE PRINCIPLES OF HEREDITY

subject: Biology
q: In living cells, ribosomes are primarily responsible for which of the following activities?
explanation: Ribosomes are the cellular structures where proteins are assembled from amino acids based on genetic instructions. These proteins are vital for cell repair, growth, and regulation of processes. Ribosomes may be free in the cytoplasm or attached to the rough endoplasmic reticulum.

topics.title: LIVING ORGANISMS : CELL STRUCTURE AND FUNCTIONS OF CELL COMPONENTS

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following is found in Meiosis but not in Mitosis?
explanation: Meiosis and Mitosis are two types of cell division. The main difference between them is that Meiosis involves two rounds of genetic separation and cellular division while Mitosis only involves one of each. In the process of Meiosis, crossing over occurs during Prophase I, where homologous chromosomes pair up and exchange segments of their genetic material. This does not occur in Mitosis, hence the correct answer is 'Crossing over'.

subject: Biology
q: What keeps the stems of young herbaceous plants upright?
explanation: Turgor pressure keeps the stems of young herbaceous plants upright. Turgor pressure is the force within the cell that pushes the plasma membrane against the cell wall. It is usually caused by the osmotic flow of water from an area of low solute concentration outside of the cell into the cell's vacuole, which has a higher solute concentration. Healthy plant cells are turgid and this provides the plant with rigidity.

topics.title: SUPPORT AND MOVEMENT : SUPPORTING TISSUES IN ANIMALS

subject: Biology
q: The following are characteristics of plant cell except?
explanation: The characteristic of plant cells that is incorrect is the 'Absence of large vacuole'. Plant cells typically contain a large central vacuole that serves multiple functions, including maintaining the shape of the cell and storing water, nutrients, and waste products.

topics.title: LIVING ORGANISMS : CELL STRUCTURE AND FUNCTIONS OF CELL COMPONENTS

subject: Biology
q: Which combination of muscle and rib movement draws air into the lungs during breathing?
explanation: When you breathe in (inspiration), the diaphragm contracts and moves downward (flattens), while the ribs are pulled upward and outward by the intercostal muscles. This enlarges the chest cavity, decreases internal pressure, and allows air to enter the lungs.

topics.title: RESPIRATION : THE MECHANISM OF GASEOUS EXCHANGE IN PLANTS AND MAMMALS

subject: Biology
q: The energy released during cellular respiration is stored in the form........
explanation: The energy released during cellular respiration is stored in the form of Adenosine triphosphate (ATP). ATP is the primary energy currency of living organisms. It is a high-energy molecule that stores the energy we need to do just about everything we do. It is present in the cytoplasm and nucleoplasm of every cell, and essentially all the physiological mechanisms that require energy for operation obtain it directly from the stored ATP.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following methods will not reduce air pollution?
options: Siting factories and power stations away from residential areas
explanation: Building tall factory chimneys will not reduce air pollution. It only disperses the pollutants over a wider area, but does not reduce the amount of pollutants released into the atmosphere. The other options, such as siting factories away from residential areas, using lead-free petrol, and recycling, can all help to reduce air pollution.

topics.title: HUMANS AND ENVIRONMENT : POLLUTION AND ITS CONTROL

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following actions helps protect endangered animals?
explanation: Creating protected reserves provides safe habitats where endangered animals can live and reproduce without threat from hunting or habitat loss. Promoting illegal hunting, ignoring laws, or encouraging habitat destruction all negatively impact wildlife conservation efforts.

topics.title: HUMANS AND ENVIRONMENT : CONSERVATION OF NATURAL RESOURCES

subject: Biology
q: A vegetable farmer will benefit from a cattle rearer if
options: the faecal remains of the cattle are made available to the farmer.
the farmer makes available fresh green vegetable for consumption by the cattle daily.
the farmer provides shelter for the cattle and cattle rearer
the cattle rearer makes available meat to the farmer daily.
explanation: The faecal remains of the cattle can be used as manure by the vegetable farmer. This is beneficial for the farmer as it enriches the soil with nutrients, which in turn promotes the growth of the vegetables. This is a form of symbiotic relationship known as mutualism, where both parties benefit.

subject: Biology
q: In which group of animals does the embryo develop inside the mother's body and receive nourishment directly from her?
explanation: Viviparity is characteristic of mammals, where embryos develop inside the mother's body and are nourished directly until birth. Most birds, reptiles, and amphibians lay eggs externally, though some reptiles show exceptions.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following structures is NOT found in the female reproductive system?
explanation: The prostate gland is part of the male reproductive system and produces fluid that nourishes sperm. The ovary, uterus, and fallopian tube are all essential components of the female reproductive system.

subject: Biology
q: In the nitrogen cycle, which of the following processes actually removes nitrogen compounds from the soil rather than adding to or maintaining them?
explanation: Denitrification is carried out by certain soil bacteria that convert nitrates (NO₃⁻) into nitrogen gas (N₂) or nitrous oxide (N₂O), which escape into the atmosphere. This reduces the nitrogen available in the soil for plants. In contrast, nitrogen fixation, nitrification, and decomposition all help to increase or maintain soil nitrogen levels in forms usable by plants.

subject: Biology
q: Crop rotation
explanation: Continuous monocropping — planting the same crop repeatedly on the same plot — removes the same set of nutrients from the soil season after season. Without replenishment, this leads to soil nutrient depletion and loss of fertility. In contrast, crop rotation, green manuring, and cover cropping help restore or maintain soil nutrients.

subject: Biology
q: Which of these is not a function of the kidney?
options: Maintenance of acid-base balance in the body
explanation: The kidney is responsible for the production of urine, maintenance of acid-base balance in the body, and osmo-regulation. However, the production of bile is a function of the liver, not the kidney.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following glands serves as exocrine gland?
explanation: The pancreas serves as both an exocrine and an endocrine gland. As an exocrine gland, it produces enzymes that are released into the small intestine to aid in digestion. These enzymes include lipase, protease, and amylase. The other options listed, the ovary and adrenal gland, are endocrine glands, and urea is not a gland at all.

subject: Biology
q: A person with blood group B can only donate blood to individuals with blood groups
explanation: A person with blood group B can donate blood to individuals with blood groups B and AB. This is because blood group B has B antigens and can be accepted by individuals with blood group B (who also have B antigens) and blood group AB (who have both A and B antigens). Blood group A would react negatively to the B antigens, and blood group O has no antigens, so it would also react negatively to the B antigens.

topics.title: HEREDITY : INHERITANCE OF CHARACTERS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: Which among these is an example of discontinuous variation observed in man?
explanation: Discontinuous variation refers to traits that fall into distinct categories with no intermediate stages. Tongue rolling is an example of this, as people can either roll their tongues or they can't, with no in-between. Skin color, body weight, and height, on the other hand, are examples of continuous variation, as they can range across a spectrum of values with many possible intermediate stages.

subject: Biology
q: Which among these is a nitrifying bacteria?
explanation: Nitrobacter is a genus of bacteria that play an important role in the nitrogen cycle, a process vital for life on Earth. They are nitrifying bacteria, meaning they convert nitrites (NO2-) into nitrates (NO3-). This is a key step in the nitrogen cycle because nitrates can be taken up by plants and used to build proteins and other necessary compounds.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following substances is contained in the chloroplast?
explanation: Chloroplasts are the site of photosynthesis in plants and algae. During photosynthesis, light energy is converted into chemical energy, which is stored in the form of starch. Therefore, starch is contained in the chloroplast.

topics.title: LIVING ORGANISMS : CELL STRUCTURE AND FUNCTIONS OF CELL COMPONENTS

subject: Biology
q: Which plant group is considered more evolutionarily advanced due to the presence of seeds?
explanation: Gymnosperms are more advanced because they produce seeds, allowing for better protection and dispersal of offspring. Unlike Bryophytes, Thallophytes, and Pteridophytes—which reproduce via spores—Gymnosperms show adaptations like vascular tissues, true roots and stems, and most importantly, seed formation, which marks a major evolutionary step in the plant kingdom.

subject: Biology
q: What tube brings urine from the kidney to the liver?
explanation: The ureter is a tube that carries urine from the kidney to the bladder, not the liver. The liver does not play a direct role in the transport of urine. The bile duct is associated with the liver and gallbladder, carrying bile to the small intestine. The urethra is the tube that carries urine from the bladder out of the body. The term 'uretus' does not exist in the context of human anatomy.

subject: Biology
q: Urea synthesis occurs in the ........
explanation: Urea synthesis occurs in the liver. The liver plays a vital role in the metabolism of proteins. When proteins are broken down by the body, ammonia is produced. The liver converts this toxic ammonia into urea, a less toxic compound, which is then excreted in urine by the kidneys.

subject: Biology
q: Legumes incorporate nitrogen gas into their protein because
options: of the presence of azobacter in their roots
they have a well developed root system.
explanation: Legumes are able to incorporate nitrogen gas into their proteins because they possess root nodules containing rhizobium. Rhizobium is a type of bacteria that can fix atmospheric nitrogen into a form that plants can use. This symbiotic relationship allows legumes to grow in nitrogen-poor soils. The presence of azobacter in their roots, a well-developed root system, or being autotrophic does not directly relate to nitrogen fixation.

subject: Biology
q: The gas produced during the decomposition of matter is
explanation: The decomposition of organic matter by bacteria and fungi results in the production of various gases. One of the most common gases produced is Hydrogen Sulphide (H2S). This gas has a characteristic rotten egg smell. The other options do not typically result from decomposition.

topics.title: ECOLOGY : ENERGY FLOW IN THE ECOSYSTEM

subject: Biology
q: What ecological conditions favours the breeding of blackflies?
explanation: Blackflies breed in fast flowing streams. The larvae attach themselves to rocks and vegetation in the fast flowing water where they filter feed on organic matter. Therefore, fast flowing streams favor the breeding of blackflies.

topics.title: ECOLOGY : FACTORS AFFECTING THE DISTRIBUTION OF ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: Prevention of erosion could best be carried out by
explanation: Building tall factory chimneys does not reduce air pollution. Instead, it disperses the pollutants over a wider area. The other options like passing waste gases through filters and absorbers, using lead-free petrol in cars, and recycling tins, cans, and bottles are all methods that can help reduce air pollution.

topics.title: HUMANS AND ENVIRONMENT : POLLUTION AND ITS CONTROL

subject: Biology
q: Only specially adapted micro-organisms are found in
explanation: Specially adapted micro-organisms, known as extremophiles, are found in extreme environments such as salty water (halophiles). These organisms have adapted to survive in high salt concentrations where most other organisms cannot.

topics.title: ECOLOGY : FACTORS AFFECTING THE DISTRIBUTION OF ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: Plants which can survive in extremely dry places are called
explanation: Plants that can survive in extremely dry places are called xerophytes. These plants have adaptations such as thick cuticles, reduced leaf areas, sunken stomata and extensive root systems to minimize water loss and maximize water uptake.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : STRUCTURAL ADAPTATIONS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: The presence of ______ for swimming in a tad-pole enables it to adapt in an aquatic habitat.
explanation: The presence of a tail for swimming in a tadpole enables it to adapt in an aquatic habitat. The tail provides propulsion for the tadpole to move in water.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : ADAPTIVE COLOURATION AND ITS FUNCTIONS

subject: Biology
q: Possession of thin membrane enables a tapeworm to
options: Fasten themselves to the wall of the host's intestine
Absorb digested food from the host's intestine to their system
Shield themselves inside the host's intestine with anti-enzymes in order to avoid being digested
explanation: Tapeworms are parasitic flatworms that live in the intestines of their hosts. They have a thin membrane that allows them to absorb nutrients from the host's digested food. This is because they lack a digestive system of their own, so they rely on their host's digested food for nourishment.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : STRUCTURAL ADAPTATIONS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following juices contains the enzyme ptyalin?
explanation: Ptyalin, also known as salivary amylase, is an enzyme that is found in saliva. It begins the process of digestion by breaking down starch and glycogen into maltose, a disaccharide.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following essential substance is contained in vegetable?
explanation: Vegetables are rich in mineral salts. These are essential substances that the body needs for various functions, including maintaining the balance of body fluids, aiding in the transmission of nerve impulses, and contributing to the structure of body tissues.

subject: Biology
q: Good quality food will perform the following functions in human except
explanation: Good quality food provides nutrients that the body needs to function properly, including providing energy, maintaining health, and sustaining growth. However, it does not provide resistance against specific diseases such as malaria. Resistance against diseases is primarily provided by the immune system, not directly by food.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following is considered to be the first product of Photosynthesis?
explanation: The first product of photosynthesis is starch. This is because the simple sugar formed is partly used by the plant and excesss of it is converted into starch immediately for storage and the starch is transported to other parts of the plant through the phloem vessels for storage.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the blood corpuscles are irregular and amoeboid in shape
explanation: Leucocytes, also known as white blood cells, are the blood corpuscles that are irregular and amoeboid in shape. They have the ability to change their shape and move around, which allows them to engulf foreign particles and pathogens in the body. This is a key part of the body's immune response.

subject: Biology
q: Which organ removes the largest quantity of water from the blood?
explanation: The kidney is the organ that removes the largest quantity of water from the blood. This is done through the process of filtration where the kidneys filter out waste products, excess substances, and water from the bloodstream. The filtered water and waste products form urine, which is excreted from the body.

subject: Biology
q: The movement of blood between the heart and all other parts of the body beside the lungs is
explanation: The movement of blood between the heart and all other parts of the body beside the lungs is referred to as systemic circulation. This is the part of the cardiovascular system which carries oxygenated blood away from the heart to the body, and returns deoxygenated blood back to the heart.

subject: Biology
q: The change in the genetic make up of an organism resulting in an inheritable characteristics which is new is
explanation: A mutation is a change in the genetic makeup of an organism that results in a new inheritable characteristic. This can occur spontaneously or be induced by environmental factors. Mutations can lead to variation within a species, which is a key driver of evolution.

topics.title: HEREDITY : INHERITANCE OF CHARACTERS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: The organic molecule(s) present in the nucleus is/are
explanation: The nucleus of a cell contains DNA, RNA, and proteins. DNA (Deoxyribonucleic acid) is the genetic material of the cell that contains the information used in the development and functioning of all living organisms. RNA (Ribonucleic acid) is a nucleic acid present in all living cells. Its principal role is to act as a messenger carrying instructions from DNA for controlling the synthesis of proteins. Proteins in the nucleus play various roles including DNA replication, gene expression, protein synthesis, and cell division.

topics.title: LIVING ORGANISMS : CELL STRUCTURE AND FUNCTIONS OF CELL COMPONENTS

subject: Biology
q: Albinism is an expression of trait controlled by
explanation: Albinism is a genetic condition where people are born without the usual pigment (color) in their bodies. Their bodies aren't able to make a normal amount of melanin, the chemical that is responsible for eye, skin, and hair color. Albinism is caused by recessive genes. A recessive gene is a gene that can be masked by a dominant gene. In order to have a trait that is expressed by a recessive gene, such as albinism, you must get the gene for that trait from both parents.

topics.title: HEREDITY : INHERITANCE OF CHARACTERS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: The male sex cells also called gametes are produced in the testes by a process called
explanation: The male sex cells, also known as sperm cells or gametes, are produced in the testes by a process called spermatogenesis. Spermatogenesis is the process by which haploid spermatozoa develop from germ cells in the seminiferous tubules of the testis. This process starts with the mitotic division of the stem cells located close to the basement membrane of the tubules. These cells are called spermatogonial stem cells.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following describes an example of fertilization in higher organisms
options: Pollen grain landing on a sticky stigma
Ejaculation of the egg and pollen grain
Ejaculation of sperm into the female body
explanation: Fertilization in higher organisms is described as the fusion of sperm and egg nuclei. This process results in the formation of a zygote, which is the first cell of a new individual. The other options do not accurately describe fertilization.

subject: Biology
q: The part labeled III in the above diagram is
explanation: Hyphae in a mushroom is a vegetative body parts made up of tiny and delicate thread containing nuclei

topics.title: INTERNAL STRUCTURE OF A FLOWERING PLANT : ROOT

subject: Biology
q: The organism above can be found in
explanation: It is found in moist environment because of its adaptive features. Forests are ideal mushroom habitats because they provide the moisture, organic matter, and darkness that mushrooms need. Mushrooms can be found growing on the forest floor, on dead logs and branches, and even on living trees.

topics.title: NATURAL HABITAT : AQUATIC TERRESTRIAL AND ARBOREAL

subject: Biology
q: The possession of chloroplast in Euglena viridis enables it to
explanation: Euglena viridis is a unicellular organism that possesses chloroplasts. Chloroplasts are organelles found in plant cells and some protists like Euglena. They absorb sunlight and use it in conjunction with water and carbon dioxide to produce food for the organism, a process known as photosynthesis. Therefore, the presence of chloroplasts in Euglena viridis enables it to carry out photosynthesis.

subject: Biology
q: Viruses are pathogens of the following diseases except
explanation: Viruses are microscopic pathogens that cause various diseases in humans, including measles, smallpox, and poliomyelitis. However, tuberculosis is not caused by a virus. It is caused by a bacterium known as Mycobacterium tuberculosis. Therefore, tuberculosis is the correct answer as it is not caused by a virus.

subject: Biology
q: The association between two organisms living together in which only one benefits from the association while the other is neither benefited nor harmed is
explanation: The association described in the question is known as commensalism. In commensalism, one organism benefits while the other is neither harmed nor benefited. This is different from predation where one organism benefits at the expense of the other, competition where both organisms may be harmed, and dispersal which refers to the movement of organisms from one place to another.

topics.title: ECOLOGY : SYMBIOTIC INTERACTIONS OF PLANTS AND ANIMALS

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following will have the least effect on the rate of change of the numbers in a population
explanation: Mutation is the least likely to have an effect on the rate of change of the numbers in a population. Mutation refers to a change in the DNA sequence within a gene or chromosome of an organism resulting in the creation of a new character or trait not found in the parental type. While mutation can lead to new traits that may benefit or harm an organism, the rate at which mutations occur is generally slow and thus they are less likely to have an immediate impact on population numbers compared to factors like food supply, disease, and predation.

topics.title: THE ECOLOGY OF POPULATIONS : FACTORS AFFECTING POPULATION SIZES

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following controls all voluntary actions
explanation: The cerebrum controls all voluntary actions. It is the largest part of the brain and is responsible for most of the brain's function. It controls functions such as thought, emotions, and problem-solving, and voluntary physical action. The olfactory lobes are responsible for the sense of smell, the hypothalamus controls body temperature, hunger, important aspects of parenting and attachment behaviors, thirst, fatigue, sleep, and circadian rhythms, and the pons varolii is involved in motor control and sensory analysis.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following statement describes an autonomic nervous system
options: It dials with external stimuli and their responses
It affects glands, cardiac muscles and smooth muscles
explanation: The autonomic nervous system is a part of the nervous system that controls and regulates the internal organs without any conscious recognition or effort by the organism. It affects glands, cardiac muscles, and smooth muscles. It does not always stimulate effectors, its activities are not voluntary, and it does not deal with external stimuli and their responses.

subject: Biology
q: The glomerular filtrate contains the following except
explanation: The glomerular filtrate is the liquid that has passed through the glomerulus in the kidney and into the Bowman's capsule. It contains water, urea, and glucose, but not blood corpuscles. Blood corpuscles (red and white blood cells and platelets) are too large to pass through the glomerular filter.

subject: Biology
q: One of the following is not the theory of natural selection stated by Charles Darwin
options: Characteristics acquired through use or disuse are transmitted offspring
Variations appear by themselves in a population
Species that are the fittest for the environment contribute more offsprings to the next needs of the generation
explanation: The theory of natural selection stated by Charles Darwin does not include the concept that characteristics acquired through use or disuse are transmitted to offspring. This is actually a part of Lamarck's theory of evolution, not Darwin's. Darwin's theory of natural selection includes the concepts that variations appear by themselves in a population, hereditary variations are transmitted to offspring, and species that are the fittest for the environment contribute more offspring to the next generation.

subject: Biology
q: Ecology is best defined as the study of
options: The inter-relationship between living organisms and the environment
explanation: Ecology is the scientific study of the interactions between organisms and their environment. This includes both the physical environment, which can be described as the sum total of local abiotic factors such as insolation (sunlight), climate, and geology, and the biotic environment, composed of other organisms.

topics.title: ECOLOGY : FACTORS AFFECTING THE DISTRIBUTION OF ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: The following processes are involved during expiration in man except
options: The thoracic activity first increases in volume
The ribs are moved downwards and inwards.
explanation: During expiration (exhalation), the process is mostly passive and the thoracic cavity decreases in volume. The intercostal muscles relax, the sternum moves inward, and the ribs move downwards and inwards. The decrease in volume causes an increase in pressure relative to the outside, which forces air out of the lungs.

topics.title: RESPIRATION : THE MECHANISM OF GASEOUS EXCHANGE IN PLANTS AND MAMMALS

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following is an example of continuous variation?
explanation: Continuous variation is variation that has no limit on the value that can occur within a population. Height in humans, for example, is a trait that can have a wide range of values (from very short to very tall) and is not limited to a few distinct possibilities, which is why it is considered a continuous variation. Other examples like blood groups, sex difference, and haemoglobin types are examples of discrete variation because they have a limited number of possible values.

subject: Biology
q: One of the following is an effect of clay soil on vegetation
explanation: Clay soil is known for its poor drainage and compact nature. This makes it difficult for plant roots to penetrate and access nutrients, hence it supports scanty vegetation. The other options are incorrect because clay soil does not support luxuriant, light or arid vegetation due to its characteristics.

topics.title: SOIL : CHARACTERISTICS OF DIFFERENT TYPES OF SOIL

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following factors is not considered in a terrestrial habitat?
explanation: Turbidity is a measure of the degree to which the water loses its transparency due to the presence of suspended particulates. It is a factor considered in aquatic habitats, not terrestrial habitats. The other factors like temperature, sunlight, and humidity are all considered in a terrestrial habitat.

topics.title: NATURAL HABITAT : AQUATIC TERRESTRIAL AND ARBOREAL

subject: Biology
q: What type of vertebrate assists in breathing alongside with the ribs?
explanation: The thoracic vertebrae assist in breathing alongside the ribs. The thoracic vertebrae are the only vertebrae that support ribs and have overlapping spinous processes. The other options, sacral, caudal, and lumber, are types of vertebrae but they do not assist in breathing.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following statement is correct about Southern Guinea Savanna in Nigeria
options: It is the largest of all the biotic community in Nigeria
It has plants species like Iroko, Opepe etc
explanation: It is the largest of all the biotic community in Nigeria

subject: Biology
q: In an ecosystem, the organism which changes light energy into stored chemical energy is the
explanation: In an ecosystem, the organism which changes light energy into stored chemical energy is the producer. Producers, also known as autotrophs, convert light energy from the sun into chemical energy through a process called photosynthesis. Consumers, decomposers, and carnivores do not have this ability.

topics.title: ECOLOGY : ENERGY FLOW IN THE ECOSYSTEM

subject: Biology
q: In the theory of use and disuse Lamarck proposed that evolution occurred because of the following except
options: There is great changes in the environment
explanation: In the theory of use and disuse, Lamarck proposed that evolution occurred because organisms develop specialized characters due to changes in their environment, and these characters are inheritable. However, he did not propose that there is natural selection of offspring. Natural selection is a concept proposed by Charles Darwin, not Lamarck.

subject: Biology
q: The basis of growth involves the following processes except
explanation: Growth in biological terms involves cell division, cell enlargement, and cell differentiation. Cell reduction, however, is not a part of growth. Instead, it is a process that occurs during apoptosis or programmed cell death.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following organs can be considered vestigial in humans but functional in other mammals
explanation: The appendix is considered a vestigial organ in humans. It is a small, tube-shaped organ that doesn't have a known purpose in humans. However, in other mammals, such as rabbits and rodents, the appendix is used to help digest cellulose in their diet.

subject: Biology
q: The study which involves the inter-relationship between groups of organisms or species of organisms living together in an area is
explanation: Synecology is the study of groups of organisms or species of organisms living together in an area. It focuses on the interactions and relationships between different species within a community or ecosystem.

topics.title: ECOLOGY : FACTORS AFFECTING THE DISTRIBUTION OF ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: Thunderstorm can be beneficial to plants because it
explanation: Thunderstorms can cause nitrogen in the air to be converted into nitrates, a form of nitrogen that plants can absorb and use to grow. This process is known as nitrogen fixation. Therefore, thunderstorms can be beneficial to plants because they add nitrate to the soil.

topics.title: HUMANS AND ENVIRONMENT : CONSERVATION OF NATURAL RESOURCES

subject: Biology
q: A flowering plant having both the male and female flowers on the same plant is said to be
explanation: A plant that has both male and female flowers on the same plant is said to be monoecious. Monoecious is derived from the Greek words 'monos' meaning 'single' and 'oikos' meaning 'house'. This term is used in botany to describe a single plant that bears both male and female flowers.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following is a behavioural adaptation that protects prey from predators?
explanation: Secreting irritating fluid is a behavioural adaptation that protects prey from predators.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : BEHAVIOURAL ADAPTATIONS IN SOCIAL ANIMALS

subject: Biology
q: The period of inactivity seen in some animals during long period of heat or drought is
explanation: The period of inactivity seen in some animals during long periods of heat or drought is called aestivation. This is a state of animal dormancy, similar to hibernation, characterized by inactivity and a lowered metabolic rate, that is entered in response to high temperatures and arid conditions. It happens during summer months.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : BEHAVIOURAL ADAPTATIONS IN SOCIAL ANIMALS

subject: Biology
q: The insect trapping movements of the leaves of the venus fly trap is a behavioural adaptation for
explanation: The insect trapping movements of the leaves of the venus fly trap is a behavioural adaptation for obtaining food. The Venus flytrap is a carnivorous plant native to subtropical wetlands. It catches its prey—chiefly insects and arachnids— with a trapping structure formed by the terminal portion of each of the plant's leaves, which is triggered by tiny hairs on their inner surfaces.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : BEHAVIOURAL ADAPTATIONS IN SOCIAL ANIMALS

subject: Biology
q: Animals are said to be gregarious when
options: they survive adverse conditions of cold weather
they transmit information from one individual to another
they are of the same species moving together in a group
they give an alarm to alert others to show the normal protective behaviour of the group when danger threatens
explanation: Animals are said to be gregarious when they are of the same species moving together in a group. Gregarious behavior is a type of social behavior where individuals of the same species aggregate together. This behavior is common in insects, birds, and mammals.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : BEHAVIOURAL ADAPTATIONS IN SOCIAL ANIMALS

subject: Biology
q: The type of nutrition shown by spirogyra is
explanation: Holophytic nutrition is a type of autotrophic nutrition where an organism produces its own food from inorganic substances using light or chemical energy. Spirogyra, being a green algae, uses sunlight, water and carbon dioxide to produce its own food through photosynthesis, which is a characteristic of holophytic nutrition.

subject: Biology
q: The collective name for the female parts of the flower is
explanation: The collective name for the female parts of a flower is 'gynoecium'. The gynoecium is the group of structures that form the female reproductive organ in flowers. It is typically composed of one or more carpels. The carpel is the individual unit of the gynoecium and has a stigma, style, and ovary.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following pairs are correctly matched with their mode of nutrition?
explanation: Euglena is mixotrophic, meaning it can make its food through photosynthesis and also feed on organic material. Chlamydomonas is photosynthetic, not saprophytic. Paramecium is heterotrophic, and Rhizopus feeds on decaying matter (saprophytic), not autotrophic.

subject: Biology
q: The type of nutrition in which two organisms of different species live together and derive nutrients from each other is
explanation: Symbiotic nutrition is a type of nutrition where two organisms of different species live together and derive nutrients from each other. This is a mutual relationship where both organisms benefit from each other. The other options do not describe this type of relationship. Saprophytic nutrition is where an organism feeds on dead and decaying matter, holozoic nutrition is a method of nutrition that involves the ingestion of liquid or solid organic material, digestion, absorption and assimilation of it to produce energy, and holophytic nutrition is a method of nutrition that involves photosynthesis.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following statement about the similarities in plant and animal transport is correct?
options: Materials and hormones are transported in fluid form
The transport medium is made up of only tissues
Their materials are transported in the same vessels
explanation: In both plants and animals, materials and hormones are transported in fluid form. This is because fluids can easily flow and transport substances throughout the organism. The other options are incorrect. The transport medium is not made up of only tissues, their materials are not transported in the same vessels, and diffusion is required for the transport of substances.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following describes the function of lymph
explanation: The function of lymph is the production of antibodies. Antibodies are proteins that help fight off infections and can bind to certain harmful substances. The other options are incorrect. Lymph does not regulate temperature, it does not engulf anything, and while it does play a role in body defence, this is primarily through the production of antibodies.

subject: Biology
q: One of the major ways in which the blood is able to defend the body against disease causing organisms is
explanation: The blood defends the body against disease-causing organisms through several mechanisms, one of which is clumping, also known as agglutination. This is a process where the immune system causes pathogens to stick together, making them easier to neutralize or remove. The other options do not relate to the blood's defensive mechanisms.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following is not a transmittable character in plants
explanation: Haemophilia is a genetic disorder that impairs the body's ability to make blood clots, a process needed to stop bleeding. This is a human genetic disease and cannot be transmitted in plants. The other options are characteristics that can be transmitted in plants.

topics.title: HEREDITY : INHERITANCE OF CHARACTERS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: Inheritable character/features possessed by an organism can be referred to as
explanation: Inheritable characters or features possessed by an organism are referred to as traits. Traits are any characteristic or feature of an organism, such as its color, height, weight, etc. Genes are segments of DNA that carry the instructions for these traits. A locus is the specific location of a gene on a chromosome, and allelomorphs, also known as alleles, are different versions of the same gene.

topics.title: HEREDITY : INHERITANCE OF CHARACTERS IN ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: One of the following is a process of transmission of hereditary characteristics by chromosomes
options: Genes relating to the same character occupy identical locations on the chromosomes
Separation of homologous chromosomes into four daughter cells
Dictating the formation of a protein by a gene in a chromosome
explanation: The process of transmission of hereditary characteristics by chromosomes is best described as the dictation of the formation of a protein by a gene in a chromosome. This is because genes, which are located on chromosomes, contain the instructions for the production of proteins. These proteins determine the traits or characteristics of an organism. This process is known as gene expression.

subject: Biology
q: The attachment of the embryo to the wall of the uterus is called
explanation: The attachment of the embryo to the wall of the uterus is called implantation. After fertilization, the fertilized egg or zygote travels down the fallopian tube and into the uterus where it implants into the uterine wall. This is the beginning of pregnancy.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the reproductive system in mammals secretes a part of the seminal fluid which raises the pH of the fluid in the female reproductive system?
explanation: The Cowper's gland secretes the seminal fluid which raises the pH of the fluid in the female reproductive system. This alkaline fluid helps to neutralize the acidic environment of the vagina, which aids in the survival and transportation of sperm.

subject: Biology
q: The part labelled a in the above diagram is used for
explanation:
Gill cover helps the tilapia fish in gaseous exchange.
Protection: The gill cover protects the gills from damage. The gills are delicate organs that are susceptible to injury from predators, debris, and other objects in the water. The gill cover helps to protect the gills from these hazards.

topics.title: STRUCTURAL/BEHAVIOURAL ADAPTATIONS OF VERTEBRATES : BONY FISH

subject: Biology
q: The above organism can be classified under which phylum in the animal kingdom
explanation: The tilapia fish can be classified under chordata phylum in the animal kingdom. Chordates are animals that have a notochord at some stage in their development. The notochord is a flexible rod that supports the dorsal (back) side of the body. Other chordate features include a dorsal nerve cord, pharyngeal slits, and a post-anal tail.

subject: Biology
q: One important characteristics of green plant is that they
explanation: Green plants are autotrophic, meaning they can produce their own food through the process of photosynthesis. They use sunlight, carbon dioxide, and water to produce glucose, which is their food, and oxygen, which is released into the atmosphere. The other options are not universally true for all green plants.

subject: Biology
q: A virus can sometimes be regarded as a living organism because it
options: It exists in a variety of shapes
Is easily visible with the aid of an electron microscope
explanation: Viruses can sometimes be regarded as living organisms because they reproduce in living cells. Viruses are considered 'obligate parasites' as they cannot reproduce without a host cell. They invade the host cell and use its machinery to replicate. The other options, while true, do not make a virus a living organism.

subject: Biology
q: A population is best described as
options: A group of the same species of organisms living together
A group of animals and plants living together
explanation: A population in biology is defined as a group of individuals of the same species living in the same area at the same time. They are capable of interbreeding and have the potential to produce fertile offspring. The other options do not accurately define a population in biological terms.

topics.title: THE ECOLOGY OF POPULATIONS : FACTORS AFFECTING POPULATION SIZES

subject: Biology
q: The number of times an organism occurs within a given area of a habitat is referred to as
explanation: Population frequency refers to the number of times an organism occurs within a given area of a habitat. Population frequency is the proportion of individuals in a population that have a particular trait. It is calculated by dividing the number of individuals with the trait by the total number of individuals in the population.

topics.title: THE ECOLOGY OF POPULATIONS : POPULATION DENSITY AND OVERCROWDING

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following is a voluntary action?
options: Pulling your hand away from a hot surface
explanation: Taking notes during a lecture is a voluntary action because it involves conscious thought, planning, and muscle control directed by the brain's motor cortex. The other actions—withdrawal from heat, sneezing, and pupil contraction—are involuntary reflexes or automatic responses that occur without deliberate control.

subject: Biology
q: The organelle responsible for regulating all cellular activities by housing genetic material is the
explanation: The nucleus controls the activities of the living cell. The nucleus is a membrane-bound organelle that contains the cell's chromosomes. It is the control center of the cell because it houses the cell's DNA, which directs the synthesis of proteins and cell division.

topics.title: LIVING ORGANISMS : CELL STRUCTURE AND FUNCTIONS OF CELL COMPONENTS

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following are components of glomerular filtrate?
options: All the components of blood except mineral salts
All the components of blood except plasma protein and cells
Blood cells, amino acid, salts and nitrogenous wastes
explanation: The glomerular filtrate is composed of all the components of blood except plasma proteins and cells. This is because the glomerulus, a network of capillaries in the kidney, filters the blood and allows small molecules like water, glucose, and ions to pass through while preventing larger molecules like proteins and cells from passing through.

subject: Biology
q: The sum total of adaptive changes from pre-existing form that have taken place over a long time resulting in diversity of forms, structures and functions among organism is referred to as
explanation: Organic Evolution is the sum total of adaptive changes from pre-existing form that have taken place over a long time resulting in diversity of forms, structures and functions among organisms. It involves changes in the genetic makeup of populations over time and can lead to new species.

subject: Biology
q: The specific portion of a habitat which is occupied by a particular species is referred to as
explanation: The specific portion of a habitat which is occupied by a particular species is referred to as an 'Ecological niche'. It describes the role and position a species has in its environment; how it meets its needs for food and shelter, how it survives, and how it reproduces.

topics.title: ECOLOGY : FACTORS AFFECTING THE DISTRIBUTION OF ORGANISMS

subject: Biology
q: In plants respiration occurs in
explanation: The correct answer is, 'All living cells'. In plants, respiration occurs in all living cells. Respiration is the process by which cells break down glucose and other organic molecules to release energy. It takes place in the mitochondria of cells, which are present in all living cells of plants. The stomata and lenticel are involved in gas exchange, but not in respiration specifically. Mesophyll cells are responsible for photosynthesis, not respiration. The root is involved in nutrient absorption, but not in respiration specifically.

topics.title: RESPIRATION : THE MECHANISM OF GASEOUS EXCHANGE IN PLANTS AND MAMMALS

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following is an example of discontinuous variation?
options: The range in height of a pea plant from 30cm to 50cm
Offsprings including two male cats and three female cats
The colour of pear plants grown in the dark
The adult body weight ranging from 50kg to 95kg
explanation: Discontinuous variation refers to the variation in characteristics that have a limited number of possible values. These characteristics are usually determined by a single gene pair. An example of this is the sex of offspring, which can only be male or female. Therefore, the option 'Offsprings including two male cats and three female cats' is an example of discontinuous variation.

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following type of soil has the highest water retaining capacity?
explanation: Clay soil has the highest water retaining capacity among the given options. This is due to its fine texture and small particle size, which allows it to hold water more effectively than loam, laterite, or sand. The small particles in clay soil are packed closely together, leaving little space for water to drain away, thus it retains water better.

topics.title: SOIL : CHARACTERISTICS OF DIFFERENT TYPES OF SOIL

subject: Biology
q: Rabbits cannot survive in an aquatic habitat because they have
explanation: Rabbits cannot survive in an aquatic habitat because they do not have gills. Gills are the respiratory organ found in many aquatic organisms that extracts dissolved oxygen from water and excretes carbon dioxide. The absence of gills in rabbits means they cannot breathe in water, hence cannot survive in an aquatic habitat.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : ADAPTIVE COLOURATION AND ITS FUNCTIONS

subject: Biology
q: Which of the following substances pass through the root cell membrane by osmosis?
explanation: Water passes through the root cell membrane by osmosis. Osmosis is a type of passive transport that involves the movement of water molecules from an area of high water concentration to an area of low water concentration, across a semi-permeable membrane. In plants, water is absorbed from the soil into the root cells by osmosis.

subject: Biology
q: Trees in Savanna habitats usually have thick bark which mainly.
explanation: Trees in Savanna habitats usually have thick bark to resist bush fires. Savanna habitats are known for their dry climate and frequent fires. The thick bark of trees in these habitats helps to protect the inner tissues of the tree from damage during fires, allowing the tree to survive and recover after a fire.

topics.title: ADAPTATION FOR SURVIVAL : ADAPTIVE COLOURATION AND ITS FUNCTIONS

subject: Biology
q: Competition among individuals of a population increases the
explanation: Competition among individuals of a population increases the 'Survival of the fittest'. This is because in a competitive environment, only those individuals who are best adapted to their environment survive and reproduce. This concept is known as 'Survival of the fittest' or natural selection.

subject: Biology
q: In natural selection, Darwin proposed that evolution occurred because of the following conditions except
options: that nature selects those that will survive to reproduce their kind
